title: Devin Nunes officially resigns from Congress, clearing way to helm Trump's social media company
summary: Devin Nunes officially resigned from Congress to become CEO of former President Trump's media and technology company, Truth Social. Nunes, a staunch ally of Trump, will lead the new platform which aims to be an alternative to mainstream social media. His move signifies his continued loyalty to Trump and his transition from a congressional career focused initially on local issues to national security and defense of the former president.

title: Qualcomm, Microsoft partner on metaverse chip for AR glasses
summary: Qualcomm and Microsoft are collaborating on custom chips for lightweight augmented reality (AR) glasses, targeting both consumers and businesses for metaverse applications. The partnership aims to integrate these chips with Microsoft's Mesh software for virtual presence and Qualcomm's Snapdragon Spaces for AR functions like spatial mapping and hand-tracking. Though no release date was provided, the initiative seeks to advance wearable AR devices for broader adoption.

title: What Crypto-related ETFs Could Hit the US Market in 2022?
summary: The article discusses the potential for various crypto-related ETFs, particularly metaverse products, to launch in the US market in 2022, while the approval of spot bitcoin and ether futures products remains uncertain. Industry experts express apprehension about the immediate investability of metaverse ETFs due to potential crossover in holdings and a lack of pure-play names. There's also skepticism regarding the SEC's approval of a spot bitcoin ETF in 2022, with some analysts expecting an ether futures-based ETF to gain approval sooner.

title: Trump’s social media app to launch in February, App Store listing says
summary: Donald Trump's new social media app, Truth Social, is set to launch on February 21, 2022, according to an Apple App Store listing. The launch comes 13 months after Trump was banned from major social media platforms like Facebook and Twitter. The app will offer features similar to Twitter and is the first of three planned stages for the Trump Media & Technology Group (TMTG), which also plans a subscription video service and a podcast network.

title: Meta leases up all office space in Austin's tallest tower in historic deal
summary: Meta Platforms Inc., the parent company of Facebook, has leased all 589,000 square feet of office space at Sixth and Guadalupe, which will be Austin's tallest building. This historic deal ends months of speculation and signifies Meta's commitment to expand its presence in Austin, with plans to hire hundreds more employees in the city. The company will also maintain its existing downtown Austin offices.

title: Tech Moves: Adaptive CFO to resign; Meta comms exec departs; AI vet joins Absci board; new Zymeworks CEO
summary: This article details several significant personnel changes in the tech and biotech sectors, including Chad Cohen's resignation as CFO of Adaptive Biotechnologies, Joseph Sirosh's appointment to the Absci board, John Pinette's departure from Meta, and Kenneth Galbraith becoming the new CEO of Zymeworks. It highlights the impact of these moves on the respective companies and their financial performance or strategic direction.

title: Meta Platforms adds DoorDash CEO Tony Xu to board of directors
summary: Meta Platforms has announced that DoorDash CEO Tony Xu has been appointed to its board of directors. Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg praised Xu for building a successful service for millions of people and numerous businesses. Xu co-founded DoorDash in 2013 at Stanford University.

title: Exercise by Accident: VR Games To Help You Work Out at Home
summary: This article highlights how virtual reality (VR) games can offer a fun and effective way to exercise at home, even for those who find traditional workouts challenging. It categorizes various VR games into dedicated fitness apps, rhythm-based games, action titles, sporty simulations, and training programs, detailing how each can contribute to physical activity and fitness goals. The piece also mentions Oculus Move, a system-level fitness tracker, to monitor calorie burn across VR apps and encourages readers to share their favorite VR exercise routines.

title: Facebook faces $3.2 bln UK class action over market dominance
summary: Facebook, now Meta Platforms, is facing a £2.3 billion ($3.2 billion) class-action lawsuit in the UK for allegedly abusing its market dominance by exploiting the personal data of 44 million users between 2015 and 2019. The lawsuit, brought by Liza Lovdahl Gormsen, claims Facebook imposed unfair terms requiring users to surrender valuable personal data to access its network. This legal challenge comes shortly after Facebook lost an attempt to strike out an antitrust lawsuit from the Federal Trade Commission in the U.S.

title: Facebook loses bid to dismiss users’ data-privacy antitrust claims
summary: A California federal judge has partially denied Facebook's motion to dismiss antitrust claims from consumers and advertisers, allowing complaints regarding false data privacy representations and an agreement with Google to proceed. While certain claims against Meta Platforms Inc. were dismissed, plaintiffs have been granted 45 days to refile amended complaints. This decision follows another recent ruling against Facebook in an antitrust case filed by the Federal Trade Commission.

title: A Guide to Investing in Emerging Asian Markets Outside of China
summary: This article discusses investment opportunities in five emerging Asian markets—Pakistan, Bangladesh, Indonesia, Vietnam, and the Philippines—dubbed "Asian Growth Cubs" by Dawn Global Management. These countries offer significant growth potential for investors looking for exposure to Asian markets outside of China and India, with a collective GDP similar to India's but a much lower market cap. The article highlights the Asian Growth Cubs ETF (CUBS) as a way for foreign investors to access these markets, focusing on industries like healthcare, telecom, and technology.

title: Certification programs build digital skills
summary: The Economic Development Corporation Michigan City (EDCMC) and Goodwill Industries of Michiana are collaborating to offer certification programs for digital skill enhancement. These online courses, including Facebook Social Media Marketing and Google IT Support, aim to help community members earn stackable certificates in high-demand fields. The programs are designed to assist individuals in overcoming barriers to education and employment by providing coursework, mentorship, and job placement services.

title: Mother sues Meta and Snap over tween daughter’s suicide
summary: An American mother has filed a lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc. and Snap Inc., alleging their social media platforms, Instagram and Snapchat, are responsible for her 11-year-old daughter's suicide. The lawsuit claims the daughter developed an "extreme addiction" to these platforms, which the companies allegedly knew were harmful to minor users. This legal action comes amid increasing scrutiny and ongoing investigations into the mental health impact of social media on young people.

title: Microsoft secures IPs like Call of Duty and Candy Crash to boost its metaverse ambitions, analyst says
summary: Microsoft's $68.7 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard is seen as a strategic move to bolster its metaverse ambitions, according to analyst Joost Rietveld. The deal secures popular gaming franchises like Call of Duty and Candy Crush, potentially differentiating Microsoft's Game Pass service and influencing cross-platform availability. While Microsoft has committed to honoring existing agreements, allowing Call of Duty to remain on PlayStation for now, the long-term impact on platform exclusivity for other IPs remains uncertain.

title: What a Bristol-Myers project to monitor off-label prescribing says about future uses of A.I.
summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb collaborated with Cortical.io to utilize AI for monitoring off-label drug prescribing by analyzing social media data. The pilot project successfully identified off-label uses of drugs, including an unexpected finding for Suboxone, demonstrating the potential of natural language processing and less data-intensive AI methods for post-market surveillance. This initiative highlights the growing importance of AI in patient safety and the value of brain-inspired AI research.

title: The 3 Best Carbon Stocks of 2022
summary: This article highlights the top three carbon-related investment opportunities for 2022, emphasizing the growing importance of carbon markets and green investing. It details the KraneShares Global Carbon Strategy ETF (KRBN) for broad market exposure, Carbon Streaming Corporation (NETZ/OFSTF) as an early mover in carbon credit streaming, and Brookfield Renewable Partners (BEP) for a more conservative renewable energy play. The article underscores how increasing net-zero ambitions among corporations and rising awareness of climate change will drive investor interest in these sectors.

title: Timing Is Everything For ETFs
summary: Thematic ETFs, which invest in specific market themes, have grown significantly. Their success often depends heavily on the timing of their launch, sometimes due to unintentional events, as exemplified by the ETFMG Prime Cyber Security ETF (HACK) benefiting from major cybersecurity breaches shortly after its inception. Conversely, funds like the Sprott Buzz Social Media Insights ETF (BUZ) failed due to poor timing, while later similar entrants thrived by tapping into peak market interest.

title: Morgan Stanley Sees $8 Trillion Metaverse Market — In China Alone
summary: Morgan Stanley predicts an $8 trillion metaverse market in China alone, replacing the mobile internet with a more "immersive experience." The investment bank acknowledges significant technological and regulatory hurdles for mass adoption, estimating an initial total addressable market of $4 trillion before expanding to $8 trillion as it disrupts offline activities.

title: Beer Summit Takeaways: A-B CEO Previews Bud Light Next; Digital Marketing Opportunities on Facebook, Instagram; Lots of Seltzer Talk
summary: The article summarizes key discussions from Beer Business Daily’s Beer Industry Summit, detailing Anheuser-Busch InBev's CEO Brendan Whitworth's insights on Bud Light Next and e-commerce, Meta's Pablo Merheb's presentation on digital marketing for alcohol brands, and growth updates from Diageo’s Lone River Ranch Water, Molson Coors' Topo Chico Hard Seltzer, and Gopuff's expansion into beverage alcohol delivery. Constellation Brands CEO Bill Newlands also provided updates on the company’s Mexican import portfolio and Fresca Mixed.

title: Meta Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2021 Results
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. reported solid financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2021, driven by continued growth in user engagement and business use of its services. The company highlighted significant investments in key growth areas such as Reels, commerce, and virtual reality, alongside a new financial reporting structure separating its Family of Apps (FoA) and Reality Labs (RL) segments. Meta also announced its upcoming ticker symbol change to 'META' and provided an outlook for Q1 2022 revenue, anticipating headwinds from increased competition, platform changes, and macroeconomic factors.

title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway Is Now Bigger Than Meta
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has surpassed Meta Platforms in market capitalization, marking a significant shift in stock-market standing. This article highlights Berkshire Hathaway's increasing valuation relative to Meta.

title: Metaverse stocks Nvidia and Roblox fall as Facebook parent Meta's Web3 business reports massive loss
summary: Shares of metaverse-related companies like Roblox, Nvidia, and Matterport fell after Meta Platforms reported a significant operating loss of $10 billion in its Web3 division, Reality Labs, for 2021. The news led to a market value wipeout of over $200 billion for Meta and a sell-off across various tech stocks connected to the metaverse concept. This downturn highlights concerns following Meta's aggressive push into the metaverse space.

title: Meta Extended Its Buybacks Into January—Just Before the Stock Slid
summary: Meta Platforms aggressively repurchased its stock in the fourth quarter of 2021 and continued these buybacks into January 2022. This occurred just prior to the release of the company's disappointing fourth-quarter earnings report. The earnings report subsequently caused a significant decline in Meta's stock price.

title: Meta rivalry with Apple inflamed as Facebook parent company share price plummets
summary: Meta's stock price plummeted by 26%, wiping out approximately $220bn in market value, primarily due to "headwinds" caused by Apple's new iOS privacy changes. Mark Zuckerberg blamed these changes, which restrict advertisers' ability to target ads, estimating a $10bn impact on Meta's business. In contrast, other tech companies like Snap, Amazon, and Pinterest reported strong ad revenue or stock growth, indicating that Apple's privacy updates did not affect all companies equally.

title: Traditional conglomerates such as GE, J&J and Toshiba are moving in a new direction
summary: Traditional conglomerates like General Electric, Johnson & Johnson, and Toshiba are breaking up their companies to increase shareholder value and simplify management. This trend is driven by past financial difficulties and the complexities of their diversified operations. In contrast, modern tech giants like Amazon and Meta thrive as conglomerates by leveraging platform-based models and network effects, which offer a different approach to managing varied businesses.

title: Opinion: $1.4 trillion? Big Tech’s pandemic year produces mind-boggling financial results
summary: In 2021, despite supply-chain disruptions, labor shortages, and rising prices, major tech companies including Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, and Microsoft achieved staggering financial results. They collectively saw over $320 billion in profit on $1.4 trillion in sales, with profits increasing more than 55% and sales growing 27% from an already record-setting base. This performance highlights Big Tech's resilience and expanding economic influence, making their combined sales comparable to a top-15 global economy.

title: Peter Thiel to step down from board of Facebook parent Meta
summary: Tech billionaire Peter Thiel is stepping down from the board of Meta Platforms Inc., the parent company of Facebook. Thiel, who co-founded PayPal and was an early investor in Facebook, has served on the board since 2015 and will not seek re-election at the company's annual stockholders meeting in May.

title: Aussie funds exposed to the Meta rout: Charts of the Week
summary: Australian managed funds and popular index-tracking ETFs, including those from Magellan, Hyperion, and Lakehouse, were significantly impacted by Meta Platforms' sharp 26% stock drop, the largest one-day market capitalization loss in US history. This "Meta rout" contributed to new year woes for growth investors, although some losses were mitigated by strong performance from Amazon.com positions within several of these funds. Sustainable funds generally avoided the Meta selloff due to ESG-related exclusions stemming from privacy concerns.

title: Meta Is Now Calling Out Google Over Apple's App Privacy Rules
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. is publicly criticizing Google, alleging it has an unfair advantage under Apple's new privacy policies. While Meta's apps must ask users for tracking consent, Google's search and browser functions do not, leading to a shift in advertiser budgets towards Google. This comes after Meta's revenue took a multibillion-dollar hit due to its inability to gather as much data on iPhone users.

title: Better Buy: Meta Platforms vs. PayPal Holdings
summary: This article compares Meta Platforms and PayPal Holdings after both companies experienced significant stock price drops due to disappointing earnings reports and future guidance. It outlines the reasons behind their respective crashes, including Apple's privacy changes and TikTok competition for Meta, and supply chain issues and increased competition for PayPal. The author ultimately argues that Meta is a more compelling investment due to its diversification, lower valuation, and wider moat, despite current risks for both stocks.

title: YouTube floats ideas of verifying NFTs, watching games in metaverse
summary: YouTube is exploring using blockchain technology and NFTs to help creators find new ways to monetize content by offering verifiable digital assets. The company also suggested ideas for the metaverse, focusing on enhancing social viewing experiences, particularly for gaming content, to allow users to watch videos together in a virtual environment. These initiatives aim to cut down on fraud in the digital art market and improve user engagement.

title: Expedia CEO says the metaverse doesn’t pose a threat to what he calls ‘real-verse’ travel
summary: Expedia CEO Peter Kern believes the metaverse does not pose a competitive threat to real-world travel, which he calls the "real-verse." While acknowledging the intrigue, Kern stated that virtual experiences cannot replicate the life-changing nature of physical travel to places like Paris or national parks. He expressed confidence that people will continue to prioritize real-world experiences, despite the growth in virtual reality investments.

title: Stanley Druckenmiller Buys Chevron as Family Offices Reveal Stock Picks
summary: Stanley Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office sold its Meta Platforms Inc. position and bought Chevron, indicating a shift away from big tech. Similarly, George Soros's family office reduced its stakes in Amazon.com, Alphabet, and a Nasdaq 100 ETF. These moves suggest a trend among some wealthy investors to re-evaluate their portfolios regarding large technology companies.

title: Texas sues Facebook parent company over use of facial recognition technology
summary: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc., the parent company of Facebook, alleging violations of state privacy and anti-deceptive trade practice laws due to its past use of facial recognition technology. The suit claims Facebook failed to disclose biometric data collection, transmitted information to third parties, and did not delete data within a reasonable timeframe, seeking billions in damages. Meta has stated the claims are without merit and plans to defend itself vigorously, while political rivals have criticized Paxton's timing and use of outside counsel.

title: Meta's Facebook to pay $90 million to settle privacy lawsuit over user tracking
summary: Meta Platforms Inc (Facebook) has agreed to pay $90 million to settle a long-standing privacy lawsuit. The lawsuit accused Facebook of tracking users' internet activity, including visits to outside websites with "like" buttons, even after they logged out, and then compiling these browsing histories for advertisers. The settlement still requires court approval and mandates the deletion of improperly collected data, addressing claims of violating federal and state privacy and wiretapping laws.

title: Facebook Stops Fighting Wiretap Act Litigation After More Than a Decade
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. (formerly Facebook) has proposed a $90 million national class action settlement in its long-running Facebook Internet Tracking Litigation, involving over 124 million Americans. The lawsuit stemmed from allegations that Facebook tracked user activity on non-Facebook sites using "Like" buttons between April 2010 and September 2011, violating federal and California Wiretap Act laws. While the settlement brings closure to a decade-long legal battle and requires deletion of old data, it also highlights potential future legal scrutiny for third-party trackers.

title: Trump's new social media application 'Truth Social' begins gradual rollout
summary: Donald Trump's "Truth Social" app began a gradual rollout on Sunday, with the aim of being fully operational in the US by the end of March. The platform is intended as an alternative to major social media sites that banned Trump after the January 6, 2021 Capitol assault. Former congressman Devin Nunes, CEO of Trump Media & Technology Group, stated that the app would be available on the Apple App Store, celebrating the return of free speech for those who felt their voices were canceled.

title: How DBMS is Used by Hulu, JPMorgan Chase, and The Weather Company
summary: This article examines how Database Management Systems (DBMS) are crucial for organizations to efficiently store, retrieve, and manage data. It provides case studies of how major companies like Hulu, JPMorgan Chase, and The Weather Company leverage DBMS to address challenges, optimize operations, and maintain data integrity and security, highlighting the benefits over traditional file systems. The article demonstrates the real-world applications and positive outcomes of integrating DBMS in various industries.

title: 11 Inspiring Women Who Pivoted into Tech Careers After Completing 2U-Powered Boot Camps
summary: This article highlights 11 women who successfully transitioned into tech careers after completing 2U-powered boot camps in various disciplines like coding, data science, cybersecurity, and fintech. The featured individuals come from diverse backgrounds, including full-time moms, mid-career pivoters, and those affected by pandemic layoffs. Their stories showcase how these boot camps equipped them with the skills needed to land new roles at companies such as Meta, Infosys, Johnson & Johnson, Lowe's, Citibank, and others, acting as role models and transforming the tech landscape.

title: College could take place in the metaverse, but these problems must be overcome first
summary: Higher education in the US faces significant challenges including rising tuition, student debt, and dissatisfaction with online learning. The metaverse could offer a solution by creating more immersive remote learning experiences, but it also presents new ethical and practical problems. Five key issues to address include ensuring academic freedom on private platforms, maintaining student focus in a highly distracting virtual environment, improving nonverbal communication through avatars, fostering a sense of community outside of physical campuses, and preventing new digital divides.

title: Google, Meta face penalties in Russia as deadline passes to open local offices
summary: Google and Meta are among several internet companies facing potential penalties in Russia for failing to establish local offices and comply with a new communications law. Russia's state communications regulator, Roskomnadzor, had set a deadline of the end of February for these companies to open local offices or face restrictions. The move is part of Russia's broader effort to control internet content, with fines previously issued for failing to censor anti-government content, and recent curtailments of access to platforms like Facebook in retaliation for censoring state media.

title: Apple, Netflix, TikTok strike back against Russian state media content
summary: Several major tech and entertainment companies, including Apple, Netflix, and TikTok, are taking action against Russian state media and suspending services in Russia following the invasion of Ukraine. This includes Netflix refusing to carry Russian federal channels, Apple pausing product sales and limiting services, and social media platforms restricting access to and demoting content from outlets like RT and Sputnik due to concerns about propaganda and disinformation. The moves reflect a global condemnation of Russia's actions and a united front from U.S. businesses.

title: Leaders in Semiconductors, Packaging, IP Suppliers, Foundries, and Cloud Service Providers Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
summary: A consortium of leading technology companies, including ASE, AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC, has formed to standardize the chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for die-to-die interconnects, aiming to enable multi-vendor compatibility for System-on-Chip construction. The consortium is open for membership and plans to develop future generations of the UCIe technology.

title: Spotify closes its office in Russia in response to attack on Ukraine
summary: Spotify announced the indefinite closure of its office in Russia in response to the country's attack on Ukraine. The music streaming platform stated its priority is its employees' safety and ensuring access to information. Spotify has also restricted the discoverability of Russian state-affiliated media and removed content from RT and Sputnik in several markets, while matching employee donations for humanitarian aid.

title: Goldman Sachs-Backed Virtual Beauty App Developer To Go Public In $1 Billion SPAC Deal
summary: Perfect Corp., a Taiwan-headquartered virtual beauty try-on app developer backed by Goldman Sachs, announced its plan to go public in the U.S. through a SPAC merger valued at $1 billion. The combination with Nasdaq-listed Provident Acquisition Corp. is expected to raise up to $335 million, including a $50 million PIPE investment from investors like Chanel and Shiseido. CEO Alice Chang stated the company aims to expand into Southeast Asia and the Middle East, and further integrate its augmented reality technologies across more platforms and categories, including the metaverse.

title: How Hyperscale Data Centers are reshaping India’s IT
summary: Hyperscale data centers are critical infrastructure for companies like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft, providing dedicated computing resources to handle massive user demands. These facilities are essential for the digital economy, supporting everything from emails to online shopping, and their importance has grown with trends like remote work and emerging technologies such as AI and IoT. India is poised for significant growth in its data center industry, driven by government incentives and an increasing need for robust data storage and processing capabilities, despite environmental concerns related to energy consumption.

title: The cloud over commercial real estate lingers
summary: The commercial real estate market, particularly office spaces, continues to face challenges despite an initial return to optimism after the pandemic's early days. While some global forecasts are cautiously positive, the underlying issues of vacancy rates, decreasing property values, and the shift to remote work persist, leading to conversions of office spaces into residential units and increased incentives from landlords. The long-term impact on property valuations and investment returns remains a significant concern for the industry.

title: Industry Leaders Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
summary: A consortium of industry leaders, including AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, TSMC, and ASE, has formed to standardize die-to-die interconnects and foster an open chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for package-level connectivity. This initiative aims to reduce development time and cost, promoting customizable and interoperable chiplet designs for future silicon innovations.

title: This CEO Has Purchased Over $1 Billion Worth Of His Own Stock Over The Last Year
summary: Dustin Moskovitz, co-founder of Meta Platforms and Asana Inc., has made an aggressive insider stock purchase, buying over $1 billion worth of Asana shares since June 2021. He now owns more than 16.5 million shares, representing about 21% of the company. This comes as Asana, a SaaS company, experienced strong demand during the pandemic but has seen its stock price tumble recently.

title: Biden must safeguard Russian internet access, civil society warns
summary: Civil society groups are urging the Biden administration to protect internet providers in Russia from sanctions, arguing that cutting off web access would isolate dissidents and strengthen the Kremlin's control. They recommend that the Office of Foreign Asset Controls formally authorize U.S. internet services, software, and hardware to preserve web access, citing precedents from past sanctions campaigns. The groups warn that without clear guidance, individuals in Russia could be cut off from vital internet services.

title: Russia says it will block Instagram
summary: Russia announced it would block Instagram, blaming a recent change in Meta's hate-speech policy that temporarily allowed some users to call for violence against Russians in the context of the Ukraine war. This move is part of Russia's broader effort to control information flow and prevent its citizens from accessing uncensored news about the invasion. The ban is expected to affect 80 million Russian users.

title: Metaverse Technology Redefining Digital Experience
summary: The metaverse is emerging as a significant technological advancement, moving beyond gaming to redefine digital experiences across various industries. Companies like Facebook (Meta), Nike, and Microsoft are investing heavily, with projections indicating global metaverse revenue could approach $800 billion by 2024. While offering potential for retail, enterprise solutions, and new markets like virtual real estate and entertainment, the metaverse also faces challenges related to data security, legal frameworks, skilled resources, VR device limitations, and potential negative societal impacts.

title: Russia to block Instagram over ‘death to invaders’ posting rule
summary: Russia plans to block Instagram and has launched a criminal case against its owner, Meta Platforms Inc., following Meta's decision to allow posts calling for violence against Russian forces. This move comes as an escalation in Russia's confrontation with US-based social media platforms, with Moscow alleging that Instagram is spreading calls for violence against Russian citizens and military personnel. Meta has defended its decision as a temporary measure in extraordinary circumstances, aimed at protecting Ukrainians' freedom of speech in self-defense.

title: Goldman Sachs CEO Says Ostracizing Russia Isn’t the Finance Industry’s Job
summary: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon stated that it is not the finance industry's job to ostracize Russia, but rather to follow government sanctions. He emphasized that businesses should implement policies set by the government, agreeing with the sanctions against Russia's actions in Ukraine. Despite this stance, the article highlights Goldman Sachs's historical involvement in Russia's economic policy and its mixed record on activism.

title: Aptos raises $200M to bring the Diem blockchain back to life
summary: Aptos Labs, a startup founded by former employees of Meta's Diem blockchain project, has raised $200 million in strategic funding led by Andreessen Horowitz. The company aims to develop a safe and scalable blockchain network, learning from Diem's regulatory challenges. Aptos is building its own open-source blockchain for Web3 applications and expects to launch its public mainnet in the third quarter of 2022.

title: ProShares Launches Metaverse ETF, Expands Innovative Fund Lineup to the Internet’s Next Frontier
summary: ProShares has launched a new Metaverse ETF (VERS) designed to offer investors access to companies at the forefront of the metaverse's發展. The ETF tracks the Solactive Metaverse Theme Index, which includes 40 companies across various industries, from device makers to social media platforms. This initiative builds on ProShares' history of product innovation, including the first bitcoin-linked ETF, and aims to capitalize on the metaverse's potential to revolutionize industries.

title: The Impact of Design on the Employee Experience
summary: Workday's Chief Design Officer, Jeff Gelfuso, emphasizes the importance of delivering seamless and engaging end-to-end experiences for employees within enterprise software, mirroring consumer software expectations. He discusses how Workday is achieving this through simplification, personalization, connectedness, and data-driven insights. The article highlights key design principles and Workday's initiatives like Engage, Everywhere, and Empower to improve the employee experience.

title: Facebook Continues To Lose Out On User Time; Netherlands Data Center Plans Face Opposition
summary: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: FB) is experiencing a decline in user engagement on Facebook, as reported by BuzzFeed CEO Jonah Peretti, which negatively impacts its commerce revenues. Concurrently, Meta's plans for a large data center in Zeewolde, Netherlands, are facing significant political opposition after the Leefbaar Zeewolde party, which campaigned against the project, won a majority in the local elections. This comes at a time when Meta is also dealing with internal pressure from employees affected by plunging stock prices.

title: Towards The Distant Virtual World: Vitalii Vashchuk Shares Insights
summary: Vitalii Vashchuk, Director and Gaming Technologist at EPAM Systems, shared his insights on the metaverse's evolution and the underlying technologies. He describes the metaverse as a concept aimed at enhancing communication and experiences, offering greater freedom than Web2.0. Key technologies enabling the metaverse include Blockchain, NFTs, AI, and IoT.

title: Brookfield agrees to buy Hibernia REIT for $1.2 billion
summary: Brookfield Asset Management Inc. has reached an agreement to acquire Hibernia REIT Plc for approximately 1.09 billion euros ($1.2 billion) in cash, causing Hibernia's shares to surge to a record high. The deal, which offers a 33.9% premium over Hibernia's average share price, sees Brookfield expanding its real estate portfolio in Dublin, a market where tech giants are rapidly growing. Brookfield is actively investing in European office landlords, capitalizing on market uncertainty and discounts to asset values.

title: Amazon Stock Rally Erases Losses for the Year
summary: Amazon.com Inc. shares rallied, becoming the first megacap tech stock to recover its losses for the year,

title: Meta to open new engineering hub in Toronto, expects to create 2,500 jobs
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. is establishing a new engineering hub in Toronto, aiming to create up to 2,500 high-tech jobs focused on immersive online experiences and the metaverse. These roles will offer both in-office and remote work options, making them accessible to Canadians nationwide. Additionally, Meta will invest $510,000 in grants for Canadian research labs to foster metaverse-related innovations.

title: TVA & Silicon Ranch break ground on 70MW solar farm serving Meta’s Tennessee campus
summary: Ground has been broken on a new 70MW solar farm in Madison County, Tennessee, a collaboration between the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA), Meta, the Jackson Energy Authority (JEA), and Silicon Ranch. This McKellar Solar Farm will supply 100 percent renewable energy to Meta's Gallatin data centers upon its expected completion by the end of 2022. Silicon Ranch will fund, own, and operate the facility, investing over $90 million in its construction.

title: Facebook advertisers can pursue class action over ad rates
summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that a lawsuit accusing Meta Platforms Inc.'s Facebook of deceiving advertisers about its "potential reach" tool can proceed as a class action. This decision allows potentially millions of advertisers to sue as a group, alleging that Facebook inflated advertising reach and charged artificially high premiums for ad placements. The lawsuit claims that even senior Facebook executives were aware of the inflated metrics but took no action.

title: MetaBank announces name change
summary: MetaBank announced its new name will be Pathward, following the sale of its existing name and trademark assets to Facebook's parent company, Meta, for $60 million in December. The full transition to Pathward, including a new brand identity and website, is expected by the end of the calendar year, with its public holding company also changing to Pathward Financial, Inc. CEO Brett Pharr stated the name change reflects the company's commitment to financial inclusion and removing barriers for the unbanked, underbanked, and underserved.

title: 3 Tech Trends Emerge for Investors
summary: Morgan Stanley's TMT Conference identified three major technology trends for investors: the metaverse, M&A in tech, and FinTech payment systems. The metaverse is creating new monetizable digital assets and opportunities for brands, while increased corporate interest and market volatility are driving M&A. FinTech is expanding with embedded financial services, cross-border payments, and digital wallets, opening new avenues for financial services across various industries.

title: Livestream game platforms in fierce competition
summary: The Vietnamese game livestream market is experiencing fierce competition, with global technology giants backing popular platforms like Facebook Gaming, YouTube Gaming, Nimo TV, and Booyah. Local platforms such as VNG's TalkTV have struggled to compete with the financial power and lucrative offers from these international players, leading to their eventual closure. The market is primarily driven by ad revenue, and platforms are investing in streamer training and unique content strategies to attract and retain users and content creators.

title: Who Really Owns Facebook, Here's What You Should Know!
summary: This article explains the ownership structure of Facebook, now Meta, detailing how Mark Zuckerberg, despite owning only about 13% of the company, maintains control due to a "dual class" share structure. It describes the evolution from Facebook to Meta, emphasizing that the rebranding did not change the corporate governance, with Zuckerberg retaining grip through Class B shares.

title: Meta cracks down heavily on fake News and spam content being shared in India
summary: Meta significantly increased its content moderation efforts in India during February 2022, removing over 23.6 million posts from Facebook and Instagram to comply with new IT Rules. The majority of these removals were due to spam, with a high proactive rate of detection before user reports. WhatsApp also banned 1.4 million "bad accounts" in India during the same period as part of regulatory compliance.

title: Meta Signs PPA for TransAlta 200 MW Horizon Hill Wind Project
summary: Meta has entered into a long-term renewable energy purchase agreement with TransAlta for 100% of the generation from its 200 MW Horizon Hill Wind Power Project in Oklahoma. This partnership will help Meta achieve its goal of supporting global operations with 100 percent wind and solar energy, while TransAlta expands its U.S. wind generation fleet. The project, estimated to cost between $290 million and $310 million, is expected to begin construction in Q4 2022 and achieve commercial operation by the second half of 2023.

title: Kendall building joins the region’s exclusive billion-dollar club
summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities' 100 Binney St. building in Kendall Square, Cambridge, has been valued at over $1 billion following a recent recapitalization. This valuation places it in an exclusive club of billion-dollar commercial properties in the region. The building houses major tenants such as Bristol Myers Squibb and Meta.

title: Meta (FB) Plans to Introduce Virtual Coins to Diversify Income
summary: Meta Platforms (FB) is exploring the introduction of virtual coins, tokens, and lending services within its applications to reduce its reliance on advertising revenue. The company plans to create in-app tokens, similar to those in games like Roblox, and also social tokens and "creator coins" for influencers, rather than a blockchain-based cryptocurrency like its failed Diem project. This strategic shift aims to diversify its income streams and engage users amidst increasing competition and regulatory scrutiny.

title: Native Advertising Vs Social Media: What Do Audiences Prefer? Outbrain-Savanta Report
summary: A report by Outbrain and Savanta indicates a shift in consumer behavior, with global audiences planning to spend less time on social media and showing a greater preference for editorial content environments. The study reveals that consumers trust editorial sites and the ads within them more than social media, and consider native advertising less intrusive. Marketers are advised to diversify their strategies to include advertising on open-web publishers to align with these changing preferences.

title: Metaverse Market Size [2022-2029] Worth USD 1527.55 Billion | Exhibiting a CAGR of 47.6%
summary: The metaverse market is projected to grow significantly from USD 100.27 billion in 2022 to USD 1,527.55 billion by 2029, showcasing a CAGR of 47.6%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing penetration of online shopping and gaming, alongside advancements in blockchain technology and VR devices. Key companies leading this expansion include Tencent Holdings Ltd., NVIDIA Corporation, Meta, and Microsoft Corporation, who are focusing on product portfolio expansion and technological innovation.

title: Li Lu Top 10 Holdings – Q4 2021, Buys FB, BAC
summary: This article details superinvestor Li Lu's top 10 holdings as of Q4 2021, derived from his 13F-HR documents. His portfolio, valued at over $2.6 billion, shows significant investments in Micron Technology, Bank of America, and Facebook, alongside other major tech companies. The report also highlights his new buys, Facebook and Bank of America, and his sell in Pinduoduo.

title: Fidelity Investments Launches New Metaverse ETF on the New York Stock Exchange
summary: Fidelity Investments has launched a new Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) on the New York Stock Exchange, providing investors access to the rapidly expanding metaverse sector. This move positions Fidelity to compete with other financial institutions already offering similar metaverse-focused ETFs. The ETF will track the Fidelity Metaverse Index, which includes global stocks related to the metaverse's development, manufacturing, and distribution.

title: eBay Appoints Eddie Garcia As Next Chief Product Officer
summary: eBay has announced the appointment of Eddie Garcia as its new Chief Product Officer, succeeding Pete Thompson. Garcia, who previously worked at eBay from 2003-2014, returns to the company after serving as CPO at Sam's Club and Head of Commerce at Facebook/Meta. He will lead eBay's Core Product organization, focusing on delivering a streamlined and world-class customer experience as part of the company's tech-led reimagination.

title: How technology is transforming the future of performance marketing
summary: Technology is becoming increasingly vital in performance marketing, offering benefits like omnichannel mapping, real-time data, mobile-first strategies, powerful AI-driven ad stacks, and robust fraud protection tools. These advancements help brands connect with audiences more effectively, optimize ad spend, and maintain ROI in an evolving digital landscape. The article highlights how ad-tech ensures campaigns are impactful and efficient.

title: Is Palantir Technologies Worth the Wait?
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) shares are down more than 30% this year, but Morningstar analysts see an opportunity for patient investors despite the company's long history of potential with limited profits. Concerns exist regarding its multi-share-class structure, political leanings, lack of consistent GAAP profits, and a recent slowdown in government contracts. However, strong commercial revenue growth and positive free cash flow offer a more optimistic outlook for its long-term potential.

title: Netflix Loses $54 Billion in Market Cap After Biggest One-Day Stock Drop Ever
summary: Netflix experienced its biggest one-day stock drop ever, losing $54 billion in market capitalization after announcing its first subscriber loss in over a decade and projecting further losses. Analysts downgraded the stock, citing market saturation, increased competition, and the impact of account sharing, leading to a significant reassessment of the company's growth prospects.

title: Apple Spends Record High on Lobbying Amid Pressure from Congress
summary: Apple Inc. spent a record $2.5 million on lobbying in the first quarter of 2022, marking a 34% increase from the previous quarter. This significant expenditure comes as the company faces growing pressure from the U.S. Congress regarding antitrust concerns. Other major tech companies like Alphabet, Meta, and Microsoft are also heavily investing in lobbying efforts as Congress considers antitrust legislation that could impact the technology sector.

title: Witty & Frank: 5 Successful Examples of Self-Referential Marketing Campaigns
summary: This article explores self-referential marketing, a strategy that plays on people's natural inclination to focus on themselves, leading to stronger emotional connections with brands. It highlights five successful campaigns from Iberia Airlines, Snapchat (Bitmoji), Munster Donuts, Spotify, and Amazon, demonstrating how personalization, local identity, and user-centric features can enhance engagement and loyalty. The piece also advises marketers to be mindful of consumer boundaries when implementing such personalized approaches.

title: The Trusted Yelp ‘Elite’ Reviewers Who Sell Their Reviews for Cash
summary: An investigation has revealed that some Yelp Elite members, who are designated as more trustworthy reviewers, are selling their positive reviews for cash. Former federal investigator Kay Dean uncovered instances where Elite members posted fake reviews for businesses like Regenics, receiving payment from third parties. This practice undermines Yelp's system of trust and distorts the marketplace, benefiting businesses that pay for reviews over those that don't, although Yelp states that only a small percentage of Elite reviewers have been tied to such activities and the company actively investigates and removes fraudulent accounts.

title: These 21 large-cap stocks have now crashed at least 50%
summary: This article highlights that 21 large-cap stocks have experienced declines of at least 50% from their 52-week highs. Notable companies mentioned include Netflix, PayPal, and Meta Platforms. The significant drops are attributed to a difficult year for technology stocks, as evidenced by major declines in broad indexes.

title: 29 Stock Funds That Flip-Flopped in the Rankings
summary: The stock market's volatility in 2022 caused significant shifts in fund rankings, with some previously high-performing funds plummeting and former laggards rising to the top. This analysis by Morningstar identifies 29 U.S. stock funds that experienced major flip-flops, moving from the top 10% to the bottom 10% or vice versa within their categories based on one-year returns. While these short-term swings don't necessarily alter long-term analyst ratings, they highlight strategic biases within funds and offer insights for portfolio construction.

title: Fidelity Enters the Metaverse with New Financial Education Experience: Invest Quest at The Fidelity Stack
summary: Fidelity Investments has launched a new gamified financial education experience called "The Fidelity Stack" in Decentraland, an eight-story virtual building designed to teach users about ETF investing through an "Invest Quest" challenge. This initiative aims to engage younger audiences, particularly the 18-35 age range, following the company's recent introduction of a metaverse-themed ETF, FMET. Fidelity views this as a significant step in providing financial education in decentralized, virtual communities.

title: MOTO & MRAD Rise on Tech Stock Rally
summary: U.S. stocks, particularly technology stocks, climbed on Thursday, with Apple and Amazon's earnings calls anticipated. Meta Platforms' strong first-quarter earnings report, showing an increase in users beyond expectations, boosted the Nasdaq Composite Index and the S&P 500 technology sector. The SmartETFs Advertising & Marketing Technology ETF (MRAD C) and the SmartETFs Smart Transportation & Technology ETF (MOTO) offer exposure to different segments of the technology sector, with MRAD focusing on advertising and marketing technology and MOTO investing in smart transportation and related tech.

title: Amazon’s biggest drop since 2006 caps dismal month for tech
summary: Amazon.com Inc. shares experienced their largest one-day drop since 2006, sinking 14% and erasing $206.2 billion from its market value, following a disappointing outlook. This slump contributed to a dismal April for major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, and Tesla, which collectively lost a trillion dollars in market value due to tumultuous earnings, rising costs, and concerns over Federal Reserve policy. The Nasdaq 100 Index also saw its biggest one-month decline since October 2008.

title: Tesla’s Ambitions in India Will Test Elon Musk’s Commitment to ‘Free Speech’
summary: Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter could provide the Indian government with leverage to curb critics due to Tesla's business aspirations in the country. Tesla is currently lobbying the Modi government for reduced import taxes, creating a potential conflict of interest between Musk's stated commitment to free speech on Twitter and his business goals. Critics worry this could lead to increased online suppression of dissent, particularly affecting marginalized groups in India.

title: Meta Northwest lead Rajeev Rajan to depart for job at Atlassian
summary: Rajeev Rajan, Meta's Northwest lead and head of engineering for the Facebook app and video division, is leaving the company on May 18 to join Atlassian. He was appointed to his role at Meta just one year ago. This departure marks a significant leadership change for Meta in the Northwest region.

title: Meta Platforms Sued Over Logo Infringement - ProShares S&P 500 Dynamic Buffer ETF (BATS:FB)
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: FB) is being sued by the DFINITY Foundation, the entity behind the Internet Computer (CRYPTO: ICP) blockchain, for alleged trademark infringement. DFINITY claims Meta's new infinity symbol logo, filed in March 2022, is too similar to their own, which has been in use since 2017 and trademarked since 2018. The lawsuit argues that the similarity will cause confusion among consumers due to both platforms targeting similar customer bases in the same industry.

title: With interest rates rising, it's time to focus on MANG stocks instead of FAANG, according to Jefferies
summary: Jefferies analysts recommend focusing on "MANG" stocks (Microsoft, Apple, Nvidia, and Alphabet) instead of the traditional "FAANG + Microsoft" group as interest rates rise. They suggest that MANG stocks present a safer investment strategy during the Federal Reserve's monetary policy tightening. This advice was issued prior to a Federal Open Market Committee meeting.

title: DevOps: Why it is now the only way to develop software
summary: DevOps is presented as a cultural shift essential for modern software development, moving beyond traditional "silos" and "waterfall" approaches. It emphasizes collaboration between development and operations teams and leverages automation to achieve frequent, seamless updates, meeting high end-user expectations set by major tech companies. The article argues that without DevOps, it's impossible for applications to remain relevant in today's fast-paced digital environment.

title: Meta Class Action Alleges Copyright Infringement of Infinity Logo
summary: Dfinity Foundation has filed a copyright infringement class action lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc., alleging that Meta knowingly uses a logo that infringes upon Dfinity's trademarked infinity-shaped logo. Dfinity claims that Meta's similar logo damages its reputation for protecting privacy and creates consumer confusion. The lawsuit seeks to prevent Meta from continued infringement and requests an award of all profits Meta earned from the alleged infringement.

title: 2022 Selloff Has Left the U.S. Stock Market Undervalued
summary: The 2022 stock market selloff has pushed the broad U.S. equity market into undervalued territory, trading at a 12% discount to fair value according to Morningstar's equity research. While value stocks remain attractive, growth stocks, particularly small-caps, now offer significant discounts after substantial declines. The article highlights undervalued opportunities in the Communications, Consumer Cyclical, Financial Services, and Technology sectors, despite ongoing headwinds like slowing economic growth, Federal Reserve tightening, rising interest rates, and inflation.

title: Tech sell-off pushes Nasdaq to steepest drop since June 2020
summary: A tech sell-off on Thursday, May 5, 2022 pushed the Nasdaq Composite to its steepest drop since June 2020, wiping out hundreds of billions in market value. This downturn was driven by disappointing earnings reports from cloud and e-commerce companies like Shopify, Amazon, and Meta, coupled with investor concerns following the Federal Reserve's interest rate hike to combat inflation. The rotation out of tech began in late 2021 due to inflation fears and escalated with events like Russia's invasion of Ukraine, leading to significant year-to-date losses for many "Covid winner" stocks.

title: 7 Charts for Making Sense of These Markets
summary: This article analyzes current market volatility through seven charts, highlighting the Federal Reserve's aggressive interest rate hikes as a primary driver. It discusses the impact on both stock and bond markets, the underperformance of growth stocks versus value stocks, and offers insights on potential opportunities for investors amidst the market adjustments.

title: The Old Tech FAANG Is Dulled, But a New Version Has Promise
summary: Bank of America strategists have introduced "FAANG 2.0," a new grouping of sectors designed for a world facing inflation, war, and geopolitical shifts, replacing the underperforming original tech FAANG stocks. This new version, comprising fuels, aerospace and defense, agriculture, nuclear and renewables, and gold and metals/minerals, has shown significantly better market performance year to date compared to both the S&P 500 and the original tech FAANG stocks. The article highlights the rationale behind this shift, citing energy security, increased military spending, rising food prices, and gold's role as a refuge asset in the current global climate.

title: Meta is opening its first store as VR headsets inch closer to mainstream reality
summary: Meta Platforms is opening its first physical store to sell VR headsets like the Oculus Quest 2, despite its Reality Labs division facing significant losses. The company is betting heavily on the metaverse, viewing it as the solution to boost virtual reality adoption beyond the current low engagement among teens. Experts suggest that widespread VR adoption could be triggered by one or two "killer applications" and that Meta's strategy is to build the foundational platform for this new computing paradigm, even if profitability remains years away.

title: 15 Top Cities for Machine Learning Engineer Salaries
summary: This article identifies the top 15 U.S. cities offering the highest salaries for Machine Learning Engineers, based on data from Blind. It highlights that major tech hubs like Silicon Valley and Seattle lead due to high demand from companies like Meta and Apple. The piece also notes the increasing importance and compensation for AI and machine learning skills across industries.

title: Uber to cut costs, slow down hiring, CEO tells staff
summary: Uber Technologies Inc. will scale back hiring and reduce spending on marketing and incentives, as CEO Dara Khosrowshahi announced a shift to a leaner investment model focusing on achieving profitability on a free cash flow basis. This move comes in response to changing investor sentiment and follows similar cost-cutting measures by Meta Platforms Inc. despite Uber's robust driver base.

title: Ten Principles For Designing The Metaverse
summary: This article outlines ten principles for designing the metaverse, emphasizing that while the metaverse doesn't fully exist yet, companies should experiment with virtual 3D worlds. Author David Truog introduces "fusion" and "empiricism" as key principles, explaining how VR, MR, and AR experiences range from physical to entirely mediated, and stressing the importance of direct experience over speculation. The article also touches upon other principles like depth, attention, and magic, all grounded in human-centered design, to help organizations navigate the development of extended reality (XR) experiences.

title: Meta Fights to Use the Infinity Symbol as Their Logo and Define the Future of the Internet
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. is being sued by Dfinity Foundation over its new infinity symbol logo. Dfinity argues that Meta's logo creates consumer confusion with its own, violating trademark laws and hindering its mission to provide a privacy-focused, decentralized alternative to Meta's vision for the internet. The lawsuit highlights a broader battle over the future of the internet, with Dfinity advocating for decentralization and user privacy against Meta's centralized approach.

title: Grand Investment Opportunity Awaits in the Metaverse
summary: Despite the current downturn in growth and technology stocks, the metaverse offers a significant long-term investment opportunity. The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) is presented as a viable option for investors who wish to capitalize on this emerging concept without individual stock picking, holding familiar large-cap tech companies. Experts believe the metaverse has the potential to transform social media, gaming, shopping, entertainment, and education.

title: Archer Datacenters Partners with Evoque to Expand Nashville Data Center Campus
summary: Archer Datacenters announced a joint venture with Evoque, a Brookfield Infrastructure Partners company, to expand its Nashville Hyperscale campus. This $200 million equity commitment aims to develop critical digital infrastructure across the Nashville and Central Southeast region, providing scaled colocation offerings with rapid delivery and a focus on sustainability. The partnership capitalizes on Nashville's significant economic and population growth, addressing an underserved market with robust power and connectivity solutions, including renewable energy initiatives.

title: Evoque Partners with Archer Datacenters to Develop 61 Acre Data Center Campus Outside Nashville
summary: Evoque has formed a joint venture with Archer Datacenters to develop a 61-acre data center campus near Nashville, Tennessee, bringing over 100MW of power capacity and 500,000 square feet of data center space to the market. Evoque is investing $200 million into the project, which includes a commitment to a 100% renewable energy solution to meet growing data center demands in the Central Southeast region. The collaboration aims to provide rapid market entry for hyperscalers and enterprises, leveraging an existing data center shell and long-term expandability with local utilities.

title: Microsoft to boost pay and stock compensation to retain employees
summary: Microsoft announced plans to significantly increase its budget for employee salary raises and boost stock compensation by at least 25% for many workers. This initiative targets early to mid-career employees and aims to retain talent amid rising inflation and a competitive labor market, particularly against rivals like Amazon, Google, and Meta. The move comes as the company nears its fiscal year-end and battles for talent in key fields like cybersecurity and AI.

title: Netflix lays off 150 employees as the streaming service contends with big subscriber losses
summary: Netflix is laying off approximately 150 employees, less than 2% of its 11,000 staffers, primarily in the U.S., due to slowing revenue growth. This move comes less than a month after the streaming service reported its first subscriber loss in a decade and saw its stock value drop significantly. The company is exploring new strategies like lower-priced, ad-supported tiers and cracking down on password sharing to regain subscribers.

title: The Majority Of Chinese Social Media Users Are Social Savants
summary: A Forrester survey reveals that Chinese social media users are highly active and receptive to social media, especially when compared to their US counterparts. The study segments users into four categories, finding that "Social Savants" constitute two-thirds of metro Chinese online adults, making them an ideal target for social marketing. This contrasts sharply with the US, where user distribution across segments is more even.

title: Goodbye, Gig Economy. It’s a Multi-Earner Era
summary: The gig economy is evolving into a "multi-earner era" driven by platforms that enable individuals to generate income beyond traditional employment. This trend has significant implications for workers, employers, and the economy, with younger generations like Gen Z actively participating in various "X-to-Earn" verticals. Employers face challenges in hiring and retention as workers seek multiple income streams, potentially requiring increased flexibility and competitive wages to attract and retain talent.

title: News Releases
summary: NextEra Energy Resources, Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA), and Meta celebrated the commissioning of the Elora Solar Energy Center in Elora, Tennessee. This new large-scale solar project will generate 150 megawatts of solar energy, support Meta's regional data centers, and bring significant economic benefits to Lincoln County through job creation and tax revenue. The project highlights a public-private partnership focused on renewable energy and sustainable economic development in the region.

title: From Bitcoin to the Metaverse: The current evolution is a revolution
summary: This article discusses the evolution of cryptocurrency from Bitcoin to the Metaverse, highlighting key technological advancements and the increasing cybersecurity threats in the Web3 space. Check Point Research details how vulnerabilities in leading platforms have led to billions in losses and emphasizes the critical need for enhanced security measures and expert involvement in this rapidly expanding digital frontier.

title: TVA, Meta commission Tennessee's newest large-scale solar energy project
summary: TVA, Meta, and NextEra Energy Resources have commissioned the Elora Solar Energy Center, Tennessee's newest large-scale solar project. This facility will generate up to 150 megawatts of solar energy, utilizing over 500,000 photovoltaic panels. The project is expected to bring significant tax revenue to Lincoln County and supported 250 construction jobs.

title: Trump sues Facebook, Twitter, Google for ‘censorship’, launches his own social media platform
summary: Former President Donald Trump filed class-action lawsuits against Facebook, Twitter, and Google for alleged "censorship" after his accounts were restricted or suspended following the Jan. 6, 2021 Capitol attack. The lawsuits claim these platforms, by coordinating with government entities, act as "state actors" and thus violate his First Amendment rights, though legal experts and court rulings have largely dismissed these arguments. Concurrently, Trump launched his own social media platform, "Truth Social," designed to be a "Big Tent" alternative to mainstream platforms.

title: TVA completes 150 MW solar project for Meta
summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) has announced the completion of the 150 MW Elora Solar project, developed in partnership with Meta to power its datacenter operations in northwest Huntsville, Tennessee. Developed by NextEra Energy Resources, the project expands TVA's operating solar capacity significantly, contributing to its goal of 10 GW of solar by 2035. The project involved the installation of over 500,000 solar modules and was sold by NextEra Energy Resources to the Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board and NextEra Energy Partners.

title: Into the Metaverse
summary: The article "Into the Metaverse" explores the concept of the metaverse, defining it as the next evolution of the internet where users interact in 3D virtual spaces through avatars. It traces the term's origin from Neal Stephenson's novel "Snow Crash" and draws parallels to films like "The Matrix" and "Ready Player One." The author highlights existing metaverse-like platforms, such as Second Life, Decentraland, and Somnium Space, discussing their functionalities, use of blockchain and cryptocurrencies, and the potential for immersive experiences through advancing VR/MR hardware.

title: Doe v. Meta and the Future of the Communications Decency Act
summary: The article discusses the class action lawsuit Doe v. Meta, filed by Rohingya refugees, alleging that Meta's algorithms promoted hate speech. It analyzes Meta's defense under the Communications Decency Act (CDA) Section 230 immunity and the plaintiffs' attempts to bypass it through product liability arguments and choice-of-law analysis. The author critiques the plaintiffs' arguments regarding extraterritoriality and choice-of-law, suggesting they are unlikely to succeed based on Ninth Circuit precedent, and concludes with a proposal for amending the CDA to address the role of machine-learning algorithms and friend suggestion features.

title: Qualcomm CEO sees growth in high-end phones even if economy slows
summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon believes the company will continue to see growth in the high-end smartphone market, despite concerns about an economic slowdown. He noted that consumers are increasingly seeking "better phones" with more capabilities, and Qualcomm is gaining market share in premium devices. Amon also highlighted the potential for augmented reality to become as significant as smartphones, expanding Qualcomm's partnerships in the AR/VR space.

title: L’OFFICIEL Makes Its Arrival in the Metaverse with Virtual Land on The Sandbox
summary: Fashion magazine L’OFFICIEL has launched its presence in the metaverse with virtual land on The Sandbox, inspired by sci-fi films like Dune and Total Recall. This "L’OFFICIEL Land" is designed as an open-air exhibition and digital theatre to showcase the magazine's 100-year content history and future editorial focused on Web3 fashion. The initiative, led by Benjamin Eymère, CEO of L’OFFICIEL and Chief Metaverse Officer of AMTD group, marks a significant move into immersive digital experiences for the brand.

title: Putting Recent Market Volatility in Perspective
summary: This article provides perspective on recent market volatility, noting that while 2022 has been a bumpy ride for investors with significant downturns in growth stocks and technology, such fluctuations are not historically uncommon. It examines the CBOE VIX Index and standard deviation data to show increased volatility, driven by inflation fears and rising interest rates, but emphasizes that market losses are a normal part of investing. The piece concludes by advising long-term investors to maintain their asset mix and avoid drastic changes unless retirement or significant near-term expenses necessitate a re-evaluation of their portfolio.

title: Micron, Netflix, and 5 Other Beaten-Up Tech Stocks That Could Be Bargains
summary: This article identifies seven technology stocks that have experienced significant drops in value during the current year but could offer attractive investment opportunities. It notes that while the broader U.S. stock market has faced challenges, high-growth technology stocks have been particularly hard hit. The piece specifically mentions Micron and Netflix as examples of these beaten-up tech stocks.

title: Facebook's transformation to Meta will be complete with new ticker symbol on June 9
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. announced it will change its stock ticker symbol from "FB" to "META" on June 9, completing its rebranding from Facebook. The company, which shifted its corporate name in October to reflect its metaverse ambitions, had previously intended to use the "MVRS" ticker but delayed the change. The "META" ticker became available after Roundhill Investments relinquished it.

title: Meta Platforms to trade under 'META' ticker from June 9
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. announced that its Class A common stock will begin trading under the ticker symbol 'META' on Nasdaq starting June 9, replacing its longstanding 'FB' ticker. This change aligns with the company's rebrand from Facebook Inc. to Meta Platforms Inc. in October last year, reflecting its focus on developing the "metaverse." The original 'FB' ticker had been in use since the company's initial public offering in 2012.

title: From June 9, Meta Platform Will Be Traded Under Symbol ‘Meta’
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. announced that its Class A common stock will begin trading under the ticker symbol "META" on Nasdaq starting June 9, replacing its current "FB" ticker. This change follows the company's rebranding from Facebook Inc. to Meta Platform Inc. last October, reflecting its focus on building the "Metaverse" as the successor to the mobile internet.

title: Sheryl Sandberg leaving Facebook after 14 years
summary: Sheryl Sandberg is stepping down as Chief Operating Officer of Meta Platforms Inc. after a 14-year tenure marked by significant sales growth and various controversies. Sandberg announced her departure in a Facebook post, stating she plans to focus on philanthropy but will remain on Meta's board. CEO Mark Zuckerberg noted he does not intend to replace her role in the company's current structure.

title: Ottawa should reject the Murdoch plan
summary: This article argues that Canada should reject Rupert Murdoch's plan to force tech companies like Google and Meta to pay news publishers for content, as successfully implemented in Australia. The author contends that the plan is a self-serving cash grab by large newspaper chains, particularly Postmedia, which is largely American-owned. The piece highlights Murdoch's history of media manipulation and questions the premise that Google and Meta are "stealing" content, while suggesting that Canada adopting this plan would create a domino effect for the UK to follow.

title: Amazon executive behind its massive delivery operation to leave after 23 years
summary: Dave Clark, the Amazon executive responsible for building its vast delivery network, is stepping down as CEO of the company's consumer business after 23 years. His departure signifies a shift in Amazon's leadership following Jeff Bezos's exit and comes as the company faces economic challenges, including a recent $2 billion hit from overbuilt warehousing and transportation capacity. Clark, known for taking risks like acquiring planes and championing warehouse robots, indicated he wants to return to "building" and leaves Amazon with a plan to address inflationary challenges.

title: Facebook completes rebrand to Meta, changes Nasdaq listing ticker
summary: Facebook Inc. has completed its rebrand to Meta Platforms Inc., changing its Nasdaq stock ticker from 'FB' to 'META' effective June 9. This move signifies the company's strong commitment to building the metaverse, despite significant losses in its Reality Labs division. Meta is also integrating NFTs into Instagram and has plans for its own digital token.

title: Facebook (FB) officially changes stock symbol to META
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has officially changed its stock symbol from FB to META, completing a major step in its rebranding from Facebook. This change means investors will now find the company under the META symbol on trading platforms. The rebranding follows a period of controversy for the company, including issues with user data and content moderation.

title: Meta Platforms (FB) Changes Ticker to META
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. changed its stock ticker symbol from FB to META to align with its corporate rebranding effort, effective June 9, 2022. This change follows the company's name change from Facebook to Meta in October 2021, and shareholders are not required to take any action. The article highlights Meta's business rationale behind the rebranding, including key acquisitions that broadened its scope beyond the Facebook social media platform.

title: Is the Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) Right for You?
summary: The Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) offers investors diversified exposure to companies benefiting from the metaverse's growth. This article examines METV's top holdings, including Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Roblox, Microsoft, and Autodesk, highlighting their roles in the metaverse ecosystem. It also discusses the fund's expense ratio and the potential market size of the metaverse industry.

title: Mastercard Celebrates Pride Month with Your True Self is Priceless Campaign
summary: Mastercard is celebrating Pride Month with its "Your True Self is Priceless" campaign, featuring live events at New York City Pride, social media activations, and immersive metaverse experiences in Decentraland and Meta’s Horizon Worlds. The campaign encourages the LGBTQIA+ community to write letters to their younger selves, building on Mastercard's commitment to inclusivity which includes the 2019 True Name feature. Events span from sponsoring Pride Island and the NYC Pride March to offering Priceless Experiences with celebrity chefs and virtual engagements in the metaverse.

title: Facebook rebranding with META as the new stock ticker sym...
summary: Facebook officially changed its stock ticker symbol from FB to META on June 9, 2022, as part of its ongoing rebranding efforts. The company, now known as Meta Platforms, Inc., has used the FB ticker since its 2012 IPO. This change was announced by CEO Mark Zuckerburg in October 2021, and no action is required from shareholders.

title: Semiconductor giant Marvell plans ‘significant expansion’ of Kanata footprint after acquisition
summary: Marvell, a U.S. semiconductor company, is significantly expanding its presence in Canada, particularly in Kanata, following its acquisition of local microchip design firms. The company plans to build out its offices in Ottawa, Toronto, and Vancouver to develop semiconductor technology for cloud, 5G, and automotive markets. Marvell expects to leverage Ottawa's tech talent and academic partnerships for its growth, with a focus on cutting-edge technologies.

title: Meta class action alleges company’s platforms harm youths’ mental health
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Facebook and Meta Platforms by a young woman alleging that the companies' social media platforms caused her to develop an addiction, leading to mental health issues like depression and attempted suicide. The lawsuit claims that Meta executives prioritized advertising profits over the mental well-being of young users by designing addictive algorithms, despite knowing the vulnerabilities of developing brains. This case is reportedly one of many similar lawsuits planned by the law firm Beasley Allen.

title: Newmark Completes Sale of Moffett Green, Fully-Leased Trophy Campus in Silicon Valley
summary: Newmark has successfully completed the sale of Moffett Green, a 719,037 square-foot, fully-leased trophy office campus in Sunnyvale, California, to CommonWealth Partners, LLC. The transaction, which represents the largest single-asset investment office sale in Silicon Valley history, involved Meta Platforms, Inc. as the long-term tenant. Steven Golubchik and his team at Newmark facilitated the deal, highlighting continued investor demand for prime assets in high-barrier-to-entry markets and the strong performance of Moffett Park.

title: Meet Naima Greenwood | President & Chief Operating Officer, Yardstick Management
summary: The article features an interview with Naima Greenwood, President & Chief Operating Officer of Yardstick Management, the nation's leading Black-owned management consulting firm. Greenwood discusses the firm's significant impact on diversity, equity, and inclusion within global organizations like Amazon and Netflix, and their commitment to leveling the playing field for marginalized communities. She also shares personal insights about Atlanta, where Yardstick Management is headquartered, and highlights the important community work of the Villages of Carver YMCA.

title: Walmart enters shoppable TV via exclusive deal with Roku
summary: Walmart has partnered with Roku to introduce shoppable TV, allowing customers to purchase products directly through their Roku remotes while streaming. This collaboration aims to simplify the shopping experience on the largest screen in the home and integrates with Roku Pay for seamless checkout. This initiative is part of Walmart's broader strategy to expand its livestream shopping efforts across various digital platforms.

title: A closer look at P&G’s ‘water positive’ pledge
summary: Procter & Gamble has announced a "water positive" goal by 2030, aiming to restore more water than its operations and products consume. The initiative includes two new restoration pledges focusing on water consumed during manufacturing and by household product use in 18 water-stressed regions, primarily Los Angeles and Mexico City. P&G is investing in various restoration projects and integrating sustainability metrics into executive compensation, while experts commend the company's rigorous approach to consumer-use impact but call for more engagement with public policy for broader impact.

title: DOJ Files Lawsuit Against Meta and Outlines Proposed Settlement Structure Over Allegedly Discriminatory Housing Advertising Practices
summary: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has filed a lawsuit and a proposed settlement framework against Meta Platforms, Inc. (formerly Facebook) over allegations that Meta's advertising algorithms for housing ads discriminate based on protected characteristics like race, color, religion, sex, disability, familial status, and national origin, violating the Fair Housing Act. The lawsuit claims Meta used tools like "Lookalike Audience" that considered protected characteristics and that its ad delivery system relied on machine-learning algorithms incorporating these characteristics. The proposed settlement requires Meta to cease using certain tools, develop a new non-discriminatory system by December 2022, implement an independent reviewer, and pay a $115,054 civil penalty.

title: 7 Top Metaverse Stocks with Massive Potential
summary: The metaverse market is projected to reach $5 trillion by 2030, with a significant majority of consumers and industry leaders anticipating positive impacts. This article highlights seven top metaverse stocks and ETFs, including Global X Metaverse ETF (VR), ProShares Metaverse ETF (VERS), Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV), Meta Platforms (META), Nvidia (NVDA), Roblox (RBLX), and Unity Software (U), recommending them as key investment opportunities in this emerging sector. It notes that many of these stocks are currently oversold, presenting potential for future gains as the metaverse concept gains traction.
