The company began notifying employees in its international sourcing division and plans further notifications at its San Francisco headquarters.

title: Accenture is cutting 75 San Jose workers
summary: Accenture is cutting 75 employees based at Google's campus in North San Jose, appearing to be cutting contractors who worked for Google. This news comes amidst a wave of other major layoffs by tech companies including First Republic, Google, Lyft, and Meta Platforms.

title: Gap Inc: Gap to cut hundreds of jobs in new round of layoffs - WSJ
summary: Apparel retailer Gap Inc. is reportedly cutting hundreds of jobs from its global workforce, a move larger than its previous layoffs in September. This decision comes as Gap joins other American firms like Meta Platforms, Clorox, and Wayfair in downsizing their workforces amid growing concerns of a recession in the United States. Notifications for layoffs in the international sourcing division began on April 18, with plans to inform San Francisco headquarters staff this week.

title: Introducing Jim Squires, EVP of Business Marketing and Growth, and New Orgs
summary: Reddit announced the appointment of Jim Squires as EVP of Business Marketing and Growth, who will report to COO Jen Wong and focus on expanding Reddit's Ads Business and international advertiser footprint. Concurrently, Roxy Young's role evolved to Chief Marketing and Consumer Experience Officer, reporting directly to CEO Steve Huffman, to lead a new "CX" organization encompassing Community, Communications, and Creative to elevate Reddit's unique community aspect. These changes aim to strengthen Reddit's market presence and innovative advertising solutions while furthering its community-driven marketing strategy.

title: Elon Musk Threatens to Sue Microsoft Over Alleged Illegal Data Usage
summary: Elon Musk is threatening to sue Microsoft, alleging unauthorized use of Twitter's data for training its AI models. This comes after Microsoft stopped supporting Twitter on one of its advertising platforms due to Musk's high asking price for API access. Musk claims Microsoft "trained illegally using Twitter data" and sees this as an opportune moment for legal action, especially given Microsoft's $10 billion investment in OpenAI and the integration of GPT-4 into Bing.

title: 2 Invesco ETFs That Offer Meta Exposure
summary: As Meta Platforms Inc. shares surge, two Invesco ETFs—the Invesco NASDAQ Internet ETF (PNQI) and the Invesco Dynamic Media ETF (PBS)—offer significant exposure to the company. Meta is the top holding in both ETFs, with weights of 9.1% and 6.6% respectively. Investors looking to capitalize on Meta's strong performance and broader internet or media sectors may consider these funds.

title: U.S. government has 'serious concerns' that some H-1B visa applicants are trying to game the system
summary: The U.S. government has expressed "serious concerns" that some H-1B visa applicants are manipulating the lottery system, as application numbers soared by 61% this year. Authorities suspect some applicants are submitting multiple bids through different company names without genuine job offers to gain an unfair advantage. U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services is conducting fraud investigations and referring some cases to federal prosecutors, noting a significant increase in registrations tied to multiple applications for the same individual.

title: Monument class action alleges telehealth platform shared personal health info with third parties
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Monument Inc., an online alcohol counseling and treatment company, alleging it shared patients' private health and identifiable information with third parties like Meta and Google without consent. The lawsuit claims Monument used a tracking pixel to transmit data including names, birthdates, and IP addresses, and that the company acknowledged disclosing information of over 108,000 individuals. The plaintiff seeks to represent a nationwide class and a Florida subclass, demanding a jury trial, injunctive relief, and damages.

title: 5 Amazing Ways Meta (Facebook) Is Using Generative AI
summary: Meta, the parent company of Facebook, is heavily investing in generative AI despite its initial focus on the metaverse. The company is implementing generative AI across various platforms to enhance advertising, power AI-driven chat functionalities, revolutionize image and natural language generation, and ultimately, simplify content creation within its metaverse platform, Horizons. These initiatives aim to make content creation more accessible and potentially increase user engagement with Meta's virtual world.

title: 3M laying off 1,100 at Maplewood headquarters
summary: 3M is laying off 1,100 employees at its Maplewood headquarters, which represents 10% of its local workforce and is part of a larger global cost-cutting initiative. This follows an earlier announcement of 6,000 global job cuts and an additional 2,500 manufacturing job reductions, bringing the total workforce reduction to 10% worldwide. Economists suggest these layoffs signal broader economic impacts from the Fed's interest rate hikes and changes in fiscal stimulus.

title: 40 Under 40: Champions of Construction 2022
summary: This article celebrates Autodesk's 40 Under 40: Champions of Construction for 2022, highlighting 40 individuals who are making significant contributions to the construction industry through innovation, leadership, and digital transformation. It details their roles, achievements, and impact on their respective companies and the broader AEC sector. The honorees are recognized for advancing digital maturity, implementing new technologies, and fostering collaborative and sustainable practices.

title: CII Opposes Admitting Dual-Class Stock to S&P Indexes
summary: The Council of Institutional Investors (CII) has expressed "surprise and disappointment" over S&P Dow Jones Indices' decision to reverse its 2017 ban on admitting new dual-class stock companies to its indexes. This policy change, which allows companies like Blackstone into the S&P 500 despite having unequal voting rights, is criticized by CII for its opaque rationale and lack of investor consultation. S&P defends the change by citing the proliferation of dual-class structures, especially in tech, and the inconsistency of its previous grandfathering policy.

title: Peter Thiel's Palantir Sees Skyrocketing Demand for AI Technologies: What Its CEO Has to Say
summary: Palantir is experiencing skyrocketing demand for its new Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP), leading to a significant stock increase after strong Q1 results. CEO Alex Karp and co-founder Peter Thiel are highly confident in Palantir's AI leadership, believing the market underestimates their progress and their strategy to dominate the AI market by creating value for hundreds of customers. The company aims for profitability on a GAAP basis throughout the rest of the year.

title: Former ByteDance executive says he was dismissed for flagging illegal activity
summary: A former ByteDance head of engineering, Yintao "Roger" Yu, claims he was fired after reporting that the company was illegally taking user content from platforms like Instagram and Snapchat. Yu's lawsuit alleges ByteDance engaged in a "worldwide scheme to steal and profit from the content of others" and fabricated user metrics for propaganda. ByteDance denies these claims, stating they will "vigorously oppose" the "baseless allegations."

title: FTC Sues to Block Amgen’s Acquisition of Horizon Therapeutics
summary: The FTC is suing to block Amgen's $27.8 billion acquisition of Horizon Therapeutics, citing concerns that it could create a monopoly in the market for critical thyroid eye disease and chronic refractory gout treatments. The agency believes Amgen could leverage its existing drug portfolio to pressure healthcare providers and insurers into favoring Horizon's drugs, Tepezza and Krystexxa, which currently face no market competition. Amgen has expressed disappointment but remains committed to completing the acquisition, asserting it will benefit patients with rare diseases.

title: 3 Tech Stocks That Could Turn $1,000 into $10,000 by 2030
summary: This article identifies three tech stocks – Snap Inc. (SNAP), Unity Software (U), and Luminar Technologies (LAZR) – that are predicted to offer significant growth potential and turn a $1,000 investment into $10,000 by 2030. The author details the specific catalysts and growth drivers for each company, such as ARPU expansion for Snap, continued gaming industry growth for Unity, and LiDAR adoption in EVs for Luminar. The piece highlights that while many tech giants have rallied, these companies present compelling entry points despite short-term challenges.

title: Giphy Sold by Meta at a Loss to Shutterstock for $53 Million
summary: Meta has sold Giphy to Shutterstock for $53 million, incurring a significant loss after acquiring it for $400 million three years prior. This forced sale follows a ruling from the U.K.'s Competition and Markets Authority that deemed Meta's acquisition anticompetitive. Shutterstock plans to integrate Giphy to expand its audience reach beyond professional marketing, expecting minimal revenue contribution in 2023 with focused monetization efforts in 2024.

title: GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: The article provides detailed information about the GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL), including its current price, holdings, and recent news. FBL is an ETF that offers 1.5x leveraged exposure to the daily price movement of Meta Platforms, Inc. Class A stock. It covers key statistics, expense ratios, news headlines, frequently asked questions about performance and splits, and fund details like inception date and management.

title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
summary: VettaFi’s Tom Lydon discusses the Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) which offers exposure to top holdings favored by hedge funds without an overweight toward any single stock. GVIP provides diversification across various companies, including some mid-cap and international stocks, making it a less volatile option for investors seeking innovative companies. It provides a consensus of top companies chosen by leading hedge fund managers, appealing to investors looking for strong, well-vetted companies.

title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
summary: The Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) is highlighted as the ETF of the Week. GVIP provides exposure to top stock picks from hedge funds, focusing on a consensus of leading managers rather than an overweighting of individual stocks. The ETF diversifies holdings across large-cap, mid-cap, and international companies, offering a less volatile way for investors to participate in innovative companies.

title: Google announces US-Taiwan-Philippines cable with Chunghwa, Globe, and AT&T
summary: Google has announced plans for a new subsea cable system, TPU (Taiwan-Philippines-US), linking the US, Taiwan, and the Philippines, partnering with Chunghwa Telecom, Innove (Globe Group), and AT&T. This new cable, expected to be ready for service in 2025 with NEC as the supplier, will provide 260Tbps of capacity and offer an alternative route to Google's existing Apricot cable, enhancing resilience and capacity in the APAC region. Additionally, the article notes another subsea cable development, the EMC West system in the Middle East, being built by Center3 and ASN.

title: Right-wing investment fund dumps Target stock over ‘woke’ agenda, adds retailer to ‘refuse to buy’ list
summary: The American Conservative Values ETF (ACVF) has divested its holdings in Target Corp. and added the retailer to its "Refuse to Buy" list, citing the company's "woke" agenda and its handling of Pride merchandising. ACVF criticized Target's "ever-increasing pandering to the Woke LGBT agenda," stating that it has damaged the brand and will likely harm its long-term stock performance. This move comes as Target faces backlash from both conservatives and liberals regarding its LGBTQ+ merchandise displays and subsequent actions.

title: NVIDIA Announces DGX GH200 AI Supercomputer
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled the DGX GH200 AI supercomputer, designed to handle massive models for generative AI, recommender systems, and data analytics. This new system connects 256 Grace Hopper Superchips to function as a single 1-exaflop, 144TB GPU, offering significantly more memory than its predecessor. Companies like Google Cloud, Meta, and Microsoft are among the first to explore its capabilities, and NVIDIA is also building its own Helios supercomputer based on this design.

title: WPP shakes up creative process with AI-powered content engine
summary: WPP has partnered with Nvidia to launch a new AI-powered content engine aimed at transforming the creative process for clients. This engine, built on Nvidia's Omniverse Cloud, will leverage generative AI tools from Adobe and Getty Images to create more realistic and brand-accurate imagery and videos efficiently. The initiative positions WPP as a leader in AI adoption within the advertising industry, addressing the fast-paced demands of digital marketing and potential economic pressures.

title: In challenge to Meta, Apple expected to unveil mixed-reality headset
summary: Apple is expected to announce a new mixed-reality headset at its annual software developer conference, positioning itself against Meta Platforms in the emerging market for virtual and augmented reality. While Meta's CEO Mark Zuckerberg has championed the "metaverse" and has products out, Apple has been more private about its augmented-reality efforts, which so far have been limited to existing devices. Analysts suggest Apple's device, rumored to cost around $3,000, is an interim step toward transparent augmented-reality glasses and aims to encourage software developers to create compelling "XR" applications where no "killer app" currently exists.

title: Judge dismisses Washington, DC, privacy lawsuit against Facebook owner Meta
summary: A Washington, D.C.,privacy lawsuit from 2018 against Facebook's parent company, Meta Platforms Inc., has been dismissed by a Superior Court judge. The judge ruled that Meta did not mislead consumers regarding the Cambridge Analytica scandal, stating that Facebook's policies had disclosed how third parties could obtain user data and provided instructions to limit data sharing. The District of Columbia attorney general's office expressed disagreement with the decision and is considering its options.

title: $9B Momentum ETF Is Unrecognizable After Makeover
summary: The iShares MSCI USA Momentum Factor ETF (MTUM), valued at $9 billion, has undergone a significant rebalancing, dramatically changing its top holdings and tilting heavily towards technology stocks. This makeover has raised questions about whether the fund is too late to capitalize on the recent tech rally, especially given its recent underperformance compared to the broader market. Despite recent struggles and outflows, MTUM is still a substantial ETF, and its future performance hinges on the durability of current market trends, particularly in technology.

title: With a New Developer Framework, Building in XR is Easier than Ever for the Spatial Computing Revolution
summary: Qualcomm is making significant strides in the Extended Reality (XR) space with its Snapdragon Spaces™ XR Developer Platform, aiming to simplify development for immersive experiences. Hugo Swart, Qualcomm's VP and GM of XR, highlights the new "Dual Render Fusion" feature which allows developers to convert 2D smartphone apps into AR Glass-capable experiences, lowering the barrier to entry for many. The company envisions an "spatial internet" where XR devices will eventually replace smartphones and PCs for most computing tasks, transforming industries from training and education to social interaction and entertainment.

title: Democratic Large Cap Core ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (DEMZ)
summary: The Democratic Large Cap Core ETF (DEMZ) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with a price target of $41.72, suggesting a 0.31% potential upside. This rating is based on 651 analyst ratings covering 25 of the ETF's constituent companies. Holdings with strong ratings include NVIDIA and Microsoft, while Loews has a lower rating score.

title: Reddit CEO Steve Huffman: 'It's time we grow up and behave like an adult company'
summary: In his first interview since a widespread subreddit blackout, Reddit CEO Steve Huffman defended the company's decision to charge third-party apps for data access, stating it's necessary for Reddit to be a sustainable business. He acknowledged the protest but maintained that the company would not reverse its plans, emphasizing that Reddit can no longer afford to give its valuable data away for free, especially with the rise of AI models leveraging it. Huffman views the move as an essential step for the 18-year-old company to mature and operate as an "adult company."

title: More Loop Tenants Shy Away From Offices With Subleases
summary: PayPal and Omnicom Group have added a combined 122,000 square feet of office space to Chicago's sublease market, contributing to a record high of over 8 million square feet available. This increase highlights a trend where the amount of sublease space being added significantly outpaces leasing, driven by companies adapting to hybrid and remote work. The West Loop and Central Loop are particularly affected, holding the largest portions of the available sublease inventory.

title: Some ESG Issues Take Center Stage at Big Tech Investor Meetings
summary: ESG principles are increasingly important at big tech companies, with shareholder demands evolving beyond traditional environmental concerns to focus on social and governance issues. Recent investor meetings at Alphabet and Meta, both holdings in the Invesco ESG Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQMG), saw a rise in shareholder resolutions concerning online safety, gender/racial equity, human rights, and executive compensation. This trend highlights the growing emphasis on the "S" and "G" in ESG, potentially positioning QQMG as a relevant investment for those seeking exposure to mega-cap growth with an ESG focus.

title: TV news at a crossroads as financial strain deepens, experts say
summary: BCE Inc.'s request to waive local news and Canadian programming requirements signals a deepening financial strain on Canadian journalism, according to media industry experts. This move, following significant job cuts and station closures by Bell, raises concerns about the future of local news coverage, which experts argue is crucial for democracy. While the industry grapples with adapting to digital consumption and the effectiveness of government solutions like Bill C-18 is questioned, the debate highlights the challenges of funding quality journalism when few Canadians are willing to pay for online news.

title: Cognizant to lay off nearly 60 working in Facebook's North Austin tower
summary: Cognizant, a contractor for Facebook, is set to lay off nearly 60 employees who work at Facebook's North Austin tower. This news follows previous job cuts by another Facebook contractor at the same location, indicating a trend of workforce reductions among companies contracting with the social media giant. The layoffs will impact employees at 11800 Alterra Pkwy., the building entirely leased by Facebook.

title: TThe High Price of Low Volatility | U.S. Equity market commentary 2Q23
summary: This article by Bill Nygren, Portfolio Manager at Oakmark, discusses how different entities define investment risk and argues against the common perception of index funds as low-risk, highlighting increasing concentration risk in indices like the NASDAQ 100 and S&P 500. He contrasts this with Oakmark's approach, which focuses on long-term value investing and accepts higher short-term volatility for potentially better long-term returns. Nygren contends that index fund investors and those seeking low volatility stocks are overlooking significant valuation risks.

title: Cisco bats for universal AI regulation
summary: Cisco is advocating for universal AI regulation, emphasizing the need for a global public-private dialogue due to AI's far-reaching impact. The company believes AI should be used responsibly with transparency and collaboration between the public and private sectors to establish regulatory frameworks, including content attribution and copyright. This stance aligns with other tech giants like Microsoft and Google who also support AI regulation, and comes as the EU is actively pursuing legislation in this area.

title: Communication Services Stock Outlook: As Meta and Alphabet Soar, Traditional Telecom Comes Under Renewed Pressure
summary: This article discusses the contrasting performances within the communication services sector, highlighting the surge in Meta and Alphabet shares due to renewed online advertising demand and AI excitement. Conversely, traditional telecom companies like Verizon and AT&T face renewed pressure, though recent price increases and cost-cutting efforts are expected to improve their cash flow. The report also provides specific picks and fair value estimates for Disney, Verizon Communications, and Pinterest.

title: Sarah Silverman sues Meta, OpenAI for copyright infringement
summary: Comedian Sarah Silverman and two authors have filed class action lawsuits against Meta Platforms and OpenAI for copyright infringement. They allege that both companies used their copyrighted material without permission to train their artificial intelligence language models. The lawsuits seek unspecified monetary damages for copyright owners whose works were allegedly infringed.

title: Deutsche Bank Maintains Meta Platforms Inc - (META) Buy Recommendation
summary: Deutsche Bank has reiterated its Buy recommendation for Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ:META) as of July 12, 2023. Despite an average one-year price target suggesting a slight downside of 2.87% from its current price, the bank anticipates a 5.71% increase in annual revenue and a non-GAAP EPS of 8.01. Institutional ownership of META has increased, with major funds like Vanguard and Fidelity holding significant positions, indicating a bullish outlook.

title: An End to Regulatory Risk in China’s Internet Sector
summary: Recent meetings between Premier Li Qiang and heads of major Chinese internet companies signal an end to a period of regulatory crackdown in the sector, turning a significant investor headwind into a tailwind. This shift, marked by commitments to transparency and growth support, is seen as paving the way for more sustainable development in China’s internet economy. Investors are looking to capture this potential growth through instruments like the KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF (KWEB), which tracks key Chinese tech companies.

title: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon speaks up about remote work: 'there are real flaws'
summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has voiced significant skepticism about remote work, stating "there are real flaws" with the model. He believes it hinders creativity, spontaneity, and is not effective for management teams or younger apprentices. Dimon noted that while he understands why some prefer remote work, it must ultimately benefit the company and its clients.

title: Caribbean phone companies suffering from “market failure” due to Over-The Top services, Social Media
summary: Caribbean telecommunication companies are experiencing "market failure" due to the prevalent use of Over-The-Top (OTT) services and social media, which consume the majority of data and international call traffic, leading to significant financial losses for local providers. Despite annual investments exceeding US$500 million in network upgrades since 2017, these companies face declining voice revenues and a lack of significant contribution from major tech companies that utilize their infrastructure. Industry leaders are advocating for government intervention and a unified CARICOM approach to address this imbalance and ensure a fair share from big tech, warning of dire consequences for regional connectivity and economic development if the issue remains unresolved.

title: Microsoft and Meta expand their AI partnership with Llama 2 on Azure and Windows
summary: Microsoft and Meta are expanding their AI partnership, bringing Meta's Llama 2 large language models to Azure and Windows. This collaboration aims to democratize AI by providing developers with open models, enabling them to build generative AI tools and experiences. Azure customers can now fine-tune and deploy Llama 2 models, while Windows developers can run Llama locally, integrating AI safety features from both companies.

title: How will the metaverse change our lives?
summary: This article explores the concept of the metaverse, its origins, and its potential impact on our lives, particularly within financial services. It delves into how the metaverse, driven by emerging technologies like Augmented Reality and Blockchain, could revolutionize banking with new e-commerce experiences and personalized financial assistance. The author also discusses the challenges and considerations, such as identity and fraud prevention, that need to be addressed as the metaverse develops.

title: Barbenheimer and Redefining Movie Marketing Strategies
summary: The "Barbenheimer" phenomenon, involving the simultaneous release and unexpected mutual bolstering of "Barbie" and "Oppenheimer," significantly boosted the theatrical movie business. Both films achieved impressive box office numbers domestically, with "Barbie" leveraging social media, especially TikTok, to drive engagement and redefine marketing strategies. This success highlights the importance of tailored social media approaches and presents new opportunities for long-term growth and audience connection for brands.

title: Meta Reports After Market Close; Watch Overnight Returns
summary: Shares of Meta (META) are trading higher ahead of its Q2 earnings release, with potential for significant overnight returns. The article highlights how most earnings reports and key macro updates, like CPI data, are released during the overnight session, impacting stock performance before the market opens. Investors can use products like the NightShares 500 1x/1.5x ETF (NSPL) to capitalize on these overnight movements.

title: Apple, Meta, Google argue they're not 'bookies' in casino app appeal
summary: Apple, Meta, and Google are appealing a 2022 ruling that found them potentially liable for facilitating purchases of virtual chips in casino game apps, arguing that this interpretation of Section 230 could jeopardize the entire internet economy. They contend that holding platforms liable for payment processing related to third-party content would force extensive monitoring and is a misinterpretation of existing legal precedents. The companies emphasize they act as publishers offering payment services, not creators of the allegedly illegal apps or chips, and that their conduct in these cases is distinct from prior rulings.

title: ETFs to Tap on Meta's Blowout Q2 Earnings
summary: Meta Platforms (META) reported strong Q2 2023 results, exceeding revenue and earnings estimates, driven by AI and marking its first double-digit revenue growth since 2021. This article suggests investors consider ETFs with significant META allocation, highlighting specific communication services and tech-focused ETFs. The strong performance led to a 7.5% share spike in aftermarket hours, and the company issued upbeat revenue guidance for the current quarter.

title: YieldMax Launches Synthetic Covered Call ETFs for Meta, Google
summary: YieldMax has introduced two new actively managed ETFs, the YieldMax META Option Income Strategy ETF (FBY) and the YieldMax GOOGL Option Income Strategy ETF (GOOY), designed to generate monthly income through synthetic covered call strategies on Meta and Alphabet stock, respectively. These ETFs, which do not directly invest in the underlying stocks, follow the recent launch of the YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (AMZY) and cater to increasing advisor interest in options-based income strategies. Both new funds carry an expense ratio of 0.99% and are advised by Tidal Financial Group and subadvised by ZEGA Financial.

title: YieldMax Launches Synthetic Covered Call ETFs for Meta, Google
summary: YieldMax has introduced two new actively managed ETFs, FBY and GOOY, designed to generate monthly income through synthetic covered call strategies on Meta Platforms (META) and Alphabet (GOOGL), respectively. These ETFs do not invest directly in the underlying stocks but instead sell call options. This expansion follows the recent launch of a similar ETF for Amazon (AMZN) and reflects a growing advisor interest in options-based strategies for enhanced income.

title: AI and Data Centers: It’s About Keeping Cool
summary: A report from Citi Research highlights the critical role data centers play in the AI revolution, emphasizing that keeping servers cool will be vital. Despite historical predictions, data center energy use has been largely stable due to efficiency gains, but AI's computational demands are accelerating, making cooling and renewable energy increasingly important for sustainability. The article notes that while AI compute has outpaced Moore's Law, increased scrutiny of carbon emissions will drive higher corporate renewable PPAs.

title: The Advisors Guide to Using NSPL to Enhance Portfolios
summary: This article explains how the NightShares 500 1x/1.5x ETF (NSPL) can enhance investment portfolios by capturing the "night effect," a historical market pattern where overnight trading sessions contribute most of the returns and less volatility. By leveraging this effect, NSPL aims to provide improved risk-adjusted returns, as demonstrated by its favorable Sharpe ratio compared to traditional S&P 500 exposure. The ETF's strategy is designed to capture overnight gains without missing daytime performance.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 07/30/2023
summary: This article details the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending July 30, 2023, highlighting that these funds are high-risk due to leverage. Key performers include China-focused ETFs like Direxion Daily FTSE China Bull 3X Shares (YINN) and Direxion Daily CSI China Internet Index Bull 2x Shares (CWEB), along with those tracking electric vehicles (EVAV) and specific tech giants like Meta (FBL) and Google (GGLL). The performance was driven by factors such as China's policy actions, growth in the EV sector, and strong tech earnings.

title: PhishForce: New phishing attack involving Salesforce and Facebook uncovered
summary: Security researchers at Guardio Labs have uncovered a new, sophisticated phishing attack dubbed "PhishForce" targeting Salesforce customers. This attack cleverly spoofs Salesforce email servers and domain names and uses legitimate email gateways to hide malicious emails that redirect users to phishing pages designed to steal Facebook account details. Both Salesforce and Facebook have been notified and have taken steps to address the vulnerability.

title: FBY Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:FBY
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the YieldMax META Option Income Strategy ETF (FBY), including its price performance, key statistics like AUM and dividend yield, and its investment strategy. FBY aims to generate monthly income and provide exposure to Meta Platforms (META) stock's price returns through a synthetic covered call strategy, although it does not invest directly in META shares. The article also includes technical analysis with oscillators and moving averages, seasonal trends, and a detailed FAQ section about ETFs and FBY's specifics.

title: 3 Best Meme ETFs to Buy Now
summary: This article explores the concept of meme ETFs, which are exchange-traded funds centered around "meme stocks" known for rapid and unpredictable price movements driven by online communities. It explains the potential benefits of investing in meme ETFs, such as high short-term returns and educational opportunities for new investors, while also highlighting their volatility. The article details three specific meme ETFs: the Roundhill MEME ETF, the Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF, and the Fidelity Crypto Industry and Digital Payments ETF, providing examples of meme stocks like GameStop and AMC Entertainment.

title: AI as an investment theme
summary: The article discusses Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a significant investment theme due to its potential to revolutionize industries and create new opportunities. It highlights the booming AI sector, strong investor interest, and the impact of AI on technology stocks and the broader market. The author also touches upon various ways to invest in AI, including specific ETFs, and emphasizes the importance of careful research despite the vast potential.

title: YieldMax Launches Option Income Strategy ETF on Coinbase
summary: YieldMax has launched the YieldMax COIN Option Income Strategy ETF (CONY) on NYSE Arca, aiming to generate monthly income through a synthetic covered call strategy on Coinbase Global (COIN). This ETF, actively managed by ZEGA Financial, is part of a growing suite of YieldMax option income strategy ETFs focusing on growth companies that may not pay dividends. It provides investors with an opportunity to earn income and potentially protect against downside while gaining exposure to a growth company like Coinbase.

title: Deadline to file a claim in the $725M Facebook settlement is this week
summary: The deadline to file a claim in the $725 million Facebook settlement is August 25th. Individuals who used Facebook between May 24, 2007, and December 22, 2022, may be eligible for compensation, even if their accounts have since been deleted. The settlement addresses allegations that Facebook shared user data with third parties, including Cambridge Analytica, without permission.

title: Class-action Facebook privacy settlement: deadline to file a claim is this week
summary: The deadline to file a claim in the $725 million class-action Facebook privacy settlement is this week, specifically August 25, 2023. The settlement addresses allegations that Facebook allowed users' personal information to be accessed by third parties without permission, including the Cambridge Analytica scandal. U.S. Facebook users who had an account between May 24, 2007, and Dec. 22, 2022, are eligible to file, with the amount received depending on usage duration and the number of claims.

title: Meta Can't Exit Drivers' Suit Over Intercepted DMV Info
summary: A federal judge has denied Meta Platforms' request to dismiss a putative class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Meta unlawfully collected drivers' personal information from the California Department of Motor Vehicles website by using its tracking pixel. The judge found that the suit plausibly claims Meta obtained information derived from "a motor vehicle record" through this method.

title: Why Ireland is well positioned to capitalise on chip ‘gold rush’
summary: Ireland is well-positioned to capitalize on the booming semiconductor industry, which is projected to double by 2030, thanks to its long-standing presence of major players like Analog Devices and Intel. However, the country faces challenges including intense global competition for investment, capacity constraints for manufacturing, and a scarcity of electronic engineering talent. To maintain its competitive edge, Ireland must continue to invest in R&D, upgrade its infrastructure, and foster international collaboration within the semiconductor ecosystem.

title: How Will EU Regulations Impact These Social Media Stocks?
summary: The EU's new Digital Services Act (DSA) and Digital Markets Act (DMA) are poised to significantly impact major social media platforms like Meta, Snapchat, and TikTok, with potential fines for non-compliance. These regulations, particularly concerning data privacy and algorithmic transparency, have already delayed the launch of Meta's Threads in the EU. The article also discusses Threads' user retention challenges and potential competition from platforms like X and BlueSky, while highlighting ETFs for investing in social media companies.

title: Qualcomm, Meta Platforms rise; J.M. Smucker, RTX fall, Monday, 9/11/2023
summary: This article details the stock performance of several companies on Monday, September 11, 2023. Qualcomm and Meta Platforms saw their stock prices rise due to specific company news, while J.M. Smucker and RTX experienced declines following significant announcements. Other notable movers include Alibaba, FuelCell Energy, Carlisle Cos., and Freeport-McMoRan.

title: More writers sue OpenAI for copyright infringement over AI training
summary: A group of U.S. authors, including Pulitzer Prize winner Michael Chabon, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in federal court in San Francisco. They allege that OpenAI misused their copyrighted writings to train its AI-powered chatbot, ChatGPT, without permission. This is at least the third such class-action lawsuit by authors against OpenAI, with the plaintiffs seeking monetary damages and an injunction against OpenAI's business practices.

title: Pulitzer winner Chabon, other authors sue Meta over AI program
summary: A group of writers, including Pulitzer Prize winner Michael Chabon, Tony-winning playwright David Henry Hwang, and authors Matthew Klam, Rachel Louise Snyder, and Ayelet Waldman, have sued Meta Platforms in San Francisco federal court. They accuse Meta of misusing their copyrighted works to train its Llama artificial-intelligence software, alleging that pirated versions of their writings were used in the training datasets. This lawsuit follows a similar proposed class-action suit against OpenAI by the same authors, highlighting a growing legal challenge for AI companies regarding intellectual property.

title: Group targets Meta and peers over diversity hiring initiative for BIPOC film crew
summary: A conservative group is suing Meta Platforms and other film production companies over a diversity initiative called "Double the Line" (DTL). The lawsuit alleges that DTL, which aims to hire Black, Indigenous, and people of color (BIPOC) for commercial film production jobs, intentionally excludes White candidates. This legal challenge follows recent U.S. Supreme Court rulings and other lawsuits questioning the legality of workplace DEI programs.

title: Instacart Pops 12% On Nasdaq Debut: 6 ETFs in Focus
summary: Instacart's shares (CART) made their Nasdaq debut with a 12% increase, though this gain was short-lived. The company's IPO valued it at nearly $9.9 billion, significantly less than its 2021 valuation. The article also highlights six ETFs that could be in focus following Instacart's market debut, offering diversified exposure to the IPO market or related sectors.

title: Communications ETF XLC Offers Loud & Clear Exposure to the Space
summary: The Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC) provides investors with exposure to the growing digital side of the U.S. economy, encompassing communications, media, and technology companies. Launched following a 2018 GICS reclassification, XLC tracks a market-cap-weighted index of U.S. telecom, media, and entertainment components of the S&P 500, with top holdings including Meta Platforms and Alphabet. This ETF offers an accessible and low-cost way to invest in leading digital economy companies.

title: Meta Platforms hit with US trademark lawsuit from tech company Metabyte
summary: Meta Platforms is facing a new trademark infringement lawsuit from tech company Metabyte, which claims Meta's rebrand causes consumer confusion due to overlapping services and geographic areas. This marks at least the fourth U.S. trademark case against Meta since its 2021 metaverse-related rebrand. Metabyte, in business since 1993, seeks to block Meta from using the "Meta" name and requests unspecified monetary damages after talks between the companies broke down.

title: FNGG ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:FNGG
summary: This article provides an analysis of the Direxion Daily NYSE FANG+ Bull 2X Shares (FNGG) ETF, detailing its key characteristics such as assets under management, dividend yield, expense ratio, and holdings. FNGG offers 200% leveraged exposure to the NYSE FANG+ Index, focusing on big technology and innovative companies. The report also covers its recent performance, dividend history, and answers frequently asked questions about the fund.

title: Supermicro At 30: From Designing AI Chips To Selling AI Systems
summary: Supermicro, founded 30 years ago, began with Charles Liang's vision to improve server motherboards and power supplies after working on an AI processor design. The company has grown into a $10 billion systems business with global manufacturing facilities and aims for $20 billion in the next three years, continuously expanding its operations and exploring new factory locations.

title: Elon Musk's X hit with trademark lawsuit from marketing agency
summary: X Corp, formerly Twitter, is facing a trademark infringement lawsuit from a legal-marketing company called X Social Media. The Florida-based company claims that X Corp's rebranding is causing consumer confusion and has led to lost revenue, given their prior use and federal trademark for "X Social Media" since 2016. This lawsuit is potentially the first of many such disputes due to the common use of the letter "X" in tech branding.

title: Procore puts name atop Sixth Street office tower
summary: The Chase Tower in Austin has been renamed Procore Tower after Procore Technologies Inc., a construction management software company, leased over 100,000 square feet in the 21-story high-rise. This move highlights Procore's growing presence in Austin and indicates a strong occupancy rate for the building in a challenging market. The article provides insights into Procore's rise and the real estate aspects of the newly named tower.

title: Top Performing ETFs: Gold, Income ETFs Top the Charts
summary: This article highlights that gold, commodities, and income options strategies were the top-performing ETFs of the week, with particular success for options-based income ETFs from YieldMax focusing on tech stocks. It notes that commodities like natural gas also performed strongly, likely due to market fears, while gold strategies and interest-rate-hedging strategies continue to exhibit strong performance. The article concludes that a diverse suite of strategies, including income ETFs and gold, are available for investors.

title: Costco Sued, Accused Of Sharing Confidential Health Data With Facebook
summary: Costco is facing a lawsuit in a Seattle federal court, accused of sharing sensitive customer health information with Meta Platforms Inc. through the Meta Pixel tracking tool used on its website. The lawsuit, filed by four California residents, claims Costco violated Washington State's privacy laws by not disclosing its data-sharing practices, especially concerning susceptible health data of pharmacy users. Plaintiffs are seeking a jury trial and financial damages, while Meta states its policies prohibit advertisers from sending sensitive information.

title: Who owns the news? Mail titles, News UK and Reach dominate, report finds
summary: A new report, "Who Owns the UK Media? 2023," reveals that three companies—DMG Media, News UK, and Reach—control 90% of the UK's national newspaper market and over 40% of the top 50 online news brands. This concentration, also prevalent in local news, raises concerns about media diversity and the influence these conglomerates exert on the national agenda. The report calls for urgent reform to address this concentrated ownership and regulate the dominant tech platforms that act as gatekeepers to news.

title: Stagwell (STGW) Acquires Left Field Labs, Deepening Technology Capabilities in AI, Digital Transformation and Immersive Experiences
summary: Stagwell (NASDAQ: STGW) has acquired Left Field Labs, a digital agency specializing in AI, digital transformation, and immersive experiences, to enhance its technology capabilities. Left Field Labs will join Stagwell's Constellation Network, bringing end-to-end services and expertise in human-centric design to guide clients. This acquisition marks Stagwell's third in 2023, reinforcing its strategy to lead AI-based digital transformation in marketing.

title: On Amazon, eBay, and Shopify, AI is the new third-party seller
summary: Amazon, eBay, and Shopify are actively integrating generative AI tools to assist third-party sellers with tasks like creating product listings. These AI capabilities aim to simplify labor-intensive duties, such as writing compelling product descriptions and titles, thereby boosting sales and reducing effort for business owners. Beyond listings, AI is expanding into data analytics, customer review analysis, and marketing campaign generation, offering comprehensive support for e-commerce entrepreneurs.

title: Biden campaign joins Donald Trump’s Truth Social platform
summary: President Joe Biden's reelection campaign has launched an account on Donald Trump's Truth Social platform to reach conservative voters and combat misinformation. The campaign's strategy involves posting clips of Republican primary candidates criticizing Trump. This move reflects a broader shift in Democratic campaign tactics towards more direct online engagement with opponents.

title: Stanford’s AI transparency index shows foundational models are shrouded in secrecy
summary: A Stanford University report reveals that most popular foundational AI models from companies like OpenAI, Google, and Meta lack transparency regarding their development and societal impact. Meta's Llama 2 topped the transparency index, while OpenAI's GPT-4 scored lower despite extensive public information from partners, indicating that open-source models generally lead in transparency. The researchers highlight the need for greater disclosure to inform policymakers and foster trust in AI, especially as regulations like the EU's Artificial Intelligence Act emerge.

title: Authors sue Meta, Microsoft, Bloomberg in latest AI copyright clash
summary: A group of authors including former Arkansas governor Mike Huckabee and Lysa TerKeurst have filed a class-action lawsuit against Meta, Microsoft, and Bloomberg, alleging unauthorized use of their copyrighted works to train AI systems. The lawsuit claims the companies used the "Books3" dataset, which contains pirated books, to train their large language models. The authors are seeking unspecified money damages and an injunction to prevent further misuse of their work.

title: Micron Low-Power Memory Solution Boosts Mixed and Virtual Reality Experiences on Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2
summary: Micron Technology announced that its LPDDR5X DRAM and UFS 3.1 embedded solutions have been qualified for Qualcomm's Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2 Platform. These solutions offer significant speed, performance, and low-power consumption in small form factors, essential for untethered mixed reality (MR) and virtual reality (VR) devices. This advancement aims to enhance immersive experiences, with the Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2 processor already commercially debuted in Meta Quest 3, promoting thinner and more comfortable headsets with extended battery life.

title: Apple to spend $1 billion on AI in catch-up move: report
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly investing $1 billion to accelerate its efforts in generative artificial intelligence. This move comes after the company was reportedly "caught off guard" by the rapid rise of AI tools like ChatGPT last year. The investment aims to integrate generative AI technology across all Apple products, with the initiative being led by senior vice presidents John Giannandrea and Craig Federighi.

title: Hartford Funds Seeks to Let $4.1B Fund Make More Concentrated Investments
summary: Hartford Funds is proposing to reclassify its $4.1 billion Growth Opportunities Fund as a non-diversified investment, which would allow it to make more concentrated investments. This change, if approved by shareholders, would increase the fund's risk profile and volatility, prompting plan sponsors and fiduciaries to re-evaluate their inclusion of the fund in retirement lineups. The reclassification comes as allocations to a few mega-cap tech stocks have pushed some diversified funds to exceed investment thresholds.

title: Shoot, Don’t Kill
summary: Byrna Technologies, a less-lethal weapon manufacturer, is attempting to create a market for "less lethal" guns for self-defense amidst a polarized American gun culture. Their devices use compressed gas to launch non-lethal projectiles, offering an alternative to traditional firearms. Despite challenges in marketing and operational scaling, Byrna aims to appeal to a broad consumer base by highlighting their products as safe and unregulated alternatives for self-defense.

title: GoodRx class action claims company shares personal info with Meta, Google, others
summary: GoodRx is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it shared customers' personal and sensitive health information with third parties like Meta, Google, and Criteo without consent. The lawsuit claims GoodRx violated federal wiretapping statutes and consumer protection laws by allowing tracking pixels to intercept user communications, including mouse movements, clicks, and entered information. Plaintiffs argue that releasing sensitive physical and mental health data to advertisers negatively impacts them.

title: The Magnificent 7 In Other Countries (Emerging Markets X-China)
summary: This article identifies seven emerging market companies (outside of China) that could become the "next Magnificent 7" in technology, similar to the dominant tech giants in developed markets. It highlights companies from Latin America, India, and Southeast Asia, spanning fintech, e-commerce, and digital services, and details their unique strategies, market positions, and growth potential. The author emphasizes a long-term perspective on these companies' appreciation and their role in the global technology landscape.

title: Costco class action alleges retailer shared customer health info with Meta
summary: Costco is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it shared customers' personal health information with Meta through embedded tracking tools like Facebook Pixel, without consent. The lawsuit claims that this data, gathered from Costco's pharmacy website where customers manage prescriptions, is not anonymous and is linked to specific Facebook user profiles. The plaintiffs argue that Costco's actions violate HIPAA regulations designed to protect private health information.

title: Coursera class action claims company shares customer viewing history with Meta
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Coursera Inc., alleging the company shares customers' video viewing history and personally identifiable information with Meta Platforms without consent. The lawsuit claims Coursera uses an invisible tracking tool called "Meta Pixel" to facilitate this data sharing, violating the Video Privacy Protection Act. The plaintiff seeks to represent a nationwide class of individuals who viewed Coursera videos while having active Facebook accounts.

title: Walmart agrees to huge South Bay sublease deal, in lift for office market
summary: Walmart's digital unit has signed a significant sublease agreement for the 719,000-square-foot Moffett Green tech campus in Sunnyvale, a move expected to bring thousands of Walmart.com employees to the Silicon Valley location. This deal offers a substantial boost to the region's struggling office market, which has seen increased vacancies and reduced demand for office space following widespread tech layoffs and the shift to remote work. The campus was previously leased by Meta Platforms and is currently owned by CommonWealth Partners, highlighting the dynamic changes in Bay Area commercial real estate.

title: US judge trims AI copyright lawsuit against Meta
summary: A U.S. federal judge has partially dismissed a copyright lawsuit filed by comedian Sarah Silverman and other authors against Meta Platforms, concerning its AI system Llama. While the judge will dismiss allegations that Llama's text output infringes copyrights, he is allowing the authors to amend most of their claims. The core accusation that Meta infringed copyrights by using authors' books to train Llama remains unchallenged by Meta in this phase.

title: Autodesk Workshop XR Delivers Immersive Design Review Experience for the AEC Industry
summary: Autodesk, Inc. unveiled Autodesk Workshop XR, an immersive design review workspace connected to Autodesk Construction Cloud, aimed at the architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) industry. This new tool enables real-time collaboration on 3D models from anywhere, facilitating better decision-making and preventing costly errors. Workshop XR will be available on Meta Quest headsets, enhancing project reviews and sustainability through improved design processes.

title: FAANG Gives Way to the ‘Magnificent Seven’
summary: The "FAANG" stock group has evolved into the "Magnificent Seven," with Netflix replaced by Nvidia, and Microsoft and Tesla added to the mix of top U.S. market names. ETF issuers are beginning to launch products specifically targeting these seven stocks, while existing ETFs like the Vanguard Mega Cap Growth ETF (MGK) already hold significant concentrations of them. Investors have various options, from highly concentrated ETFs to diversified funds, to gain exposure to these influential companies.

title: Tepper’s Appaloosa dumps Apple stake and loads up on Amazon’s stock
summary: David Tepper’s Appaloosa hedge fund significantly increased its stake in Amazon.com Inc. and completely divested its position in Apple Inc. during the last quarter. The fund also raised its holdings in Google parent Alphabet Inc. and Facebook parent Meta Platforms Inc., as detailed in a recent SEC filing. This move indicates a shift in Tepper's portfolio allocation towards these tech giants.

title: ‘I was addicted to social media - now I'm suing Big Tech’
summary: Taylor Little, a 21-year-old, is among hundreds of families suing major tech companies like Meta, TikTok, Google, and Snap Inc. for allegedly designing harmful and addictive social media products for children. Taylor details their journey through social media addiction, which led to suicide attempts and depression after being exposed to graphic content and body image issues at a young age. A recent federal court ruling boosted the families' case by rejecting the tech companies' attempts to use freedom of speech and Section 230 as blanket protections.

title: AI ETFs see huge performance dispersion in 2023
summary: Artificial Intelligence ETFs have shown significant performance dispersion in 2023, with top strategies more than doubling the returns of the lowest performers. This disparity is largely attributed to the heavy weighting of certain ETFs towards US equities and the "magnificent seven" tech stocks. While these mega-cap companies have driven much of the AI theme's returns, there is still potential for pure-play AI companies to join the rally.

title: Cognizant Technology Solutions to lay off employees tied to Apple in Austin
summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions announced layoffs impacting employees linked to Apple in Austin. The contractors affected will receive pay while exploring other positions within Cognizant. This move adds to a series of technology sector layoffs in Austin, though Central Texas is noted to be somewhat sheltered from broader industry cuts.

title: AI Alliance Launches as an International Community of Leading Technology Developers, Researchers, and Adopters Collaborating Together to Advance Open, Safe, Responsible AI
summary: The AI Alliance has launched as an international community of over 50 founding members, including IBM and Meta, to advance open, safe, and responsible AI. The alliance aims to foster open innovation and science in AI by bringing together diverse organizations to accelerate responsible AI development, establish benchmarks, and build a vibrant AI ecosystem. Its main goal is to ensure that AI advancements are harnessed in ways that prioritize safety, diversity, economic opportunity, and benefits for all, addressing societal challenges and promoting ethical AI practices.

title: Meta Platforms, IBM create industrywide AI alliance to share technology
summary: Meta Platforms and IBM are leading an industry-wide AI Alliance of over 40 companies and organizations to promote open-source artificial intelligence development. The coalition aims to share technology, reduce risks, and focus on responsible AI development, including safety and security tools. This initiative seeks to increase open-source AI models and foster collaboration with academic researchers.

title: AMD releases new chips to power faster AI training
summary: AMD announced the availability of its new MI300 chips, including the Instinct MI300X accelerator and Instinct M1300A APU, designed to power demanding AI training and large language models. The company also unveiled the Ryzen 8040 mobile processors, which will bring enhanced AI capabilities to laptops in 2024. These releases position AMD to compete more directly with Nvidia in the booming AI chip market.

title: CastleArk Enters ETF Market With Large Growth ETF CARK
summary: CastleArk has launched its first exchange-traded fund, the CastleArk Large Growth ETF (CARK), an actively managed ETF focused on large-cap growth companies. The fund targets 25-30 leading growth companies, identifying competitive strength and seeking to optimize risk through quantitative inputs. CastleArk aims to provide an actively managed alternative to index-heavy portfolios, especially given the concentration of "Magnificent Seven" stocks in the large-cap growth sector.

title: AMD debuts new chip with eye to rival Nvidia product
summary: AMD has unveiled its new MI300 accelerator chips, aiming to compete with Nvidia in the burgeoning artificial intelligence (AI) chip market. CEO Lisa Su projected the AI chip industry could exceed $400 billion in the next four years, and mentioned Microsoft, Oracle, and Meta Platforms as customers. The company claims its new chip outperforms Nvidia's H100 in AI inference and matches it in training capabilities.

title: Salesforce’s Benioff Cashing Out at Rate of $3 Million a Day
summary: Marc Benioff, co-founder of Salesforce, has been consistently selling 15,000 shares of the company's stock, worth about $3 million, almost daily since July. This strategy has resulted in unloading over $475 million worth of shares through approximately 170 transactions this year, a practice he employs to fund charitable gifts. Experts view his systematic selling approach as a "safest" way for CEOs to diversify their holdings without significantly impacting stock prices or violating insider-trading rules.

title: What are the most visited social media platforms among Gen Z?
summary: This article analyzes the most visited and fastest-growing social media platforms among Gen Z (18-24 year olds) based on mobile app usage. It highlights YouTube as having the highest reach, while BeReal and LinkedIn are the fastest growing platforms for this demographic. The analysis also compares Gen Z's social media usage to the general population and other age groups, noting their higher engagement on most platforms except for Facebook and LinkedIn in terms of reach.

title: Breaking Through the Echo Chamber: The Dynamic Power of Out of Home + Social Media
summary: This article explores how the integration of social media and out-of-home (OOH) advertising is a game-changer for brands seeking to engage new digital audiences, particularly Gen Z. It highlights TikTok's embrace of OOH, the enhanced social media engagement driven by #sOOH, and the tangible presence OOH offers for both emerging and legacy brands. The synergy helps brands break free from digital echo chambers, foster meaningful connections, and build trust among consumers.

title: Possible IPO Stars for 2024: Private Equity Firms
summary: Despite a challenging period, private equity firms are looking towards a wave of IPOs in 2024, driven by strong returns from existing public PE companies. Firms like General Atlantic, CVC Capital Partners, Ardian, and L Catterton are reportedly considering going public, seeking capital and significant paydays for their principals. However, this move introduces greater regulatory oversight and transparency requirements, making timing critical.

title: Meta: Sell it? Buy it?
summary: Meta's stock price has experienced significant volatility in recent years, oscillating from a high of $384 in August 2021 to below $100 in October 2022, before rebounding to around $340. This article explores the company's journey from being considered a "sell" to a "buy," focusing on challenges such as Apple's privacy policy changes and competition from TikTok, and how Meta has responded by investing heavily in AI and re-evaluating its metaverse strategy. The future success of Meta is tied to its AI applications across its businesses, especially in advertising and new ventures.

title: 6 Reasons to Avoid JPMorgan (JPM) Immediately
summary: This article outlines six reasons why investors should consider avoiding JPMorgan (JPM) stock immediately. Key concerns include its expensive valuation relative to expected revenue drops, a shrinking dividend yield compared to competitors, potential economic slowdowns in an election year, the eventual departure of CEO Jamie Dimon, Dimon's recent stock sale, and the bank's focus on high-net-worth investors rather than comprehensive individual investor services. The author suggests waiting for the stock to trade lower before buying.

title: US surveillance giant’s network of operations has subsidiaries in India
summary: US surveillance giant Verint Systems has a network of operations including four subsidiaries in India, as revealed by the Cyprus Confidential investigation. One of Verint's spin-off companies, Cognyte, has faced controversy for allegedly selling spyware to "repressive regimes" and was named by Meta as a "cyber mercenary" for indiscriminately targeting journalists and dissidents. The Indian subsidiaries show significant revenue from foreign group companies and have had run-ins with the Income Tax Department.

title: GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL) Dividend Yield 2026 & History $FBL
summary: The GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL) offers an annual dividend of $0.67 per share, resulting in a 2.09% dividend yield. The ETF pays dividends monthly, with its most recent monthly payment of $0.6742 per share made on December 31, 2025, following an ex-dividend date of December 29, 2025. This article provides historical dividend data, payment schedules, and compares FBL's dividend performance against market averages.

title: Magazine roundup: Top 100 data centre companies
summary: Data Centre Magazine reviews its Top 100 data centre companies for 2023, highlighting their commitment to advancing technology and sustainability. The article rounds up the top 10 companies, including industry giants like AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform, detailing their founding, CEOs, and key contributions to the data centre sector. It showcases their innovative solutions, global reach, and efforts toward environmental responsibility.

title: Apple's stock underperformed top tech peers in 2023 due to longest revenue slide in 22 years
summary: Apple's stock underperformed its mega-cap tech peers in 2023, experiencing its longest revenue slide in 22 years with four consecutive quarters of declining sales. This decline was attributed to a combination of a weak economic environment for smartphones and PCs, and company-specific issues such as the lack of new iPad models and an Apple Watch sales ban. Despite a 49% stock rally for the year, Apple lagged behind competitors like Nvidia, Meta, Tesla, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft.

title: Miami doesn’t need to be the Silicon Valley of the South

The company reported strong Q3 2023 results, including a GAAP net income of $7 million and adjusted EBITDA of $185 million, with revenue growth driven significantly by Europe and increasing ARPU in the U.S. and Canada. Analysts are largely bullish on Pinterest, with several firms upgrading their ratings and issuing positive price targets.

title: Fundsmith Underperforms, Smith Takes Aim at AI Hype

In his annual letter, Smith questioned the market's early coronation of AI winners like Nvidia, drawing parallels to past tech leaders that faded. While the fund sold stakes in Estée Lauder, Adobe, and Amazon, it made new purchases in Procter & Gamble, Marriott, and Fortinet, maintaining its long-term investment strategy.

summary: Wells Fargo's virtual assistant app, Fargo, powered by Google's AI, has handled 20 million interactions since its March launch and is projected to reach 100 million annually. Wells Fargo emphasizes a multi-LLM, multi-cloud strategy on its "Tachyon" platform, and views multimodal LLMs and "agentic" AI as future critical developments, while navigating regulatory challenges.

title: Meta to default teen Facebook, Instagram users to most restrictive settings
summary: Meta Platforms announced it will set the default content settings for teenage users on Facebook and Instagram to the most restrictive to enhance their safety. This decision follows a lawsuit by 42 attorneys general alleging harm to teenage users by social media platforms. The update will hide more sensitive content, such as discussions of self-harm and eating disorders, and apply to both new and existing teen users.

title: CuriosityStream class action
summary: A federal court in Maryland denied CuriosityStream's motion for reconsideration in a class action lawsuit. Subscribers claim the video subscription service unlawfully disclosed their personally identifiable information to Facebook. The court found that CuriosityStream failed to prove that online users assented to their terms of service.

Their success is attributed to shifting focus from fixed-price construction contracts to per-hour consulting fees, diversifying through acquisitions, expanding into areas like water infrastructure, and disciplined growth strategies focused on profitability rather than just revenue. Both companies prioritize strategic acquisitions, debt management, and maximizing workforce utilization, making engineering an attractive investment sector.

This surge aligns with strong demand reported by TSMC, their manufacturer, and Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's plans for large GPU purchases.

summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg stated that by the end of 2024, Meta's computing infrastructure will include 350,000 H100 graphics cards from Nvidia, suggesting billions of dollars in expenditure given the chips' high cost. This massive investment is driven by Meta's pursuit of artificial general intelligence (AGI) and expanding AI capabilities. Zuckerberg also mentioned plans to open-source its general intelligence development and streamline research efforts between its FAIR and GenAI teams.

CEO Jamie Iannone informed employees that the company will also reduce its alternate workforce contracts and highlighted the need for better organization and nimbleness.

The nearly 700,000-square-foot facility at River Ridge Commerce Center will be Meta's 18th in the U.S. and is expected to begin construction this month and be operational by 2026. The project received incentives including a 35-year sales tax exemption from the Indiana Economic Development Corporation.

title: Meta chooses new operation location in Southern Indiana
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. announced plans to build an $800 million data center campus in Jeffersonville, Indiana, which will be its 18th in the United States. The nearly 700,000-square-foot facility at River Ridge Commerce Center is expected to create approximately 100 operational jobs and will be powered by 100% renewable energy. Indiana and local authorities have offered performance-based economic incentives, including a 35-year term data center sales tax exemption, to support the project.

This move is significant given Google's market share with Android and YouTube, aiming to combat misinformation by making AI-generated content more transparent. While the C2PA aims for widespread adoption to make unlabeled content stand out, critics question its tamper-proof nature, suggesting that bad actors could strip or swap metadata, and it may not fully address the issue of malicious misinformation.

summary: A class action lawsuit alleges that FuboTV illegally shares its users' personally identifiable information and video viewing data with third parties like Facebook and Google without their consent. The lawsuit, filed by Catherine Beasley, claims FuboTV violates the Video Privacy Protection Act by failing to obtain informed written consent before disclosing this sensitive data. Beasley is seeking damages and a jury trial for herself and a nationwide class of affected consumers.

The court also decertified a separate class seeking injunctive relief but allowed the damages class action to proceed, which could potentially result in over $7 billion in damages.

These developments suggest a global movement towards stricter regulation of big tech, challenging Apple's long-held justifications of privacy and security for its closed ecosystem.
