title: TikTok, Snapchat respond as Ontario school boards sue social media giants
summary: Four major Ontario school boards are suing social media giants Meta Platforms (Facebook, Instagram), Snap Inc. (Snapchat), and ByteDance Ltd. (TikTok) for $4.5 billion, alleging their platforms harm students' mental health and disrupt education. The school boards claim these apps are designed to be addictive, promote cyberbullying and misinformation, and are causing an "attention, learning, and mental health crisis." TikTok and Snapchat have issued responses, highlighting their safety features, while Ontario Premier Doug Ford has disagreed with the lawsuit, calling it "nonsense."

title: Esports Investing: Opportunities & Strategies
summary: Esports is a rapidly growing industry with significant investment opportunities despite its relative newness. Investors can gain exposure through companies involved in game development, streaming platforms, hardware/software manufacturing, or through dedicated esports ETFs. While growth prospects are strong, investors should be prepared for volatility and perform thorough due diligence.

title: Meta Joins Hand With Mukesh Ambani’s Reliance To Set Up Firm’s First Data Center In India
summary: Meta Platforms is partnering with Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries Limited to establish its first data center in India, which will be hosted on RIL’s Chennai campus. This move aims to localize data processing for Meta's platforms, including Facebook, Instagram, and Whatsapp, which have a vast user base in India, currently having their data managed from Singapore. The collaboration strengthens the existing relationship between the two companies, as Meta previously invested in Reliance's Jio Platforms.

title: The top performing funds that beat the S&P 500 in Q1
summary: This article highlights the top-performing US equity funds in Q1 2024 that outperformed the S&P 500 index. Funds from GQG, Artemis, and Alger are featured, with GQG Partners U.S. Equity leading the pack. The piece details the investment strategies of these funds and specific holdings that contributed to their success.

title: FBY ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:FBY
summary: This article provides an analysis of the YieldMax META Option Income Strategy ETF (FBY), detailing its objective to generate monthly income and provide exposure to Meta Platforms stock. It outlines the ETF's synthetic covered call strategy, key financial metrics like AUM and dividend yield, and its current holdings primarily in US Treasury Bills. The analysis also covers dividend history, fund flows, and performance metrics, noting recent decreases in price and NAV returns.

title: VOX: How Meta, Alphabet, And The Streaming Industry Could Outshine The Market
summary: The article suggests that the Vanguard Communication Services ETF (VOX) warrants a buy rating due to the strong potential of its top holdings, Meta Platforms and Alphabet, to outperform the market. This outperformance is expected to be driven by technological advancements from these companies and anticipated growth in streaming services. Despite historical underperformance of the communication services sector, these factors are likely to reverse the trend, making VOX an attractive investment due to its diversification, low expense ratio, and favorable price-to-earnings ratio compared to peers.

title: Apple Signs Deal For AI Training Data from Image Service Shutterstock
summary: Apple has signed a deal worth between $25 million and $50 million with Shutterstock to access its vast archive of images, video, and music for training AI systems. This move indicates Apple's strategic intent to develop its own AI models, rather than relying solely on existing solutions. The deal highlights Apple's characteristic approach to adhere to rules and protect user privacy, contrasting with some competitors' controversial methods of data acquisition.

title: Adobe Is Buying Videos for $3 Per Minute to Build AI Model
summary: Adobe (ADBE) is actively procuring videos to develop its artificial intelligence text-to-video generator, aiming to compete with technologies like OpenAI's Sora. The company is offering artists $120 to submit videos depicting everyday actions and emotions for AI training purposes. This initiative highlights Adobe's strategy to enhance its AI capabilities in the face of competitive pressure.

title: FBL ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:FBL
summary: This article provides an analysis of the GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL), detailing its investment strategy, performance metrics, and key characteristics. FBL aims to deliver twice the daily price return of META stock, but warns about increased volatility and the need for frequent rebalancing for long-term holders. The analysis also covers dividend history, assets under management (AUM), fund flows, expense ratio, and classification of the ETF.

title: Creator-Platform Company Spotter Hires Adobe and Microsoft Alum Ivo Manolov as CTO
summary: Spotter, a company providing capital and software to online creators, has appointed Ivo Manolov, an alum of Adobe Systems and Microsoft, as its new Chief Technology Officer. Manolov will lead the development of AI-powered software for YouTubers. This strategic hire underscores Spotter's commitment to enhancing its technology and AI initiatives for creators.

title: Terry Smith – Top 10 Holdings – Latest 13F
summary: This article analyzes Terry Smith's top 10 holdings based on his latest 13F-HR filing as of December 31, 2023. His portfolio has a market value of over $23.8 billion, with 65.29% concentrated in his top ten positions. Microsoft, Meta Platforms, and Stryker Corp are among his largest holdings.

title: 1 Stock-Split ETF That Could Turn $500 Per Month Into $1 Million, With Help From Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple
summary: The iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (IGM) recently undertook a 6-for-1 stock split, making its shares more accessible to a wider range of investors. This ETF, which has outperformed both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100, holds top tech stocks like Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Meta Platforms, and Alphabet, benefiting significantly from the burgeoning AI industry. The article suggests that a consistent monthly investment of $500 into IGM has the potential to grow substantially, potentially reaching $1 million or more over the long term, especially if the current high growth rates driven by AI continue.

title: Here's What's Happening in Markets: April 19
summary: Markets are showing uncertainty as investors react to a weaker-than-expected Q2 forecast from Netflix, causing tech stocks to dip. Meanwhile, cryptocurrency ETFs are gaining momentum as the highly anticipated Bitcoin halving approaches, with many watching to see if this event will trigger a familiar rally in Bitcoin prices given the current market dynamics.

title: The pressure’s on for big tech firms, says BofA
summary: Bank of America strategists indicate high pressure on big tech firms, particularly the Magnificent Seven, to deliver on AI promises as their earnings growth is set to decelerate. While the group is still expected to post significant growth, the contribution of AI to profits and AI capital expenditure outlook will be key focuses for investors. This comes amidst a recent market downturn for tech stocks, with predictions of a rotation to value-oriented stocks later in the year.

title: Microsoft CEO to meet with Samsung, SK Hynix, LG, SK Telecom chiefs
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella and founder Bill Gates will meet with leaders of Samsung, SK Hynix, LG, and SK Telecom at the MS CEO Summit 2024 in May to discuss strengthening AI partnerships. The meetings aim to explore joint development of AI chips, cooperation in AI technology for consumer electronics, and business tie-ups in 5G telecommunications and cloud sectors. This gathering signifies an increasing trend of Big Tech firms seeking AI business collaborations to gain a competitive edge.

title: Meta earnings: Stock decline could wipe out about $200 billion in market cap
summary: Meta Platforms reported first-quarter earnings that, despite beating expectations, led to a significant stock decline due to a conservative revenue outlook and increased capital expenditure forecasts for AI infrastructure. The after-hours drop could result in a market cap loss of approximately $200 billion, marking one of the steepest single-day declines in U.S. company history. Analysts noted Meta's strong underlying fundamentals and revenue growth, but investors were concerned by the higher spending and the impact on profitability.

title: Meta Reports First Quarter 2024 Results
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. reported strong financial results for Q1 2024, with revenue increasing by 27% year-over-year to $36.46 billion and net income more than doubling to $12.37 billion. The company attributes this success to healthy growth across its apps and progress in building the metaverse, alongside significant investments in AI. Meta also updated its full-year 2024 expense and capital expenditure outlooks, anticipating increased investments in infrastructure to support its AI roadmap.

title: Why Is GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL) Stock Down 31% Today?
summary: The GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL) stock is down 31% today following Meta Platforms' (META) Q1 earnings report. Despite strong Q1 results, Meta's Q2 revenue outlook of $36.5 billion to $39 billion disappointed investors, as the midpoint fell below Wall Street estimates. FBL is designed to track 2x the daily percentage change of META stock, directly reflecting the downward movement caused by the earnings report.

title: 杰富瑞：下调Meta目标价至540美元
summary: Jefferies has reduced its price target for Meta Platforms from $585 to $540. This adjustment was announced on April 25, 2024.

title: Sundar Pichai confirms Alphabet Inc. is going to issue first-ever dividend, $70 billion buyback - Hindustan Times
summary: Alphabet Inc., Google's parent company, announced its first-ever cash dividend of 20 cents per share and a new $70 billion stock repurchase program following strong Q1 2024 earnings. CEO Sundar Pichai highlighted robust performance from Search, YouTube, and Cloud, attributing success to the "Gemini era" and AI leadership. This move, similar to Meta's recent dividend, signals tech industry maturity and has been positively received by investors.

title: After Earnings, Is Meta Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assessed Meta Platforms' stock following its Q1 earnings report, maintaining a "Wide" economic moat rating but deeming the stock overvalued with a 2-star rating and a $400 fair value estimate. The article highlights Meta's strong core advertising business, driven by AI investments, but also points out the high uncertainty due to speculative AI initiatives and competition. Investors are advised to be comfortable with Meta's aggressive investment in new technologies, as regulatory risks and dependence on online advertising remain significant concerns.

title: Alphabet's First Dividend: What You Need to Know
summary: Alphabet has announced its first-ever quarterly dividend of $0.20 per share, payable on June 17 to shareholders of record as of June 10. While the initial dividend yield will be modest at approximately 0.5%, the company also authorized $70 billion in share repurchases. This move makes Alphabet one of the eight largest US companies by market capitalization to pay a quarterly dividend, alongside a significant share repurchase program.

title: Alphabet's First Dividend: What You Need to Know
summary: Alphabet announced its first-ever quarterly dividend of US$0.20 per share, following a similar move by Meta Platforms. This initial dividend will result in a modest yield of approximately 0.5% for Alphabet shares, significantly less than its historical buyback yield. While the new dividend rate represents only 12% of morningstar equity analyst's earnings per share forecast, leaving substantial room for future increases, share repurchases are expected to outweigh dividends in the near term.

title: Calamos launches first ETF from planned suite seeking 100% downside protection
summary: Calamos has launched its first exchange-traded fund (ETF) from a planned suite that aims to offer 100% downside protection. This new ETF is part of a broader trend towards innovative investment products in the market. The article also touches upon several other financial news items, including SEC work delays, endowment performance, private equity AUM, and pension fund voting.

title: Pinterest Users Should Be Marketers’ Secret Weapon
summary: Frequent Pinterest users exhibit psychographic and behavioral traits similar to social media influencers, making them a powerful but often overlooked marketing target. These users are typically younger, wealthier, urban, and influential within their real-life communities by actively seeking and sharing new trends and products. As other platforms face scrutiny, Pinterest offers marketers a valuable channel to reach taste-making consumers who are willing to spend on quality and experiences.

title: Can Crowding Scores Quantify US Stocks’ Fragility?
summary: The U.S. equity market has reached an unprecedented level of concentration, largely driven by mega-cap tech stocks, sparking concerns about market fragility and crowding risk. While crowded stocks globally have historically underperformed uncrowded ones, the U.S. market has recently bucked this trend, with crowded stocks outperforming since mid-2022. The MSCI Security Crowding Model offers insights into identifying areas of the market most susceptible to price correction if sentiment deteriorates.

title: Tech Stock Dividends Are Changing the Face of Dividend Growth Investing
summary: The article discusses how major tech companies like Alphabet, Meta, and Salesforce are starting to pay dividends, shifting the landscape of dividend growth investing. This trend is increasing the representation of tech stocks in dividend growth strategies, potentially improving market correlation but also lowering overall portfolio diversification. While tech dividends are generally low-yielding, their potential for future growth alongside earnings is attractive to investors focused on long-term dividend appreciation.

title: Corporations buy into sustainable fuel certificates to address air travel emissions
summary: Corporations are increasingly purchasing sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) certificates to offset their Scope 3 emissions from business travel, aiming to reduce their carbon footprint and boost demand for lower-carbon fuels. Boston Consulting Group, Netflix, Meta, Microsoft, and others have invested significantly in these certificates, which critics warn could lead to greenwashing. Experts advise companies to understand the early market risks, work with established players, and not use SAF certificates as an excuse for more flights.

title: The Digital Markets Act Roundup: March 2024
summary: The Digital Markets Act (DMA) enforcement has continued actively, particularly with non-compliance investigations against major companies like Alphabet, Apple, and Meta. Key developments include discussions on competition law, AI integration in core platform services, and the role of national authorities in DMA enforcement. Additionally, Apple faced a significant fine for anticompetitive app store rules, small browsers have seen market share growth due to DMA-mandated choice screens, and Apple's iPadOS was designated as a gatekeeper under the DMA.

title: Alternatives to LinkedIn Ads: Best Advertising Platforms
summary: This article explores alternatives to LinkedIn Ads for marketers, highlighting its pros (precise B2B targeting) and cons (high cost, lower engagement time). It details five alternative platforms: Google Ads, Microsoft Advertising, X (Twitter), Quora, and Taboola. The piece emphasizes diversifying advertising strategies across various platforms to maximize ROI and reach different audience segments.

title: Navigating the Cookieless Era Part 5: What is Cohort Marketing? Google Privacy Sandbox and Seller-Defined Audiences
summary: This article, part of the "Navigating the Cookieless Era" series, explains cohort marketing as an alternative to traditional user tracking. It delves into key cohort-based solutions: Google Privacy Sandbox, specifically the Topics API and Protected Audience API, and Seller-Defined Audiences (SDA). The article outlines how these technologies aim to balance user privacy with effective targeting in a cookieless digital advertising landscape.

title: Google Glass Making A Comeback? New Prototype AR Glasses Make A Cameo Appearance At I/O 2024
summary: Google revealed a prototype of new augmented reality (AR) glasses at its I/O 2024 conference, sparking speculation about a revival of the Google Glass project. The new prototype was shown in a demo video for Project Astra, worn by an employee, and suggests Google is actively pursuing its AR ambitions amidst a growing AR market. This comes after Apple's Vision Pro launch and the success of Meta's smart glasses.

title: Nvidia shares jump 4% as the AI boom fuels another blockbuster quarter and better-than-expected forecast
summary: Nvidia reported another strong quarter, exceeding revenue and earnings expectations, driven by the ongoing AI boom. The company forecasted second-quarter revenue of $28 billion, surpassing analyst predictions, and announced a 10-for-1 stock split and a 150% dividend increase. CEO Jensen Huang highlighted the diversification of AI adoption beyond hyperscalers into various industries and sovereign AI initiatives.

title: Autodesk (ADSK) Acquires Wonder Dynamics for 3D Animation & VFX
summary: Autodesk (ADSK) has acquired Wonder Dynamics, creators of the AI-powered 3D animation solution Wonder Studio, to enhance its offerings in 3D animation and VFX. This acquisition will enable easier integration of 3D animated characters into projects and aligns with Autodesk's strategy to incorporate AI ethically. The move pits Autodesk against major players like Microsoft, Adobe, and Meta Platforms in the rapidly growing generative AI market.

title: Live Nation Entertainment Stock Tumbles as DOJ Announces Antitrust Suit
summary: The Department of Justice filed an antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation Entertainment, alleging monopolistic practices that harm fans, artists, and venues. The DOJ seeks to break up the company, causing Live Nation's stock to tumble 8% following the announcement. Live Nation denies the claims, arguing its profitability is low and its commission rates are competitive.

title: Anant Ambani, Radhika Merchants guests to get personalised gift at wedding
summary: Guests attending the wedding of Anant Ambani and Radhika Merchant are reportedly set to receive exclusive, personalized gifts, specifically silver filigree jewellery crafted by artisans in Karimnagar, Telangana. This detail emerged as preparations for the July wedding are in full swing, following a lavish pre-wedding celebration hosted by Mukesh Ambani in March. The couple is also planning a pre-wedding celebration on a cruise to Italy.

title: AWS and SAP Unlock New Innovation with Generative AI
summary: AWS and SAP have announced an expanded strategic collaboration to enhance cloud ERP experiences and integrate generative AI into enterprise applications. This partnership will make it easier for customers to adopt RISE with SAP on AWS, improve SAP workload performance, and leverage generative AI models from Amazon Bedrock within SAP AI Core. Additionally, SAP plans to use AWS chips like Graviton, Trainium, and Inferentia for future SAP Business AI offerings and has boosted performance for RISE with SAP on AWS.

title: If You Can Only Buy One Metaverse Stock in May, It Better Be One of These 3 Names
summary: Despite initial hype and subsequent correction, the metaverse sector is projected to be valued at up to $13 trillion by 2030, presenting long-term investment opportunities. Three top metaverse stocks to consider are Meta Platforms (META) for its extensive reach and AI investments, Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) for diversified exposure to 38 holdings, and Unity Software (U) which is favored by Cathie Wood for its digital twin platform and positive cash flow. These selections offer potential for significant returns for patient investors as the metaverse matures.

title: Eddie Bauer Smart Eyewear collection includes voice access to ChatGPT
summary: Innovative Eyewear has launched an Eddie Bauer Smart Eyewear collection, which features voice access to ChatGPT and Bluetooth microphones and speakers. The collection introduces the first-ever rimless smart eyewear design and will be available in department stores, sports retailers, and optical shops. This initiative follows similar smart eyewear collaborations by Authentic Brands Group with its Nautica and Reebok brands, highlighting a trend of merging fashion with wearable technology.

title: The AI Chatbot Can Help You Book That Mediterranean Cruise
summary: Online travel companies like Booking Holdings and Expedia are rapidly integrating AI-powered tools, such as chatbots and trip planners, to enhance vacation planning for customers. These innovations aim to offer hyper-personalization, improve efficiency, and potentially reduce costs, with the goal of capturing customers early in their travel planning process. Tech giants like Google and Meta are also developing their own AI chatbots, signaling a significant shift in how trips will be organized and booked.

title: Facebook India Battles a Sea of Spam: Over 6.8 Million Content Removed In Just One Month
summary: Meta's platforms, Facebook and Instagram, removed over 11.7 million pieces of harmful content in India during April 2024, with spam accounting for the largest portion at 6.8 million removals from Facebook. The company's monthly report outlines its content moderation efforts, highlighting its "Content Actioned" and "Proactive Rate" metrics, as well as user grievance reports regarding issues like fake profiles and abusive content. Given India's status as a major market, Meta continues to prioritize combating harmful content to ensure safer user experiences.

title: 大模型，正在排队寻求卖身
summary: The article discusses the current trend of AI unicorns, such as Stability AI and Adept, seeking mergers or acquisitions due to financial difficulties and intense market competition. It highlights the high operational costs, fierce rivalry with tech giants, and unclear commercialization paths as key challenges faced by these startups. The piece suggests that the AI investment landscape is shifting towards consolidation, with funding concentrating on top-tier companies and application-level innovations, leading to a period where many AI startups might be acquired.

title: Uvalde shooting victims’ families file lawsuits against Meta, video game cos., others
summary: Families of the 17 children killed in the 2022 Uvalde mass shooting have filed lawsuits against Daniel Defense, Meta, and Activision. They allege that Meta and Activision contributed to the shooting by allowing the shooter to be exposed to firearm advertisements via Instagram and through his engagement with Activision's Call of Duty game. The lawsuits claim these companies collaborated with the firearms industry to target minors, leading to devastating consequences.

title: 估值1000亿，辍学90后华裔天才，刚刚融了72亿
summary: 人工智能公司Scale AI近日宣布获得10亿美元（约72亿人民币）F轮融资，估值达到138亿美元（约999亿人民币），为IPO铺平道路。公司由95后华裔天才Alexander Wang于2016年创立，专注于数据标注和AI模型训练，其主要任务是建立AI数据工厂。Scale AI的业务在AIGC时代快速增长，客户包括OpenAI、英伟达、Meta等，2023年年度经常性收入增长三倍至7.5亿美元。

title: Direxion Completes Mag 7 Collection With Leveraged & Inverse Meta ETFs
summary: Direxion has expanded its leveraged and inverse ETF offerings with the introduction of the Direxion Daily META Bull 2X Shares (METU) and Direxion Daily META Bear 1X Shares (METD), completing its "Magnificent Seven" collection. These new funds allow investors to gain leveraged or inverse exposure to Meta Platforms Inc. (META) daily performance, catering to traders looking to capitalize on the stock's short-term fluctuations. Direxion is now the sole provider offering single-stock ETF pairs for all Magnificent Seven companies.

title: Meta Withheld Information on Instagram, WhatsApp Deals, FTC Says
summary: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) accused Meta Platforms Inc. of withholding information during its initial reviews of the Instagram and WhatsApp acquisitions. This accusation was made during a court filing as part of the FTC's ongoing lawsuit to break up the social networking giant. Both Instagram (acquired in 2012) and WhatsApp (acquired in 2014) were scrutinized by the FTC when their deals were being finalized.

title: 'Cradle to Grave': Children's Clothing Company Carter's Sued in Digital Privacy Class Action
summary: Children's clothing retailer Carter's is facing a new digital privacy class action lawsuit. The suit alleges that Carter's violated California's "Trap and Trace Law" (California Penal Code § 638.51) by using TikTok software on its website, which purportedly deanonymizes consumer data without consent. This case is part of a broader trend of digital privacy class actions targeting companies' use of third-party software.

title: Nvidia overtakes Apple as No. 2 most valuable company
summary: Nvidia's valuation has surpassed $3 trillion, making it the world's second most valuable company, overtaking Apple. The AI chipmaker's stock surge is attributed to high demand for its processors among tech giants building AI capabilities. This shift highlights Nvidia's dominance in the current AI landscape, while Apple faces challenges with iPhone demand and AI integration.

title: FBL: A Leveraged Bet On Meta Platforms’ Strong Growth (NASDAQ:FBL)
summary: The article discusses the GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL), highlighting its potential for high returns but also its significant risks due to its leveraged daily reset characteristic. Investors are advised to use momentum indicators and exercise caution, considering the associated costs and risks. Despite these risks, the fundamental analysis of Meta Platforms (META) itself shows positive performance.

title: 2 Popular Stocks That Top Managers Are Selling
summary: Top-rated fund managers are significantly reducing their holdings in two popular stocks: Microsoft (MSFT) and Meta Platforms (META). While Microsoft remains widely held and its fair value estimate increased, it is currently considered fairly valued, not a bargain. Meta Platforms, after a volatile period, is now viewed as overvalued by Morningstar analysts despite experiencing a major rebound in ad spending and stock performance.

title: Immersion Corp completes major BNED asset acquisition
summary: Immersion Corporation (NASDAQ:IMMR) has completed the acquisition of assets from Barnes & Noble Education, Inc. (NYSE:BNED) through a rights offering and a PIPE transaction, establishing a significant stake in BNED. Immersion's subsidiary, Toro 18 Holdings, LLC, acquired 200,670,135 shares of BNED Common Stock and invested $90 million in new BNED shares. This strategic move diversifies Immersion into the education sector, with several Immersion board members also joining BNED's board.

title: AI, Economy Affect 2024 Tech Workforce Trends, Survey Shows
summary: A TechTarget survey reveals that AI and economic factors are significantly shaping the 2024 tech workforce. AI skills are becoming critical, with AI/ML/data science climbing to the second-highest hiring priority, while economic uncertainties are impacting non-salary compensation despite an overall increase in average IT pay. Organizations are increasingly using AI to address skills gaps and improve efficiency, reshaping existing IT roles rather than necessarily reducing staff.

title: Nvidia eclipses Microsoft as world's most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has surpassed Microsoft to become the world's most valuable company, with its market capitalization reaching $3.341 trillion. This surge is driven by high demand for its processors essential for artificial intelligence technology. Nvidia's stock has nearly tripled this year, reflecting the market's strong optimism about its role in the booming AI sector.

title: Nvidia becomes world’s most valuable company, dethroning Microsoft
summary: Nvidia has dethroned Microsoft to become the world's most valuable company, with its market capitalization reaching $3.335 trillion. The chipmaker's stock price surged by 3.5 percent to $135.58, driven by the strong demand for its GPUs essential for artificial intelligence development. This milestone follows Nvidia's consistent rise, fueled by its dominant market share in AI chips and strategic investments by CEO Jensen Huang.

title: Microsoft Strikes 2 Record-Breaking Carbon Credit Deals
summary: Microsoft has made two significant moves in the carbon credit market, signing a record-breaking deal with BTG Pactual Timberland Investment Group for 8 million carbon removal credits, and an additionally largest-ever individual purchase of 40,000 agricultural soil carbon credits from Indigo Ag. These agreements are part of Microsoft's ambitious commitment to become carbon-negative by 2030 and eliminate all historical emissions by 2050, demonstrating its strategy to invest in large-scale, high-quality carbon removal projects, especially in nature-based solutions. The company's efforts also include a collaboration with other tech giants and a focus on reducing its significant Scope 3 emissions.

title: Apple Intelligence won't launch in EU in 2024 due to antitrust regulation, company says
summary: Apple announced that its Apple Intelligence AI product and three other new features will not be launched in the European Union in 2024. This delay is attributed to "regulatory uncertainties" stemming from the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which Apple believes could compromise user privacy and data security due to interoperability requirements. Apple stated it would work with the EU to find a solution that allows the features to be rolled out without compromising safety.

title: Apple is first tech company accused of violating EU's new competition law
summary: The European Union has accused Apple of violating its new Digital Markets Act, making Apple the first tech company to face charges under the law. The investigation focuses on Apple's app store rules, which allegedly hinder developers from directing consumers to alternative purchasing methods. Apple has a chance to respond before a final decision in March 2025, potentially facing fines up to 20% of its global revenue for repeated infringements.

title: Anthony Denier, President of Webull - Building a $7Bn Disruptor, Expanding US Markets Access Globally, What Makes a Great Trader
summary: This article features an interview with Anthony Denier, Group President of Webull, a $7 billion global investment platform. Denier discusses Webull's rapid expansion, its strategy for providing international investors access to US markets, and his perspective on the qualities of a successful trader. He also elaborates on Webull's decision to go public via SPAC and the evolution of the trading industry due to technology and regulations.

title: Tractor Supply Co. Just Made a Bold Change to Drop DEI at the End of Pride Month
summary: Tractor Supply Co. announced it would be eliminating its DEI department at the end of Pride Month, a decision influenced by a conservative activist campaign. This move follows similar quiet cutbacks in DEI funding by other major companies, despite general public support for DEI initiatives, highlighting a correlation between DEI support and political affiliation. The article suggests that for companies with a conservative customer base, pulling back on DEI might be a smarter business move to avoid controversy.

title: The Scoop: How conservative activists targeted Tractor Supply Company on DE&I
summary: Conservative activist Robby Starbuck successfully targeted Tractor Supply Company, forcing them to eliminate DE&I initiatives and end sponsorships of Pride festivals, which then led to a counter-backlash. This incident highlights the importance for companies to understand their customer base and organizational values to prepare for similar campaigns, following examples like Target and Bud Light. The article also touches on Meta's EU regulatory issues and Nissin's S'mores flavored Cup Noodles.

title: Snowflake moving into larger office in Bellevue to support growth plans
summary: Cloud computing company Snowflake is expanding its presence in Bellevue, Washington, by moving into a larger 326,000 square-foot office in the Spring District in 2025. This move is to support the rapid growth of its Seattle-area engineering center, which currently employs over 650 people. The company aims to reflect its growth trajectory with a larger space, despite a recent increase in overall office availability in the Seattle-area market.

title: 3 Reasons to Buy BULZ, and 4 Reasons Not To
summary: This article examines the MicroSectors Solactive FANG Innovation 3x Leveraged ETN (BULZ), outlining three reasons to consider investing in it and four reasons to be cautious. The ETN offers a concentrated, equal-weighted, and leveraged investment in top tech stocks, but comes with high volatility, potential for the underlying premise to fail, high expense ratios, and a warning from the issuer against long-term holding.

title: Checks in the mail from settlement with Meta, Fairlife, HelloFresh, others
summary: Top Class Actions readers are reporting receiving settlement checks from six different class action lawsuits involving companies like Meta, Fairlife, and HelloFresh. These settlements address various claims including false advertising, privacy violations, telemarketing abuses, and price-fixing of cancer medications. Payments range from initial disbursements to second rounds, with amounts varying based on the specific settlement and individual claims.

title: Declare your AIndependence: block AI bots, scrapers and crawlers with a single click
summary: Cloudflare has launched a new "easy button" feature allowing all customers, including those on the free tier, to block AI bots, scrapers, and crawlers from their websites. This initiative responds to concerns from content creators about AI companies scraping content for model training without transparency or permission. The article details the prevalence of various AI bots like Bytespider, GPTBot, and ClaudeBot across Cloudflare's network and explains how Cloudflare's machine learning models effectively identify and block even evasive AI bots attempting to spoof user agents.

title: What broadening rally? AI stocks dominate US market again in Q2
summary: AI stocks, particularly a select group of major tech companies, continued to dominate the US market rally in Q2 2024, contributing almost all of the Morningstar US Market Index's gains. This concentration of returns in AI-related large-cap growth stocks, led by Nvidia and Apple, indicates that the anticipated broadening of the stock market rally has not yet occurred. Other sectors and smaller capitalization stocks either showed minimal gains or detracted from overall market performance, while some AI-adjacent companies like Intel and Salesforce experienced significant drops.

title: Driving surge in demand for power, data centers eye Kentucky
summary: Kentucky is emerging as a potential location for new data centers driven by the artificial intelligence boom, with state lawmakers offering tax incentives and utilities preparing for increased power demand. However, this development raises concerns among environmental advocates regarding the financial burden on consumers for new infrastructure and the potential reliance on fossil fuels, as Kentucky currently has minimal renewable energy sources. The article highlights the significant power needs of data centers and the considerations for balancing economic growth with environmental impact.

title: Peloton accused of providing customer chat data to train AI
summary: Peloton is facing a class-action lawsuit in California, accused of using customer chat data to train AI without user permission. The lawsuit, filed by Consumer Advocates, alleges Peloton violated the California Invasion of Privacy Act (CIPA) by sharing chat content with AI-powered marketing firm Drift. The core issue is whether Peloton properly informed users about the data sharing and obtained consent, with the court allowing the case to proceed despite Peloton's attempts to dismiss it.

title: Intuit Slashes 1,800 Jobs in AI Embrace
summary: Intuit announced the elimination of 1,800 jobs, representing 10% of its workforce, as it deepens its use of AI in services like TurboTax and QuickBooks. However, the company plans to hire an equal number of new employees, primarily in engineering, product, and customer-facing roles, to support its AI-augmented strategy. Intuit CEO Sasan Goodarzi stated that the job cuts were not for cost-cutting but for strategic reinvestment in critical AI-driven areas.

title: PLDT completes biggest data center
summary: PLDT Inc.'s data center arm, VITRO Inc., has completed its 11th and largest data center, VITRO Sta. Rosa, in Laguna. This $350-million facility covers five hectares and will double VITRO's capacity by adding 50 megawatts to its network. It is designed to cater to the growing demand for information storage from both public and private sectors and hyperscalers, with plans for further expansion to meet future cloud and AI technology requirements.

title: Richard Simonelli returns to CoStar amid C-suite changes
summary: CoStar Group has announced key executive appointments, with Richard Simonelli rejoining as head of investor relations and Cyndi Eakin promoted to chief accounting officer. These moves aim to strengthen CoStar's C-suite amidst an intensifying portal war in the real estate industry. Both Simonelli and Eakin will report to Christian Lown, the company's new chief financial officer, as CoStar focuses on growth, particularly for its Homes.com platform.

title: Deal for “high-performance” batteries signed for mystery Mixed Reality leader
summary: Battery manufacturer Enovix has signed a deal to supply high-performance batteries to a "clear leader in the mixed reality market" based in California. This mysterious client is speculated to be either Apple, potentially for a future Vision Pro model, or Meta, possibly for the rumored Meta Quest 3S. The deal aims to address the generally poor battery life of current mixed reality headsets, a significant issue for immersive devices.

title: EssilorLuxottica surges 7% as Meta explores multi-billion investment in company 📈
summary: EssilorLuxottica (EL.FR) saw its stock surge by nearly 7% following reports that Meta Platforms (META.US) is considering a multi-billion euro investment in the company. This potential deal is speculated to involve Meta's interest in integrating AR technology into Ray-Ban 'smart glasses' and comes shortly after EssilorLuxottica acquired an 80% stake in Heidelberg Engineering.

title: Skild AI估值100亿，今年最快独角兽诞生了
summary: Skild AI，一家由两位前卡内基梅隆大学教授于2023年5月创立的具身智能初创公司，在成立仅一年后完成了3亿美元的A轮融资，估值达到15亿美元（约109亿人民币），成为今年增长最快的独角兽之一。该公司致力于开发通用人工智能（AGI）驱动的人形机器人产品，专注于构建可应用于各种机器人的通用大脑Skild Brain。尽管融资火热，具身智能领域也面临商业化前景不明、大厂竞争激烈以及行业泡沫等挑战，如李开复等投资人对其短期落地和人形机器人必要性持保留态度。

title: CuriosityStream Lawsuit Alleges User Data Unlawfully Shared With Facebook
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against CuriosityStream, alleging the company unlawfully shared users' private data, including video-viewing histories and Facebook IDs, with Facebook without consent. The lawsuit claims CuriosityStream used a "Meta pixel" tracking tool to collect and transmit this information, violating the federal Video Privacy Protection Act. The suit seeks to represent U.S. users who have accounts on both platforms and viewed videos on CuriosityStream.

title: Reddit (RDDT) Stock: Unpacking This Newly Listed Tech Co.
summary: Reddit stock has seen a 30% return since its March IPO, outperforming the S&P 500, but its dual share structure raises questions about valuation sustainability despite robust user growth. The article delves into Reddit's market debut and compares it to past tech company IPOs like Meta and Snap. The author expresses skepticism regarding the current valuation.

title: KraneShares Artificial Intelligence and Technology ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:AGIX)
summary: The KraneShares Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AGIX) invests in companies developing AI technologies globally. Launched on July 18, 2024, by KraneShares, AGIX has experienced a 5.0% decrease in stock price since January 1st, 2026, and holds significant stakes in major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms, along with private AI firms like OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI. Its top institutional investors include LPL Financial LLC and Westwind Capital.

title: Solar R&D innovation center advances PV technology
summary: First Solar has commissioned the largest thin-film R&D center in the Western Hemisphere in Lake Township, Ohio, investing $500 million in R&D infrastructure to accelerate innovation in PV technology. Meanwhile, talks between Google and cybersecurity firm Wiz for a $23 billion acquisition have stalled, and several R&D World Index members have experienced mixed fortunes.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Meta a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: This article by Morningstar analyzes Meta Platforms (META) ahead of its Q2 earnings release on July 31st, evaluating if the stock is a buy, sell, or fairly valued. Key points of focus include advertising demand, AI monetization strategies, and platform usage growth. Morningstar assigns a 2-star rating, indicating Meta's stock is overvalued compared to its long-term fair value estimate of $400.00, but notes the company's wide economic moat and strong financial strength.

title: Walmart class action alleges company shares customer data with Meta
summary: Three Walmart customers have filed a class action lawsuit against Walmart, alleging the company illegally shared their video viewing information with Meta. The lawsuit claims Walmart used the "Meta Pixel" to transmit customers' Facebook IDs and details of purchased video content to Meta without consent, violating the Video Privacy Protection Act. The plaintiffs are seeking to represent all U.S. customers who bought video material from Walmart's website while having a Meta account in the past two years, asking for certification of the class action and $2,500 for each class member.

title: Top 10 Data Centre CEOs: Leading the Future of Digital Infrastructure
summary: This article lists the top 10 CEOs in the data centre industry, highlighting their roles in shaping strategies and driving growth in their respective companies. These leaders from major players like Digital Realty, Equinix, Google Cloud Platform, Microsoft Azure, and AWS are recognized for their contributions to technological innovation, efficiency, sustainability, and customer satisfaction in the global data centre sector. Each CEO's background, company's revenue, and key achievements are detailed.

title: Tesla, Google Spark Sell-Off in Technology ETFs
summary: Tesla and Alphabet's Q2 earnings results triggered a significant sell-off in the technology sector, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping 3.6%. Tesla's weaker-than-expected performance and Alphabet's lower-than-consensus YouTube ad revenues impacted the "Magnificent 7" stocks and several technology-focused ETFs. Investors are now keenly awaiting the upcoming earnings reports from other major tech companies like Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Nvidia.

title: Did Coursera.com Violate Your Privacy Rights?
summary: Attorneys are investigating Coursera.com for potential privacy law violations, specifically regarding the alleged secret sharing of user video-watching data with Facebook via a tracking tool. This action may violate the federal Video Privacy Protection Act, prompting a mass arbitration effort to seek compensation for affected users. Users with Coursera accounts who have taken courses are encouraged to sign up, with attorneys only getting paid if they win.

title: 5 Stocks to Buy Now and Hold for the Long Term
summary: This article discusses the Federal Reserve's potential monetary policy changes, analysis of various tech stocks like Microsoft, Meta, Apple, Amazon, AMD, and Intel after their earnings reports, and the performance of Tesla and Alphabet. It also highlights investment opportunities in Johnson & Johnson, Exxon Mobil, Alphabet, Pepsi, and Verizon, recommended by Morningstar for long-term holding.

title: Meta faces AI accuracy issues as tech industry tackles hallucinations, deepfakes
summary: Meta is addressing accuracy issues with its AI chatbot, Meta AI, after it incorrectly claimed the assassination attempt on Donald Trump didn't happen and misapplied a fact-checking label to a photo. This comes as other tech giants like Google and Baidu also work on improving AI safety and accuracy, with Google focusing on deepfake removal and Baidu developing a "self-reasoning" mechanism for LLMs. The incidents highlight the ongoing challenges of AI hallucinations and the industry's efforts to mitigate them.

title: Meta Platforms ahead of Q2 2024 earnings📊 What to expect?🔎
summary: Meta Platforms is set to report its Q2 2024 earnings, with investors keenly focused on the health of its advertising segment, the impact of new AI products, and the company's margins amid high capital expenditures. Analysts expect revenues of $38.29 billion and EPS of $4.70, with particular attention on how AI translates into business growth and the potential benefits from a TikTok ban in the US. The market will also scrutinize Reality Labs' estimated operating loss and Meta's capital spending forecasts.

title: Stock price takes off after performance report! Meta's financial report is full of surprises, with Q2 net profit soaring by 73%, thanks to AI-assisted advertising business growth.
summary: Meta Platforms (META.US) announced its Q2 2024 financial results, reporting a 73% surge in net profit to $13.465 billion and a 22% increase in total revenue to $39.071 billion, largely driven by AI-assisted advertising growth. The company's financial data, including revenue and EPS, exceeded market expectations, leading to a significant pre-market stock price increase. Meta continues to invest heavily in AI, confident in its future prospects despite high capital expenditures.

title: IVV ETF or VAS ETF: Where should I invest?
summary: This article compares two popular ETFs, the Vanguard Australian Shares Index ETF (VAS) and the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV), for Australian investors. While IVV has outperformed VAS significantly over the past five to ten years due to the surge in American tech giants, the author argues for a balanced approach, suggesting an equal allocation to both ETFs. This strategy would create a diversified portfolio blending IVV's growth-oriented, tech-heavy exposure with VAS's income-focused, Australian banking and mining tilt.

title: ETF Spotlight: FBL Soars on Meta Stock Performance
summary: The GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL) experienced a significant surge after Meta Platforms Inc. exceeded second-quarter earnings expectations. FBL's gains, up over 68.5% year-to-date, are directly linked to Meta's improved performance, driven by increased digital advertising revenue and investments in AI technology. Analysts note Meta's strong revenue growth surpassed that of other tech giants.

title: Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence and Big Data ETF Holdings
summary: The article details the holdings of the Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence and Big Data ETF (XAIX), which was established on August 2, 2024. The ETF focuses on theme equity with an emphasis on artificial intelligence and big data, holding 92 positions and managing 111.51 million in assets under management. Key holdings include tech giants like Samsung Electronics, Micron Technology, Alibaba, and Meta Platforms.

title: Coursera Introduces New Gen AI Skills Training and Credentials
summary: Coursera is expanding its Generative AI Academy with "GenAI for Teams," designed to equip teams with AI skills tailored to business functions. The platform also introduced seven new generative AI courses, specializations, and certificates, and updated eight entry-level Professional Certificates with Gen AI content. These offerings aim to enhance AI knowledge across various professional roles from software development to marketing.

title: Patreon to Pay $7.25 Million in Deal Over Info-Sharing With Meta
summary: Patreon Inc. has agreed to pay $7.25 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging that it unlawfully shared subscribers' video viewing information and personal data with Meta Platforms Inc. without consent. The lawsuit claims Patreon used Meta's "pixel" and other tracking tools on its website, violating the Video Privacy Protection Act by collecting users' video habits and Facebook IDs. Class members are entitled to a pro-rata share of the settlement fund.

title: Ziff Davis Acquires CNET for over $100M
summary: Digital media firm Ziff Davis (NASDAQ: ZD) is acquiring CNET from Red Ventures for over $100M, further expanding its portfolio of technology-focused online news brands. This acquisition complements Ziff Davis's existing tech properties like Mashable, PC Mag, and Lifehacker, and is part of a broader strategy as Ziff Davis eyes more deals with approximately $800M in cash on hand. Ziff CEO Vivek Shah anticipates more industry consolidation due to increased digital ad spending by tech giants and the disruptive potential of artificial intelligence.

title: 挑战英伟达，AI 芯片公司 Groq 成功融资 6.4 亿美元：估值飙升至 28 亿美元
summary: AI芯片初创公司Groq近日成功完成6.4亿美元融资，使其总融资额超过10亿美元，公司估值飙升至28亿美元。本轮融资旨在加速开发其LPU推理引擎，以更快处理AI任务，有望挑战英伟达在AI芯片领域的领先地位。Groq还宣布Meta首席人工智能科学家Yann LeCun将担任技术顾问，并计划扩大产能和增加模型支持。

title: Amplitude Snares Private Data of Millions Via Apps, Suit Says
summary: A proposed class action lawsuit alleges that Amplitude Inc.'s technology, embedded in popular mobile apps like DoorDash, surreptitiously collects and shares millions of consumers' geolocation data and other sensitive information with third parties without consent. The complaint, filed in the US District Court for the Northern District of California, states that this collected data, including names and email addresses, reveals users' locations. This practice raises significant privacy concerns about unsolicited data harvesting and sharing by analytics firms.

title: Daniel Peris: The Case for Dividend Investing
summary: Daniel Peris, a portfolio manager at Federated Hermes and author of "The Ownership Dividend," discusses the historical importance of dividends and argues for a paradigm shift back to dividend investing. He criticizes modern portfolio theory's overemphasis on share price appreciation and extreme diversification, advocating for a business-owner's perspective grounded in tangible cash flows. Peris attributes the past decline in dividends to falling interest rates, the rise of buybacks, Silicon Valley's growth, and global sociopolitical shifts, and believes a return to more normal interest rates will renew focus on cash payouts.

title: Infillion Named to Inc. 5000 List for 8th Consecutive Year
summary: Infillion, an advanced media buying platform, has been named to the 2024 Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing private companies in America for the eighth consecutive year. The company's consistent growth is attributed to its ability to adapt to changing advertising landscapes and its recent acquisition of MediaMath, expanding its multiplatform digital advertising solutions. Infillion's founder and executive chair, Rob Emrich, and CFO, Angela Birkemeier, highlighted the team's dedication and rapid innovation.

title: Big XR News from Meta, Vuzix, Pico, Apple
summary: The XR industry is abuzz with upcoming hardware announcements from Meta, Pico, and HTC VIVE, coinciding with a new wave of mainstream interest. Meta is expected to reveal more about its Quest 3s MR headset, while Pico's new 4S MR headset has been leaked. Concurrently, Vuzix Corporation reported a significant 77 percent revenue drop in Q2 2024 but anticipates improvement, and rumors suggest a lower-priced Apple Vision Pro could launch next year.

title: A Record 8.5% of U.S. Homes Are Worth $1 Million or More
summary: A record 8.5% of U.S. homes, totaling over 8 million, are now valued at $1 million or more, an increase from 7.6% a year ago and more than double the pre-pandemic share of 4%. This surge is driven by historically low housing inventory and rising home prices, even amidst high mortgage rates that dampen demand. While this benefits homeowners, it presents significant affordability challenges for prospective buyers, especially in high-cost areas like California metros.

title: AMD to acquire server builder ZT Systems for $4.9 billion in cash and stock
summary: AMD announced its plan to acquire server maker ZT Systems for $4.9 billion in cash and stock to expand its AI chip and hardware portfolio and bolster its competition with Nvidia. The acquisition aims to integrate ZT Systems' engineering expertise to accelerate the testing and deployment of AMD's AI GPUs for cloud computing giants, with AMD planning to divest ZT Systems' manufacturing business post-acquisition. The deal is expected to close in the first half of 2025 and contribute to AMD's adjusted financial performance by the end of 2025.

title: AMD to acquire ZT Systems for $4.9B
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is set to acquire server manufacturer ZT Systems for $4.9 billion in a cash and stock transaction. This move is part of AMD's strategy to expand its AI capabilities and compete more effectively with Nvidia, leveraging ZT Systems' expertise in cloud computing design services and data center infrastructure. The deal is expected to close by the end of next year, with AMD planning to sell ZT Systems' U.S.-based data center infrastructure manufacturing business afterward.

title: Going into earnings, is Nvidia stock a buy, a sell, or fairly valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Nvidia (NVDA) stock ahead of its Q2 earnings report, assigning it a 3-star rating, indicating it is fairly valued at a $105 fair value estimate. The article highlights Nvidia's strong Data Center business growth and wide economic moat, driven by its GPU technology and CUDA software platform, while acknowledging risks from increasing competition and the potential for a future inventory correction in AI demand. Analysts expect Nvidia to exceed guidance due to supply constraints and strong hyperscaler capital expenditure spending on AI.

title: Will Alphabet (GOOG) AI Phones Crush Apple’s AI Plans?
summary: This article examines whether Alphabet's new AI-powered Pixel phones will challenge Apple's AI ambitions, highlighting the stabilization and potential rebound of AI stocks. It delves into analyst opinions from RBC Wealth Management and Wedbush, who see the recent pullback as a healthy correction and an early stage of an AI bull market. The piece focuses on Alphabet (GOOG), its Gemini Nano AI assistant, despite market pressures like a potential Justice Department breakup and competition from OpenAI.

title: Immersion Stock Surges as It Monetizes Haptic Technology Patents
summary: Immersion Corp. (NASDAQ: IMMR), a pioneer in haptic technology, saw its stock surge after it settled a patent lawsuit with Meta Platforms Inc. for $87.5 million and secured a licensing agreement. The company specializes in licensing its extensive patent portfolio for haptic feedback used in billions of devices and has also made a significant investment in Barnes and Noble Education Inc. Immersion continues to evolve its active sensing technology and monetize its intellectual property.

title: Classroom quitters to industry leaders: These 14 billionaires dropped out of school to chase success
summary: This article highlights 14 billionaires who dropped out of formal education to pursue their entrepreneurial dreams and achieve significant success. It spotlights individuals like Ralph Lauren, Oprah Winfrey, Bill Gates, and Mark Zuckerberg, detailing their early career decisions and current net worth according to Forbes. The piece illustrates that success is not always linear and can be achieved through ambition and unconventional paths.

title: A Dot-Com-Like Bust Isn’t in the Cards for AI Investors Who Diversify
summary: Concerns about an AI bubble akin to the dot-com bust are overblown, according to J.P. Morgan Private Bank, citing lower valuations and stronger earnings expectations for current AI leaders. While investing in an S&P 500 index fund provides some AI exposure, investors seeking outperformance should diversify across AI infrastructure (AI 1.0) and AI applications (AI 2.0), potentially including global companies and low-cost ETFs for broader exposure. A long-term perspective is crucial as AI is expected to be a multi-year or multi-decade trend.

title: Global data center industry to emit 2.5 billion tons of CO2 through 2030, Morgan Stanley says
summary: The global data center industry is projected to emit approximately 2.5 billion metric tons of CO2-equivalent emissions by 2030, according to Morgan Stanley research. This surge is driven by hyperscalers like Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon expanding AI and cloud computing. The emissions are expected to accelerate investments in decarbonization solutions, clean power, and green building materials, as tech companies strive to meet climate pledges.

title: Gaia & International Yoga members may be eligible for a Share of a $2 million settlement
summary: Gaia, Inc. has agreed to a $2 million class action settlement for allegedly violating the Video Privacy Protection Act (VPPA) by sharing personally identifiable information with Facebook via a tracking pixel. Users who watched videos on Gaia or Yoga International websites between September 12, 2020, and July 19, 2024, and had a Facebook account during that period, may be eligible to claim a pro rata share of the settlement fund. Claims must be submitted by December 2, 2024.

title: New Mexico accuses Snapchat of being ‘breeding ground’ for child predators
summary: New Mexico has filed a lawsuit against Snap Inc., accusing Snapchat of facilitating child sexual exploitation and becoming a "breeding ground" for predators due to its ephemeral messaging features. The state's attorney general, Raúl Torrez, stated that an undercover investigation found a "vast network" of dark websites sharing nonconsensual images from Snapchat. Snap Inc. is reviewing the complaint and maintains its commitment to making Snapchat a safe platform for young users, highlighting ongoing efforts to combat bad actors and enhance online safety tools.

title: IXP ETF: Growth Profile Not Attractive
summary: The iShares Global Comm Services ETF (IXP) carries significant concentration risk, with Meta Platforms and Alphabet constituting 42.6% of its portfolio, making its performance highly dependent on these companies' results. The fund's reliance on digital advertising spending is a concern due to decelerating growth in this sector, potentially limiting future returns. Despite an attractive valuation, IXP's slow growth profile and sensitivity to economic conditions make it less appealing for long-term investors.

title: Cousins lands IBM for Domain building left vacant by Meta
summary: IBM has leased the entire 320,000-square-foot office space at Domain 12 in North Austin, previously vacated by Meta Platforms. This move, starting in January 2026 and extending the lease through 2040, marks a significant shift in Austin's tech real estate, especially given the city's high office vacancy rates. Meanwhile, Meta continues to downsize its Austin footprint as part of broader cost-cutting measures, highlighting contrasting real estate strategies between the two tech giants.

title: The 2024 Proxy Season: Which ESG Proposals Made the Podium?
summary: Support for environmental and social resolutions in the 2024 proxy season continued to decline, while governance proposals saw a rebound. Morningstar's analysis introduces a new three-tier system (Gold, Silver, Bronze) to evaluate ESG proposals, revealing that while top-tier support has shrunk, a committed core of sustainability-conscious asset managers continues to back proposals in the lower tiers. The article also notes an increase in the number of resolutions with less than 30% adjusted support, raising questions about resolution quality and the future of shareholder democracy.

title: Big tech firms balk at Ohio electric utility demand to pay for grid upgrades
summary: Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta are resisting demands from an Ohio utility, American Electric Power Ohio, to pay for electric grid upgrades needed to support their energy-intensive AI data centers. The utility argues these payments are necessary to prevent costs from being passed on to consumers, while the tech firms deem the proposed tariff "unfair" and "discriminatory." A hearing is scheduled for October 30 to address the dispute, which highlights the increasing energy demands of AI technology and the associated infrastructure costs.

title: BuzzFeed $9 Million Facebook Settlement: Can You Claim?
summary: BuzzFeed is facing a class-action lawsuit for allegedly sharing user data with Facebook without consent, leading to a proposed $9 million settlement. Users who watched videos on BuzzFeed, HuffPost, Tasty, or Complex websites between May 16, 2021, and November 10, 2023, and were account holders or newsletter subscribers, may be eligible to claim an $8 payment and a free one-year BuzzFeed+ subscription. The deadline to file a claim is November 5th.

title: The Scoop: Philip Morris International sells inhaler company after backlash
summary: Philip Morris International (PMI) is selling Vectura, a British pharmaceutical inhaler company it acquired three years ago, due to significant backlash and "unreasonable burden of external constraints and criticism." The acquisition was part of PMI's "Beyond Nicotine" initiative but faced accusations of hypocrisy given its tobacco products. The article highlights the PR challenges associated with such mergers and acquisitions, suggesting the importance of early PR involvement in strategic decision-making.

title: Is It Too Late to Buy Rezolve AI Stock?
summary: Rezolve AI Limited's stock soared over 103% due to a major healthcare contract, innovative AI integration, and new partnerships with ePages, ChatWerk, and OXID. These strategic alliances and the unveiling of new AI solutions, Brain Suite and brainpowa LLM, are expected to drive significant growth and redefine retail and commerce sectors, despite some financial complexities like a total equity deficit. The article suggests that investing in Rezolve AI is an investment into the future of eCommerce and AI, emphasizing high risk with potential high rewards.

title: 13 Black leaders in technology
summary: This article highlights the significant contributions of 13 influential Black leaders in the technology sector, acknowledging the industry's diversity challenges despite these innovations. It also recognizes seven historical Black tech leaders whose work continues to impact modern technology. The piece aims to inspire and encourage greater representation of Black professionals in tech by showcasing their achievements and ongoing efforts to foster diversity and inclusion.

title: Call it what you want, Taylor Swift is a model community manager
summary: This article defines social media community management, distinguishing it from social media management, and explains its importance for brands in building loyalty, gaining insights, and enhancing reputation. It outlines a strategic approach to community management including goal setting, audience identification, moderation, and measurement, offering best practices and real-world examples from brands like Oatly, Texas A&M University, and Lyft.

title: Google announces $3.3B investment in SC data centers, 200 new jobs
summary: Google announced a new $3.3 billion investment in its South Carolina data centers, creating 200 new jobs. This includes breaking ground on two new centers in Dorchester County worth $2 billion and a $1.3 billion expansion of its Berkeley County center. The investment comes amidst ongoing discussions in the Statehouse about the significant energy demands of data centers and the incentives offered by the state and local governments.

title: Guo Mingxi: Meta AR glasses Orion will be mass-produced as early as 2027, positioning to replace mobile phones and computers.
summary: Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo from TF International Securities predicts that Meta's AR glasses, Orion, could enter mass production as early as 2027, aiming to replace smartphones and tablets. Orion's key selling points are its integration of AI technology and spatial computing, though technical challenges and production partner decisions could delay the timeline. Meta may release a lower-spec version before the full-scale mass production to gather market feedback.

title: David Abrams – Top 10 Holdings – Latest 13F
summary: This article details the top 10 holdings of superinvestor David Abrams based on his latest 13F-HR document (as of June 30, 2024). His portfolio has a market value exceeding $5.1 billion, with 96.98% concentrated in his top 10 holdings. The list includes companies like LOAR HOLDINGS, LITHIA MOTORS, and ALPHABET INC CLASS A.

title: Arkansas AG's lawsuit accuses YouTube and Google of targeting minors with harmful content
summary: Arkansas Attorney General Tim Griffin has filed a consumer protection lawsuit against Google, YouTube, and their parent companies, alleging they intentionally target minors with addictive and harmful content. The lawsuit, filed in Phillips County Circuit Court, claims YouTube exploits the "undeveloped capacity for self-regulation" in children's brains, leading to mental health issues and addiction. Griffin is seeking monetary damages, disgorgement of profits, and funding for prevention education and treatment for excessive social media use.

title: NBXG: Deep Discount, Attractive Yield On This Technology Fund With A Termination Date
summary: The Neuberger Berman Next Generation Connectivity Fund (NBXG) is highlighted as an attractive investment due to its focus on tech and communications, appealing 12%+ discount, and activist involvement. The fund also offers a strong yield through its managed monthly distribution plan and features a limited-term structure with a termination date in 2033. This combination, along with improved recent performance and strong Quant Profitability Grades for top holdings, suggests potential for capturing alpha.

title: Meta hit with new author copyright lawsuit over AI training
summary: Novelist Christopher Farnsworth has filed a class-action copyright lawsuit against Meta Platforms, accusing the tech giant of misusing his books and others to train its Llama artificial-intelligence large language model. This lawsuit is similar to other class-action claims brought by authors like Ta-Nehisi Coates and Sarah Silverman. Meta is being asked for unspecified monetary damages and an order to halt alleged infringement, arguing that its AI training is protected by fair use.

title: Meta, Shutterstock Accused of Underpaying Engineer by $2 Million
summary: A former Giphy Inc. engineer, Yael Elmatad, has filed a lawsuit against Shutterstock Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc., alleging she was underpaid by over $2 million compared to her male colleagues in similar engineering roles. The lawsuit asserts that Elmatad discovered the pay disparity earlier this year through a spreadsheet revealing discrepancies in restricted stock unit award agreements. The complaint was filed in the US District Court for the Southern District of New York.

title: Oracle's Larry Ellison is getting richer, rivaling Musk, Bezos, Zuckerberg
summary: Oracle co-founder and CTO Larry Ellison's net worth has surged to an estimated $208 billion, placing him firmly among the wealthiest individuals globally, behind Elon Musk and ahead of Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos, according to Forbes. His wealth growth is largely attributed to his substantial 41% stake in Oracle, whose stock has seen a 63% increase year-to-date. Oracle's strong financial performance, with reported revenues of $13.3 billion and net income of $2.9 billion in Q1, contributes significantly to this rise, with forecasts suggesting Oracle could reach a $1 trillion market capitalization by 2030.

title: Ubisoft Targeted in Lawsuit Also Involving Meta
summary: Ubisoft is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it unlawfully collected and shared customer data with Meta Platforms using Meta's Pixel tool without explicit consent. The lawsuit claims these actions violate state and federal wiretapping laws, including the Video Privacy Protection Act and Wiretap Act. Plaintiffs are seeking monetary damages and injunctive relief to prevent future alleged privacy violations.

title: MSTX Share Price & Live Chart | Defiance Daily Target 1.75X Long MSTR ETF
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the Defiance Daily Target 1.75X Long MSTR ETF (MSTX), including its current share price, historical performance, and key financial metrics. It highlights technical analysis, holdings, and dividend information, while also offering tools for investors to evaluate the ETF. The article also provides FAQs and a market overview with various indices and commodities data.

title: Meta funds European entity that lets people challenge social media companies' content decisions
summary: Meta is funding a new European entity, the Appeals Centre Europe, which will allow individuals to challenge content decisions made by social media companies. The center will be based in Ireland and aims to provide an avenue for users to dispute content moderation outcomes. This initiative comes amidst ongoing regulatory scrutiny and fines against Meta in the EU.

title: How AI gives Hershey an edge for high-stakes Halloween season
summary: Hershey is leveraging artificial intelligence to optimize its advertising during the high-stakes Halloween season, focusing on underserved markets rather than oversaturated ones. By working with Chalice Custom Algorithms, the company aims to allocate marketing budgets more effectively across platforms like Meta, YouTube, and The Trade Desk, especially given the influx of political advertising during an election year. This precision marketing strategy proved successful in a trial last Halloween, leading to increased sell-through with a key retailer.

title: Instagram and TikTok Remain the Most Popular Social Apps Among Teens
summary: A Piper Sandler survey of over 13,000 U.S. teens indicates that Instagram is still the most used social app, despite TikTok being preferred for entertainment. While Snapchat has declined, Pinterest and Facebook have seen slight resurgences in teen interest. These insights suggest that targeting young audiences should still primarily focus on Instagram and TikTok, with potential to explore Pinterest and Facebook's AI ads.

title: US Stock Market Outlook Q4 2024: Will the Great Rotation Persist?
summary: This Morningstar Q4 2024 outlook discusses the potential persistence of the market "great rotation" from big-cap growth to value and small/mid-cap stocks. Analysts Dave Sekera and Preston Caldwell provide insights into market valuations, sector performance, top stock picks, and the US economic outlook, including inflation and interest rate expectations, maintaining a soft landing forecast. The article emphasizes undervalued opportunities in certain sectors despite the overall market trading at a premium.

He emphasizes investing in "old economy" companies with sustainable business models and growing dividends, citing a new era for dividend investing as more companies, including tech giants, begin to initiate payouts. Peris highlights the importance of real returns after inflation and the sustainability of income streams for long-term investors.

title: Wipro's hybrid policy: Employees to work from office three days a week

The policy aims to balance innovation through face-to-face collaboration with employees' personal needs, allowing flexibility in choosing in-office days and offering remote work options for personal well-being or caregiving. This move aligns with a broader industry trend of major tech companies requiring employees to return to the office, including TCS, Infosys, Amazon, and IBM.

title: Meta Can’t Escape States’ Claims It Hooked Kids on Platforms (4)

title: Investing In Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks Can Be Risky, but Buying This Global X ETF Might Be a Great Way to Do It
summary: Investing in individual AI stocks can be risky due to the difficulty of picking winners, as historical technological revolutions have shown. A safer approach for investors looking to capitalize on AI's growth is to buy an exchange-traded fund (ETF) with a high concentration of AI stocks, such as the Global X Artificial Intelligence and Technology ETF. This ETF offers diversification across 84 AI-focused companies and has historically outperformed the S&P 500 despite its higher expense ratio, making it a potentially attractive option for long-term growth as part of a diversified portfolio.

title: iShares Top 20 U.S. Stocks ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:TOPT) $TOPT
summary: The iShares Top 20 U.S. Stocks ETF (TOPT) tracks the S&P 500 Top 20 Select index, aiming to replicate the performance of the 20 largest U.S. firms by market capitalization within the S&P 500. summary: The article explores the rising trend of AI-created virtual avatars in digital selling and influencing, highlighting their growing acceptance and appeal in various industries. The author emphasizes the importance of ethical practices and transparency as the line between real and virtual content blurs, urging brands to carefully consider the limitations, purpose, and value-add of these technologies.

title: Which AI Stocks Are Leading the Market to New Highs?
summary: After a sharp selloff, AI-related stocks, including Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon, are once again driving the market to new highs, contributing significantly to recent gains. This current tech-led recovery mirrors earlier market trends since October 2022, with technology and financial services sectors consistently leading the way.

summary: The Layer 1 blockchain Sui has launched an "incubator" hub in Dubai, positioning it as the first in a global series. This hub aims to support blockchain developers and entrepreneurs, with a vision to facilitate "on-the-spot" solution engineering, particularly for government entities requiring rapid blockchain-based proofs of concept. summary: The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS) offers a diversified, quarterly rebalanced investment strategy focused on the Magnificent Seven tech giants, which are expected to lead market growth. title: Paltalk, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:PALT) 28% Dip Still Leaving Some Shareholders Feeling Restless Over Its P/SRatio
summary: Paltalk, Inc. (PALT) shares have dropped 28% in the last month, yet its Price-to-Sales (P/S) ratio of 1.9x still stands above the industry average of 1.2x, causing concern among shareholders. The company's revenue is declining, and analysts predict further decreases, making its current high P/S ratio questionable. Despite warnings from analysts about future revenue declines, many investors appear to be holding onto their stock, potentially risking significant investment.

title: OpenAI launched a new browser to challenge Google's market position, causing Alphabet's stock price to fall.
summary: OpenAI launched its new AI-powered browser, ChatGPT Atlas, directly challenging Google's Chrome and causing a fluctuation in Alphabet's stock price. The new browser features an intelligent assistant and agent mode, prompting speculation about its potential impact on Chrome's market share. This launch comes amidst an increasingly competitive AI browser landscape and a recent antitrust ruling against Google, further intensifying competition among tech giants.

title: Internet Ad Spending Could Be Key for These ETFs
summary: The article discusses how internet ad spending, particularly from Meta Platforms (META) and Alphabet (GOOG), could be a key factor for ETFs like Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) during upcoming earnings reports. Analyst commentary suggests Meta continues to gain market share in advertising, while Alphabet's YouTube and Google Search could also see strong growth driven by AI enhancements. The performance of these tech giants' advertising revenues will offer insights into broader market trends.

title: Instagram poised to crack Korean webcomics market
summary: Instagram is rapidly gaining traction in the South Korean webcomics market, with its usage among Korean users rising significantly, surpassing Kakao Webtoon to become the fourth most-used digital comics platform. This rise is attributed to Instagram's lower barriers for creators and users, as well as its support programs for webcomics. The increased presence of global big tech companies like Instagram and Google in the Korean webcomics market is putting pressure on local platforms, prompting Kakao Entertainment to incorporate AI to enhance its competitiveness.

title: Twitter Names Marjorie Scardino to Board
summary: Twitter has appointed Marjorie Scardino, former CEO of Pearson PLC, to its board of directors, addressing previous criticism regarding the absence of women on its all-male board. Scardino, 66, was the first woman to lead a FTSE 100 company and joins the board alongside CEO Dick Costolo and co-founder Jack Dorsey. Her appointment comes after Twitter faced scrutiny, similar to Facebook, for its lack of board diversity.

title: Zumba Fitness Shared Users’ Data With Meta, Pinterest, Suit Says
summary: A proposed class-action lawsuit alleges that Zumba Fitness LLC unlawfully shared users' personal information and viewing history with Meta Platforms Inc. and Pinterest Inc. without consent. The suit claims Zumba's website used Meta Pixel software to track activity and disclose data, including names and email addresses, violating a federal video privacy law. The complaint was filed in the US District Court for the Southern District of Florida.

title: Is Deckers Stock a Buy After Its Beat-And-Raise Quarter?
summary: Deckers Outdoor (DECK) stock rose after reporting strong fiscal second-quarter earnings, beating expectations and raising its full-year profit forecast, driven by significant growth in its Hoka brand. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Buy" rating for DECK, anticipating further upside based on continued product innovation and market segmentation. The company's robust performance year-to-date and positive outlook for the holiday season suggest ongoing growth potential.
