summary: Nandan Nilekani, co-founder of Infosys, believes India should focus on becoming the "use case capital for AI" rather than building large language models (LLMs). He highlights how digital public infrastructure (DPI) can provide a foundation for AI to grow rapidly and address issues like inclusion and education, leveraging existing AI tools to solve real-world problems for a billion people in a frugal manner. Nilekani advocates for a balanced approach to responsible AI and innovation.

title: How Nvidia Overtook Apple as World's Most Valuable Company
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Apple to become the world's most valuable company, driven by the immense demand for its specialized AI chips crucial for advanced AI applications. This shift highlights the growing importance of AI-driven technologies in the tech market, with Nvidia benefiting significantly from the race among tech giants to dominate AI. While Apple later regained its lead, Nvidia's surge underscores the impact of AI on market valuations and investment trends.

summary: Sierra Technologies, an AI startup co-founded by ex-Salesforce co-CEO Bret Taylor, has secured $175 million in funding, pushing its valuation to $4.5 billion. The company develops AI-powered virtual assistants for customer service, featuring personalized conversational models capable of utilizing business data to resolve customer issues and reduce human agent intervention. Sierra differentiates itself through customizable AI agent personalities and a "constellation" approach to large language models for enhanced reliability.

summary: COPT Defense Properties is planning a 1GW data center campus near Des Moines, Iowa, after acquiring a 365-acre land parcel for $32 million. The campus is projected to include 15 buildings totaling 3.3 million square feet and will serve an existing Fortune 100 cloud computing tenant. This expansion leverages Des Moines' attractive land values, power availability, renewable energy access, and tax incentives.

While the fund's top holdings in e-commerce, robotics, metaverse, and online gambling show promise, their individual performance needs to solidify for ARKK to confirm a sustained upward trend.

title: Meta Reports Third Quarter 2024 Results
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. reported strong Q3 2024 financial results, with total revenue reaching $40.59 billion, marking a 19% increase year-over-year. The company's net income rose to $15.69 billion, a 35% increase, driven by progress in AI across its applications and business. Meta also provided optimistic financial guidance for Q4 2024, projecting revenue between $45-48 billion, while expecting continued significant investments in Reality Labs and infrastructure.

title: Meta Earnings: Strong Q3 and Promising AI Developments | Morningstar UK
summary: Meta reported strong third-quarter results with revenue up 19% year over year and improved profitability, driven by its advertising engine and significant AI investments. The company's AI-powered ad opportunities are enhancing user engagement and monetization, while progress in Threads and Reality Labs, particularly with Ray-Ban AR glasses, also contributed to growth. While these layoffs are devastating, overall numbers for tech layoffs in 2024 are down compared to 2023, with 142,532 employees laid off from 484 companies this year versus 264,220 from 1,193 companies last year. Dropbox, for instance, cut 20% of its workforce, citing softening demand and an overly complex organizational structure.

title: Bay Area tech layoffs hit Upwork, Coursera, Kraken and other companies
summary: Several major Bay Area tech companies, including Coursera, Kraken, dYdX Trading, Miro, and Upwork, announced significant layoffs in October, impacting hundreds of employees. These companies are aiming for greater efficiency, profitability, and renewed focus as they navigate changing economic landscapes.

summary: Analysts from TD Cowen and Goldman Sachs have issued bullish sentiments on IAC/InteractiveCorp. and Meta Platforms, respectively, within the Communication Services sector. Both companies also received positive consensus ratings and price targets from other analysts, indicating potential upside from current levels.

While acknowledging uncertainty from investments in generative AI and Reality Labs, Morningstar highlights Meta's wide economic moat, strong financial health, and long-term monetization opportunities in Asia.

title: United States Supreme Court to Hear Two Securities Cases This Term
summary: The Supreme Court is scheduled to hear two securities cases, Facebook, Inc. v. Amalgamated Bank and NVIDIA Corp. v. E. Ohman J:or Fonder AB and Stichting Pensioenfonds PGB, both concerning the pleading standard in securities fraud class actions. These cases will clarify whether companies must disclose past instances of risk materialization and further define the heightened pleading standards for scienter and falsity under the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. The outcomes could significantly impact future securities fraud litigation and the role of expert opinions in such cases.

summary: A federal court has largely allowed a lawsuit against Costco to proceed, challenging its use of Meta's tracking pixel on its pharmacy website. The lawsuit alleges that Costco illegally collected and shared sensitive prescription-related data, potentially violating federal and California privacy laws. The court's decision emphasizes that prescription searches are considered protected content, raising significant liability concerns for healthcare providers using similar tracking tools.

summary: The European Commission has fined Meta Platforms €797.72 million ($840.24 million) for breaching EU antitrust rules. The fine relates to abusive practices that benefited Facebook Marketplace, specifically tying its online classified ads service to Facebook and imposing unfair trading conditions on other providers. Meta has stated it will appeal the decision but will comply in the interim to launch a solution.

This strategic pivot includes leadership changes and a push to tailor content for platforms like TikTok, Facebook, YouTube, Netflix, and Hulu.

These devices aim to enhance user interaction in extended reality (XR) experiences by interpreting neural signals from wrist movements, offering a hands-free interface compatible with various operating systems. The technology allows gesture recognition even when hands are not visible, setting a new standard for user interaction in the rapidly evolving tech landscape.

The app is designed to be user-propelled, allowing individuals to ask and answer questions about local recommendations, and also facilitates real-world meetups and educational programming. The initiative seeks to create a welcoming "third place" for people to connect and feel a sense of belonging in Dallas.

Rather than developing its own models, Oracle is leveraging Cohere's technology for its databases and vertical market applications, and customizing Meta's Llama models for its cloud platform. This approach prioritizes open architecture and deep partnerships to fine-tune AI models for enterprise-specific business solutions.

summary: NODWIN Gaming has acquired Trinity Gaming for INR 24 Crore (~£2.2m). This acquisition, which includes a combination of cash and stock swap, makes Trinity Gaming a fully owned subsidiary and aims to expand NODWIN Gaming's influence in the Indian gaming and content ecosystem, as well as new international markets. Trinity Gaming's founders will become shareholders of NODWIN Gaming, and their talent management operations will merge with NODWIN's Unpaused Talent business.

title: Why Momentum Stocks & ETFs Are Crushing the Market

Companies like Amazon, NVIDIA, Meta Platforms, Berkshire Hathaway, and Eli Lilly are key holdings driving the performance of these momentum-focused ETFs.

title: GoodRx Agrees to $25 Million Settlement to Resolve Tracking Technology Lawsuit
summary: GoodRx has agreed to a $25 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit alleging its use of website tracking technologies shared user data, including sensitive health information, with third parties like Meta and Google without consent. This follows a previous settlement with the FTC for $1.5 million over similar allegations and a requirement to cease sharing health information for advertising. The current settlement, if approved by a District Court Judge, would allow affected individuals to claim a share of the fund.

However, a company executive acknowledged that meeting the increased demand might necessitate extending the operational life of natural gas assets into the 2030s, even as coal-fired plants are phased out. Skepticism from state officials highlights challenges in meeting both clean energy goals and the rapidly growing electricity needs of data centers.

summary: Meta Platforms Inc. plans to invest $10 billion in building artificial intelligence data centers in Richland Parish, Louisiana. A team comprising Turner Construction Co., DPR Construction, and M.A. Mortenson Co. has been selected for the project. The mile-long facility will span 4 million sq ft, becoming Meta's largest data center yet, with site work commencing in December and completion expected by 2030.

summary: Meta announced its plan to build a $10 billion artificial intelligence data center in northeast Louisiana, a move expected to create 500 direct jobs, over 1,000 indirect jobs, and 5,000 construction jobs. Entergy will work with Meta to provide 100% clean and renewable energy for the facility and contribute to community assistance programs.

title: Netgear to downsize HQ after leasing 89K sf building in San Jose
summary: Netgear is downsizing its headquarters by leasing an 89,400-square-foot office building in north San Jose, a significant reduction from its current 142,700-square-foot hub. This move into the building at 3553 North First Street is part of a growing trend of office transactions in Silicon Valley, which market insiders view as a potential rebound for the commercial real estate market following a period of high vacancy. The deal follows other major office leases and subleases by companies like Nvidia, Amazon, and Snowflake, suggesting renewed activity in the Bay Area's office sector.

title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) Leads AI Investment in 2024, Purchasing 485,000 Nvidia GPUs to Power AI Infrastructure
summary: Microsoft Corporation is leading AI investment in 2024 by acquiring 485,000 Nvidia Hopper GPUs, significantly outpacing rivals. This massive investment powers Microsoft's AI infrastructure, including its Azure cloud platform and AI services, reflecting the increasing demand for specialized hardware in the AI sector. The article also highlights the resource-intensive nature of AI, requiring substantial energy and materials, and discusses the broader implications for infrastructure and climate.

title: Analyst says Celestica’s momentum has ‘continued’ through the year
summary: Robert Young, managing director at Canada Canaccord Genuity, highlights Celestica's strong performance, attributing it to conservative guidance and a "beat and raise cadence." The company's significant business with top hyper scalers, including Google and Meta, for designing networking gear and AI servers, is a major growth driver. Young notes that Celestica is increasingly recognized for its higher-value OEM role due to its IP, moving beyond its traditional EMS vendor perception.

title: Instagram Addiction Arbitration: Users’ Mental Health Harmed?
summary: ClassAction.org is organizing Instagram users for mass arbitration against Meta, alleging the company intentionally designed Instagram to be addictive, harming users' mental health. The initiative seeks compensation for affected individuals aged 18 and older who used the app in the past five years and experienced mental health issues like depression and anxiety. This action is pursued as mass arbitration due to Instagram's terms of use, which include an arbitration clause, and aims to cover medical bills, lost wages, and pain and suffering.

title: AI Is Reshuffling Utilities Stocks. Entergy, NiSource, and Others Could Be Winners.
summary: The article discusses how artificial intelligence (AI) is impacting the utilities sector, leading to a division between companies poised to benefit from increased demand from AI data centers and those expected to grow more slowly. It highlights several utilities, including Entergy and NiSource, as potential winners due to this explosive demand. The piece notes that utility stocks have seen a strong year, recovering from a difficult 2023.

title: Townsquare media CEO Bill Wilson's family buys $1,545 in stock
summary: Bill Wilson, CEO of Townsquare Media, Inc. (NYSE: TSQ), had his adult child purchase 165 shares totaling $1,545, as disclosed in a recent SEC filing. These transactions occurred while TSQ trades near its 52-week low, offering an 8.56% dividend yield. The company is also actively managing its capital through a new share repurchase plan and debt refinancing efforts, with analysts projecting potential upside.

title: Mark Zuckerberg cashes in on Meta’s record stock price with fresh share sales
summary: Mark Zuckerberg has been selling shares of Meta Platforms Inc. (META) this year, cashing in on the company's record stock price. Last week alone, he sold approximately $23 million worth of Meta stock through multiple transactions. This follows a trend of sales throughout the year, totaling over $2 billion.

title: Sustainable and Successful: 3 Climate ETFs Beating the Market
summary: This article highlights three climate-focused ETFs—iShares Climate Conscious & Transition MSCI USA ETF (USCL), Xtrackers MSCI USA Climate Action Equity ETF (USCA), and SPDR MSCI USA Climate Paris Aligned ETF (NZUS)—that have outperformed the S&P 500. These ETFs offer investors ways to support companies mitigating climate change while also achieving strong financial returns. The article details each ETF's approach to sustainable investing, their expense ratios, asset bases, and recent performance.

title: Thanks to AI Demand, Analysts are Doubling Down on These 2 Utility Stocks for 2025
summary: Analysts are recommending PPL Corporation (PPL) and Entergy Corporation (ETR) as top utility stock picks for 2025, driven by surging electricity demand from AI data centers. PPL is highlighted for its Kentucky generation and Pennsylvania transmission opportunities, while Entergy recently secured a significant deal with Meta Platforms to build new natural gas plants and support solar/battery capacity for a data center. Both companies are expected to benefit from increased earnings growth and robust infrastructure investment plans fueled by AI demand.

title: Dana White, John Elkann and Charlie Songhurst to Join Meta Board of Directors
summary: Meta has appointed Dana White, John Elkann, and Charlie Songhurst to its board of directors, effective January 6, 2025. Mark Zuckerberg highlighted their expertise will be crucial for Meta's focus on AI, wearables, and future human connection. White brings experience as CEO of UFC, Elkann as CEO of Exor, and Songhurst as a technology investor with prior strategic roles at Microsoft.

title: Can Kwai and Bigo Live replace TikTok? alternatives for 2025
summary: With a potential TikTok ban looming in early 2025, marketers and users are seeking alternative short-form video platforms. This article explores Kwai and Bigo Live as strong contenders, alongside established alternatives like YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels. It details their strategies, target demographics, and features, assessing their potential to fill the void left by TikTok.

title: A Comprehensive And Maddening History Of Color Star, Would-Be Titans Of Just About Anything
summary: This article details the bizarre and convoluted history of Color Star Technology Co., Ltd., a company that transformed from a ready-mix concrete supplier to a self-proclaimed metaverse developer. The author outlines Color Star's questionable business practices, including its brief, tumultuous partnership with the Philadelphia 76ers, its series of ever-changing CEOs, and its pattern of announcing non-existent ventures and partnerships with seemingly fake entities. The article suggests that Color Star's primary goal is to maintain its Nasdaq listing despite having no real product or sustainable business model.

title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
summary: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" list of stocks, initially identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the U.S. stock market's performance. It suggests that these stocks, including giants like Nvidia, are poised for continued revenue growth in 2025. The piece also mentions other groups of dominant stocks, such as "BATMMAAN," which includes Broadcom and the "Magnificent Seven."

title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
summary: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" stocks identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the US stock market's performance over the past five years. The piece also mentions other dominant stock groupings like "BATMMAAN" and the "Magnificent Seven."

title: Israeli companies Teva, Wix listed as 2025 top stock picks by analysts
summary: US analysts have identified two Israeli companies, Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. and Wix.com Ltd., as top stock picks for 2025. Bank of America raised its price target for Teva, citing its stabilizing financial profile and strong product pipeline, while Cantor Fitzgerald selected Wix, highlighting its product roadmap, pricing strength, and potential as an acquisition target. Both companies saw significant stock price increases in 2024.

title: Apple, Google And Meta Can't Escape Gambling Lawsuits, Judge Rejects Section 230 Shield In Landmark Ruling On Casino-Style Apps
summary: A federal judge has ruled that Apple, Google, and Meta must face lawsuits alleging they profited from illegal casino-style apps, rejecting their Section 230 defense. The companies are accused of hosting and promoting addictive gambling apps and taking 30% commissions on in-app purchases, with plaintiffs seeking compensatory damages for alleged addiction and its consequences. This ruling adds to the growing legal scrutiny faced by tech giants.

title: Fundsmith Equity Underperforms for the Fourth Year | Morningstar UK
summary: Fundsmith Equity, the UK's largest fund, underperformed global markets for the fourth consecutive year in 2024, despite an 8.9% growth rate compared to the MSCI World Index's 20.8%. Fund manager Terry Smith attributed this to extreme market concentration in a few tech stocks and made several portfolio changes, including selling Diageo, McCormick, and Apple, and buying Atlas Copco and Texas Instruments. The article also discusses Smith's views on passive funds' role in AI growth and notes that despite recent underperformance, Fundsmith Equity still boasts strong long-term returns since its inception.

title: Three Reasons Why FTV is Risky and One Stock to Buy Instead
summary: This article analyzes Fortive (NYSE: FTV), suggesting it's a risky investment despite its stable stock price movement recently. The author highlights three main concerns: slow organic revenue growth, minimal earnings per share (EPS) growth, and a historically mediocre return on invested capital (ROIC). Instead of FTV, the article recommends considering Meta, a digital advertising platform, and mentions other high-quality stocks from their curated list, citing impressive past performance of their picks like Nvidia and United Rentals.

title: AI revolution: Impact Series
summary: The article discusses the significant infrastructure demands of the AI revolution, highlighting the strain on resources like power, land, water, and materials due to increasing AI adoption and model training. It emphasizes the need for responsible and sustainable development of AI, examining energy consumption by data centers and the growing demand for "firm" and "dispatchable" power sources like nuclear and geothermal energy. Authors Christian Keller, William Thompson, and Akash Utsav contributed to this Impact Series report from Barclays Research, stressing the global challenge of balancing economic opportunities with environmental sustainability in AI development.

title: CHAT: Generative AI Stocks Still Look Relatively Attractive
summary: The Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT) has seen a significant rise of +13.60% since October 2024, outperforming the S&P 500. The ETF maintains a diversified portfolio with key holdings in NVIDIA, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms. While previously rated a "Strong Buy," the rating has been revised to "Buy" due to macroeconomic uncertainties, even as it appears between fair value and "slightly undervalued."

title: Why This Top 4% Cancer-Tied Stock Just Hit A 13-Month High
summary: Guardant Health (GH) stock reached a 13-month high after Medicare approved coverage for its colon cancer blood test, Guardant Reveal. This test identifies tumor DNA in blood, enabling early detection of recurrent cancer, which is crucial for better patient outcomes. The decision by Medicare officials to cover the Guardant Reveal test is a significant development for the company.

title: Sonos takeover speculation grows, with Spotify or Amazon tipped as potential suitors (report)
summary: Speculation is mounting that audio technology company Sonos could be acquired following a problematic app rollout in 2024 and recent leadership changes, including the departure of CEO Patrick Spence. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman identifies Spotify and Amazon as the most likely suitors. While Amazon could integrate Sonos' premium audio into its Echo devices, Spotify could gain immediate entry into the hardware market, despite its past struggles with products like the "Car Thing."

title: Singapore explores metaverse; Thailand mulls ETF approval
summary: Singapore's parliament is exploring the metaverse to engage young citizens in legislative processes, with a gamified experience on Roblox. Meanwhile, Thailand's securities regulator is considering approving local exchanges to list Bitcoin ETFs, following global trends in cryptocurrency adoption while ensuring investor protection.

title: Unveiling the Future of Agency Media Platforms: Omnicom’s Omni vs. the Competition - White Paper
summary: This white paper by The Myers Report examines the evolving landscape of agency media platforms, focusing on Omnicom Media Group's Omni. It analyzes Omni's advanced AI capabilities, cross-channel measurement tools, and personalization features, comparing them against rival platforms such as Publicis’ Epsilon PeopleCloud, WPP’s mPlatform, Dentsu’s Merkle M1, and IPG’s Acxiom and MAGNA. The report provides a detailed analysis of each platform's strengths, weaknesses, and future outlooks in the battle for dominance in data-driven media planning and buying.

title: 7 Best Content Distribution Platforms to Grow Your Reach
summary: This article identifies and compares seven top content distribution platforms for 2026, including Buffer, Bynder, Distribution.ai, Hootsuite, Mailchimp, RELAYTO, and StoryChief. title: Texas Instruments, Meta, Verizon, Boeing, Tesla, Amex, and More Stock Movers

Companies like Texas Instruments, Meta, Verizon, Boeing, Tesla, Amex, and Novo Nordisk were among the most significant stock movers. The article details major companies experiencing stock fluctuations in response to the economic insights.

title: Meta at ATH after Announcing Massive $65B CapEx Plan for AI Push 📈
summary: Meta Platforms has announced a massive $60-65 billion capital expenditure plan for 2025, a significant increase aimed at bolstering its AI infrastructure and becoming a leader in the AI landscape. This aggressive investment includes building a new data center and acquiring 1.3 million GPUs, with the goal of developing Meta AI into a leading assistant serving over a billion users. Despite the substantial spending, market reaction has been largely positive, pushing Meta's stock to an all-time high as investors support the company's strategic AI focus.

title: Aurora Mobile Partners With China's DeepSeek To Boost AI Capabilities
summary: Aurora Mobile's enterprise AI platform, GPTBots.ai, has integrated China's DeepSeek R1 large language model (LLM), enhancing its capabilities alongside existing models like OpenAI, Meta Llama, and Google Gemini. This integration aims to provide businesses with more efficient, adaptable, and cost-effective advanced AI solutions, leveraging DeepSeek R1's strong reasoning tasks performance and affordability. The partnership positions GPTBots.ai as a comprehensive platform for diverse LLMs, allowing businesses to select models catering to specific operational, customer service, or marketing needs.

title: Investors Shovel Over $100 Million Into This ETF Betting on the Rise of Nuclear Power
summary: The Range Nuclear Renaissance Index ETF (NUKZ) has attracted over $100 million in investments, demonstrating significant interest in nuclear power as a cleaner, safer, and more reliable energy source. Launched in January 2024, the ETF has shown impressive growth and returns, appealing to investors seeking energy security and sustainable solutions. Experts anticipate continued growth for the nuclear power industry and related investments due to recent innovations, international agreements, and increasing public demand.

title: AI stock rout: The US selloff in 4 charts
summary: US stocks experienced a selloff, particularly in large-cap growth, following news that Chinese AI startup DeepSeek developed an advanced AI model that requires less sophisticated semiconductor chips. This development triggered concerns about the necessity of significant capital expenditures by large US tech companies in generative AI, leading to a 17% plunge in Nvidia stock and declines in other chipmakers and utility stocks. However, some tech giants like Apple and Meta Platforms saw gains, and Morningstar analysts maintain their fair value estimates for Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet, expecting long-term benefits from reduced AI model pricing.

title: US judge in Facebook data privacy case rejects class action bid
summary: A federal judge in California has rejected a bid by millions of Facebook users to band together in a class action lawsuit against Meta Platforms. The judge dismissed key evidence, including an expert's finding that Meta would owe users $5 a month for their data in a competitive market, which estimated damages exceeding $52 billion. Meta welcomed the ruling, stating it faces fierce competition and denying any wrongdoing.

title: Meta Posts Q4 Earnings Beat, But Reality Labs Losses Mount
summary: Meta Platforms reported strong Q4 2024 earnings, exceeding analysts' expectations in revenue and EPS, driven by its Family of Apps (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp). However, the Reality Labs division continued to incur substantial operating losses, raising concerns about the metaverse's future and prompting significant AI infrastructure investments. Despite these losses, Meta's shares performed well, indicating investor confidence in Zuckerberg's overall strategy.

title: Meta Platforms shares near all-time high zone ahead of today earnings report release📈
summary: Meta Platforms (META.US) shares are gaining ahead of its Q4 earnings report, with investors focusing on the impact of AI investments on profitability and upcoming CAPEX plans. The company's stock has seen significant growth in 2024 and from its 2022 lows, making the earnings report crucial for potential profit-taking. Wall Street expects strong revenue and EPS growth, alongside scrutiny of Meta's $60-65 billion CAPEX plans for 2025, heavily weighted towards AI infrastructure, and potential competition from DeepSeek.

title: Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, UPS, IBM, Nvidia, Comcast, Las Vegas Sands, American, and More Movers

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Alphabet (GOOGL/GOOG) ahead of its Q4 earnings report, maintaining a 4-star "undervalued" rating with a fair value estimate of $220 per share. The report highlights key areas to watch including GenAI monetization, YouTube's growth, and the overall ad business performance, while noting the company's strong financial position and wide economic moat despite antitrust concerns.

title: Outbrain-Teads $1 Billion Merger Deal Cleared by U.K. Antitrust Regulator
summary: The U.K.'s antitrust watchdog has approved the $1 billion merger between U.S. online recommendation platform Outbrain and media company Teads. The companies announced their merger plans in August 2024, aiming to strengthen their position in the digital advertising market against major players like Google and Meta. The Competition and Markets Authority will publish its full decision on the deal shortly, having initiated its probe last December.

title: Rio Tinto, Glencore Look To Consolidate
summary: The article discusses the ongoing consolidation trend in the metals and mining industry, highlighting potential merger talks between Glencore and Rio Tinto. A successful merger would create a $140 billion industry leader with significant synergies, especially in copper. Despite challenges, including Glencore's coal exposure, industry leaders are actively exploring consolidation strategies as evidenced by BHP's recent attempt to acquire Anglo American Plc.

The company is actively working to attract new businesses through a resilient grid and support major projects like the River District Neighborhood Initiative and Five O’ Four Golf, aiming to further boost economic activity and reduce residential rates.

title: BlackRock, Inc.'s Strategic Acquisition of Amentum Holdings Inc. Shares

This move demonstrates BlackRock's confidence in Amentum, a company providing engineering and technology solutions, which now constitutes 6.90% of BlackRock's holdings in the stock and has seen a 7.85% gain since the transaction. While Amentum's GF-Score indicates potential challenges, its focus on critical solutions and the involvement of other major investors make it a stock to watch.

title: Qualcomm and Arm shatter Wall Street’s targets, but investors aren’t too happy
summary: Qualcomm and Arm both reported strong quarterly earnings and optimistic guidance that surpassed Wall Street's expectations, yet their stock prices fell in after-hours trading. Qualcomm saw record revenue driven by strong smartphone, automotive, and IoT chip sales, while Arm's revenue climbed due to the rapid adoption of its Armv9 architecture. Despite impressive growth and positive outlooks from both companies, investors appeared dissatisfied, with concerns about Qualcomm's licensing business and Arm's flat licensing revenue from its more profitable platform.

title: Redfin video privacy lawsuit voluntarily dismissed by plaintiff
summary: A video privacy lawsuit against Redfin, alleging violations of the Video Privacy Protection Act and California Invasion of Privacy Act, has been voluntarily dismissed with prejudice by the plaintiff, Guillermo Mata. Mata had accused Redfin of sharing consumers' personally identifiable information from video home tour views with third-party companies. The dismissal occurred after Mata agreed to individual binding arbitration for his claims.

title: Cathie Wood’s Stock Portfolio: 2025 Stock Picks

title: State senator pushes bill to protect Georgia Power customers from rate hikes fueled by data centers
summary: A Georgia state senator has filed a bill (SB 34) to prevent Georgia Power from raising electricity rates on residential and commercial customers to cover the costs of energy-intensive data centers. While Georgia Power and a data center association oppose the bill, arguing rate-setting belongs to the Public Service Commission (PSC), supporters believe it offers stronger protections than a recent PSC rule concerning data center energy costs. The debate highlights concerns about the massive energy demands of a growing data center industry in Georgia and its potential impact on utility bills and the environment.

title: Brazil denies report of potential tax on US tech firms after Trump steel tariffs
summary: Brazil's finance minister, Fernando Haddad, denied a report speculating that the country would tax U.S. tech companies in retaliation for former President Donald Trump's proposed 25% tariffs on steel imports. Haddad stated that the information was incorrect and that the government would only make statements based on concrete decisions. The report from Folha de S.Paulo had suggested a potential levy on companies like Amazon, Meta Platforms, and Google.

title: Alphabet (Google) Stock Price Dropped by 10%: Should You Buy?
summary: Alphabet Inc.'s stock price dropped by 10% after its Q4 FY 2024 earnings report, despite strong growth across all segments. Concerns revolve around slightly lower-than-expected Google Cloud revenue growth and anticipated increases in depreciation and capital expenditure. The article analyzes these concerns, suggesting the dip might present an investment opportunity given the company's strong financial position and the rapidly expanding AI market.

title: Shopify Merchant Success Powers Q4 Outperformance Across Both Top and Bottom Line

title: Report: Arm to build its own chips, and Meta is first in line to buy them
summary: Arm Holdings Plc. reportedly plans to design and build its own server CPUs, with Meta Platforms Inc. lined up as a first customer, marking a significant shift from its traditional licensing model. This move, driven by the lucrative AI chip market, could put Arm in direct competition with its existing licensees, potentially alienating customers but offering substantial new revenue streams. Analysts highlight both the immense opportunity in AI and the risks of disrupting established customer relationships.

title: Arm may be on the verge of a big strategic shift — competing with its customers
summary: Arm Holdings PLC is reportedly planning a major strategic shift by debuting its own chip as early as this summer, with Meta Platforms Inc. as its first customer. This move would put Arm in direct competition with its existing customers like Nvidia Corp. and Qualcomm Inc., a significant change from its traditional business model of licensing chip designs. Arm's stock jumped 6% following the report.

title: Arm plans to sell its own chips in an effort to ramp up revenues
summary: Chip designer Arm is reportedly shifting its strategy to manufacture and sell its own chips, aiming to increase revenues and compete with its existing clients like Qualcomm. This move signifies a departure from its traditional business model of licensing intellectual property and could significantly disrupt the semiconductor industry, particularly in the data center and AI sectors. Arm, owned by SoftBank Group, is expected to reveal its first chip by summer 2025.

title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg sells shares worth $14.4 million
summary: Mark Zuckerberg, CEO of Meta Platforms, Inc., recently sold 17,855 shares of Meta's Class A Common Stock for approximately $14.4 million through a pre-established trading plan. Meta has also announced a dividend increase and is investing in AI-powered humanoid robots, alongside other strategic moves.

title: Want to be a millionaire by 2040? Here's how $200K today could grow to $1 million tomorrow with this BlackRock Tech ETF
summary: BlackRock's iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (ETN) offers a potential path to turn $200,000 into $1 million by 2040, primarily by investing in major technology companies with strong AI exposure like Meta, Apple, Nvidia, and Microsoft. Despite these sales, Meta demonstrates strong financial health with a high gross profit margin and increased quarterly dividends.

title: Meta settles Israeli privacy lawsuit for $338,000
summary: Meta has settled an Israeli class-action lawsuit for $338,000, which will be transferred to the Israel Class Action Fund. The lawsuit alleged that Meta misused phone numbers provided for two-step verification by making them available to advertisers. As part of the settlement, Meta has revised its policies to ensure such phone numbers are used solely for security, safety, and integrity purposes.

summary: Baidu Inc. shares fell 7.2% after its annual results, despite beating EPS expectations. title: AI Data Center Demand Isn't Collapsing, It's Shifting

This move ensures that the immense capital expenditures for AI infrastructure are conserved and merely redistributed among various partners.

Despite strong revenue, Nvidia's forward-looking statements signaled slowing profit-margin growth, and concerns about sustaining the rapid pace of AI investment and recent economic slowdowns have impacted its stock.

title: Earnings call transcript: Eventbrite Q4 2024 misses EPS forecast, stock drops
