(NYSE: EB) reported a wider-than-expected loss in Q4 2024 with an EPS of -$0.09, missing the -$0.05 forecast, despite revenue slightly surpassing expectations at $76.5 million. The stock subsequently dropped nearly 12% in after-hours trading. The company outlined 2025 as a "year of transition," focusing on expanding product offerings and market presence, including reintroducing free ticket listings and investing in Eventbrite Ads, while anticipating a revenue range of $295 million to $310 million for the full year.

title: Milberg Lawsuit Accuses Meta of Tracking Users Without Consent
summary: Milberg has filed a class action lawsuit against Meta, alleging that the social media giant bypasses user privacy settings by directing them to Meta's in-app browser to track their online activity. The lawsuit claims Meta uses JavaScript injection to monitor user interactions on third-party websites without consent, despite users' privacy preferences. This legal action seeks compensatory damages and injunctive relief for a national class, a California subclass, and an iOS subclass of Meta users.

title: QQQX: 8%-Plus Yield, 9% Discount, Long-Term NAV Growth (NASDAQ:QQQX)
summary: QQQX is a closed-end fund that generates an 8%+ yield by selling covered calls on NASDAQ 100 stocks, aiming for attractive returns with reduced volatility. The fund boasts a 35% NAV/share increase since inception and currently trades at a 9.11% discount to NAV, providing regular quarterly distributions with potential tax advantages. Despite risks like price volatility and call option risks, QQQX is considered a speculative Buy for investors seeking high yield.

title: 8 Top Performance Marketing Trends for SMBs To Know in 2026
summary: This article outlines eight key performance marketing trends for SMBs in 2026, focusing on how businesses can adapt to evolving technology and consumer behavior. Key trends include AI-driven optimization, personalized marketing, signal-based targeting in a post-cookie world, and the growing importance of various forms of video content, niche partnerships, outcome-based marketing, and creative as a primary performance lever. The article emphasizes that successful performance marketing in 2026 will blend technology with human oversight, prioritizing measurable results and creative agility in a privacy-conscious landscape.

title: Nature’s Sunshine Launches First Integrated Brand Platform
summary: Nature's Sunshine Products has launched its first integrated brand platform, "Be More Earth," in partnership with Crispin. The campaign highlights Nature's Sunshine's half-century history of utilizing nature's healing properties and sourcing premium natural components for its supplements. The platform and visual design are inspired by the Earth's uniqueness and restorative power, emphasizing honesty and transparency in the brand's approach.

title: First Class Action Filed Under Washington’s MY Health MY Data Act Draws Parallels to Previous SDK Litigation
summary: The first class action lawsuit under Washington's MY Health MY Data Act (MHMDA) has been filed against Amazon, alleging improper collection and use of consumer location data via Software Development Kits (SDKs). The lawsuit, Maxwell v. Amazon.com, Inc., draws parallels to prior SDK class actions, but introduces MHMDA's broad private right of action for violations related to "consumer health data," which includes precise location information. Businesses are advised to be mindful of SDK use as more states enact privacy legislation and similar lawsuits are expected.

title: JFrog advances AI security with Hugging Face partnership, Nvidia NIM and new MLOps platform
summary: JFrog has announced new initiatives to enhance AI security and deployment, including a partnership with Hugging Face for advanced security scanning of machine learning models. The company is also integrating its platform with Nvidia Inference Microservices (NIM) for secure deployment of GPU-optimized models and launching JFrog ML, an MLOps solution to unify ML development with DevSecOps practices. These efforts aim to bring greater trust, transparency, and security to the AI supply chain, addressing concerns about malicious models and simplifying generative AI application delivery at scale.

title: Summit energizes hundreds of MBA students to lead climate innovation
summary: The 2025 ClimateCAP Summit at Berkeley Haas brought together 500 attendees, including 359 MBA students from 43 schools, to foster climate leadership and innovation. The event featured discussions on various climate-related topics, networking opportunities, and insights from industry experts and Haas faculty. Interim Dean Jennifer Chatman emphasized the students' critical role as the next generation of climate leaders, while Amy Chan, Chief Sustainability Officer, highlighted the attention to detail in organizing a sustainable event and passed the baton to the University of Washington's Foster School of Business for next year's summit.

title: Parents are blaming Snapchat for their teens' fentanyl deaths. Will an L.A. lawsuit shape the future of social media?
summary: Over 60 families are suing Snap Inc., the company behind Snapchat, alleging that the app's design facilitates drug sales to teenagers, leading to fentanyl overdose deaths. The lawsuits, filed in Los Angeles County Superior Court, challenge Snap's protection under Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act, with a judge ruling that the case can proceed based on allegations of the app's destructive design. This legal battle could have significant implications for how social media companies are held accountable for content and user safety.

title: Hot Stocks from 2024 Have Grown Cold
summary: Many of 2024's top-performing stocks, particularly in the AI sector, have seen significant declines in early 2025 after being overvalued. While these stocks stumbled due to various negative news events and market corrections, healthcare stocks have seen notable gains and are leading the market in 2025. This shift highlights a return to fairer valuations for some previously inflated growth stocks and a rotation into defensive sectors.

title: Salesforce joins growing list of companies ending diversity hiring initiatives
summary: Salesforce Inc. has become the latest company to end its diversity hiring initiatives, removing language about diversity and inclusion from its annual financial disclosure. This decision follows executive orders signed by President Donald Trump targeting the elimination of DEI programs. Other major companies like Google, Meta, Target, Amazon, Walmart, and Deloitte have also rolled back similar initiatives.

title: Is Meta Platforms Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold Ahead of Llama 4 Launch?
summary: Meta Platforms (META) is preparing to launch its Llama 4 AI model in April 2025, which is expected to enhance its competitive position in generative AI. The company recently reported strong Q4 2024 earnings, significantly beating revenue and EPS expectations. Analysts widely rate META as a "Strong Buy," with a considerable upside potential indicated by their price targets.

title: Advocates want light shed on power plans for Meta’s Louisiana data center
summary: Environmental and consumer protection advocates are demanding transparency from Meta and Entergy regarding the power plans for Meta's new data center in Louisiana. They want Meta and its developer, Laidley LLC, to be listed as parties on Entergy's application to build three natural gas power plants, which would require disclosure of energy demand and job creation figures. Advocates are concerned about the $3 billion cost being passed to ratepayers and are pushing back against Entergy's request for expedited permitting.

title: Why FPA Global Equity ETF Is Worth Considering
summary: The FPA Global Equity ETF (FPAG) is highlighted as a promising active ETF that is essentially a carve-out of FPA Crescent's equity portfolio. Managed by Brian Selmo and Mark Landecker, it focuses on 30-50 value-oriented, quality-focused stocks, differing from its benchmark by being fully invested and sometimes overweighting sectors like communication services. While it has outperformed its prospectus benchmark and category benchmark since its December 2021 launch, its fully invested nature means higher volatility and less downside protection during downturns but better participation in rising markets.

title: Vodafone: Be in the office 8 days a month or lose bonuses
summary: Vodafone is mandating its UK employees to work in the office at least eight days a month, or face disciplinary action and potential loss of bonuses starting April 1. This policy, in place since 2021, is now being strictly enforced, despite staff concerns about reduced office space and the need to collaborate with remote international teams via video calls. The company emphasizes the importance of in-office collaboration while employees point to research questioning the productivity benefits of RTO mandates and the impact on morale.

title: Market outlook: How investors with US shares should respond to tariffs, uncertainty in March
summary: This article advises investors with US shares to focus on fundamentals and valuations amid the uncertainty of potential tariffs in March 2025. It suggests overweighting value and small-cap stocks, while underweighting growth stocks and certain large-cap stocks. The author also predicts potential negative impacts of tariffs on several sectors and companies, highlighting the importance of strategic portfolio positioning.

title: Tech Moves: Longtime Google leader Umesh Shankar joins Microsoft as new corporate VP
summary: Umesh Shankar, a veteran leader from Google, has joined Microsoft AI as a corporate vice president of engineering, focusing on privacy and security for the Copilot AI assistant. This move is part of a trend of Google executives moving to Microsoft AI. The article also details other significant personnel changes at Remitly, Crelate, Bloomberg, Pioneer Square Labs, Seattle Aquarium, Built Oregon, Fred Hutch Cancer Center, and Trove.

title: Is it time to buy the Magnificent Seven?
summary: The "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks have experienced a significant selloff, making some of them appear undervalued according to Morningstar's fair value metrics. While Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet are now rated as buys, Nvidia and Tesla are fairly valued, and Apple remains overvalued. Morningstar strategists still recommend underweighting growth stocks overall, favoring value stocks due to market volatility and slowing economic growth.

title: Meta Faces Legal Challenge by French Publishers Over AI Training
summary: French publishers and authors have collectively filed a lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc., alleging copyright infringement. The complaint, lodged by several leading French publishing and authors' associations, claims that Meta utilized their copyrighted literary works without permission to train its generative artificial intelligence models. This legal action targets Meta in a Paris intellectual property court.

title: Graf Global Corp. Class A (GRAF) reports earnings
summary: Graf Global Corp. Class A (GRAF) has filed its Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. As a blank check company, it has no operations or revenues to date but holds $235.8 million in a trust account for a future business combination, which must be completed by June 27, 2026. The company is evaluating potential targets and operates under reduced disclosure requirements as an emerging growth and smaller reporting company.

title: Stock Market Today: Dow Sinks 537 Points On Tariff Threats As S&P 500 Corrects; Gold Miners Jump, Meta Triggers Sell Rule (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 537 points, and the S&P 500 corrected amid new tariff threats from the Trump administration. Gold miners rallied while Home Depot and Meta Platforms experienced sell-offs, with Meta triggering a sell rule. Despite a late attempt by traders to cover short positions, major indexes ended the trading day lower.

title: Why Meta’s New Content Moderation Policy Could Pose Risks to Its Stock
summary: Meta's new content moderation policy, shifting from third-party fact-checking to a user-led community notes approach, poses significant risks to the company's stock. This change could increase the spread of misinformation and hate speech, potentially leading to heightened regulatory scrutiny, especially from European bodies like the EU Digital Services Act, and financial repercussions such as reduced user engagement and advertiser spending due to brand safety concerns. While Meta's reliance on small and medium-sized businesses for advertising might offer some insulation compared to platforms like X, the potential for substantial fines and a challenging regulatory landscape globally are key concerns for investors.

title: Bumble adds an ID verification feature to win over cautious daters
summary: Bumble has introduced new safety features, including government ID verification, to attract safety-conscious users and counter slowing growth in the dating app market. This initiative aims to authenticate users' identities and offers filters for verified profiles, alongside tools like "Review Before You Send" for messages and a "Share Date" feature for sharing date details with contacts. These changes come as the dating industry faces post-COVID slumps, generational shifts in dating behavior, and the complexities introduced by AI.

title: When Facebook Ads Won’t Help Performance Advertisers
summary: This article discusses when Facebook ads are beneficial for performance advertisers and when they may not be. While Facebook offers extensive targeting and can drive traffic, marketers should be aware of diminishing returns due to factors like inappropriate budgets, ad frequency fatigue, rising CPA, and audience demographics. The piece emphasizes the importance of a diversified media mix and exploring alternative advertising channels for sustainable growth.

title: The most innovative companies in social media for 2025
summary: Fast Company has released its 2025 list of the most innovative companies in social media, highlighting those that successfully revived old trends or found novel ways to expand their reach. Duolingo is featured for its playful marketing and gamified learning approach, which has broadened its influence beyond language education into math and music, incorporating AI for enhanced user experience. Other companies recognized include Bluesky, Partiful, Feeld, Strava, Pinterest, Yelp, and Sesame Workshop for their unique contributions to the social media landscape.

title: Apple loses German antitrust fight, faces greater scrutiny
summary: Apple lost its challenge in Germany's top civil court against its classification as a significant market power, a designation that grants antitrust regulators more authority to scrutinize its business practices. The Federal Court of Justice upheld the German cartel office's 2023 ruling, placing Apple alongside Google (Alphabet) and Meta as tech giants subject to measures curbing dominance. This decision strengthens the German antitrust watchdog's position, especially concerning Apple's App Store and tracking regulations, though the EU's Digital Markets Act remains the primary standard for Big Tech.

title: Magnificent 7 Stocks Are Falling: How Low Can They Go?
summary: The author discusses the current downturn in the Magnificent 7 stocks, viewing it as a market correction rather than a crash, with most stocks trading 20-25% off their all-time highs. He analyzes the technical indicators of the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS) and provides specific insights and support levels for Meta Platforms (META), Alphabet (GOOG), and Microsoft (MSFT), highlighting potential buy opportunities.

title: Palmetto Grain Brokerage -
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) is outperforming the Nasdaq, with its shares up 5.1% over the past three months compared to the Nasdaq Composite's 8.4% decline, and 29.7% over the past year. Despite beating Q4 earnings estimates, the stock dropped due to underwhelming Q1 revenue guidance. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, with a modest upside potential from current levels.

title: 4 tech giants look cheap ahead of earnings
summary: Ahead of their earnings reports, four major tech companies—Apple, Amazon, Facebook, and Microsoft—are considered undervalued. Analysts see Apple as undervalued despite recent revenue guidance adjustments, Amazon as continuing its impressive growth, Microsoft showing broad-based strength, and Facebook maintaining its competitive advantages in digital advertising despite recent controversies.

title: Canary Files for PENGU ETF Tracking Pudgy Penguins Token
summary: Canary Capital has filed with the SEC to launch a Canary PENGU ETF, which would primarily hold PENGU tokens, along with Solana and Ethereum for transactions. This new fund aims to provide investors with exposure to niche cryptocurrencies beyond Bitcoin and Ethereum, specifically the PENGU token linked to the Pudgy Penguins NFT collection. The firm has been actively expanding its cryptocurrency ETF offerings, with PENGU representing its latest filing following others for Solana, Litecoin, XRP, Hedera, Axelar, and SUI.

title: These are the best platforms to work as a freelancer
summary: This article identifies and details the top 10 freelance platforms, including Freelancer, Guru, Toptal, Upwork, and Fiverr. It thoroughly outlines the characteristics, fee structures, and application processes for each platform, catering to freelancers looking to offer professional services globally. The guide aims to help individuals choose the best platform for their skills and desired work environment.

title: What Do DEI Rollbacks Mean for Investors?
summary: Recent DEI rollbacks by major U.S. companies, influenced by a 2025 executive order, are raising concerns among investors regarding potential impacts on corporate environmental, social, and governance (ESG) risks. While not all changes are equally material, those affecting core initiatives, particularly in knowledge-intensive industries, could modestly increase human capital and overall ESG risk scores. Morningstar Sustainalytics analyzes these changes by focusing on their relevance to workforce diversity assessments and their weight within ESG risk ratings.

title: Is the US Stock Market Cheap Yet?
summary: The US stock market is currently trading at a 5% discount to its fair value, primarily due to significant selloffs in mega-cap technology and AI stocks. While valuations are more attractive than at the start of the year, they haven't reached the deep discounts seen in 2022. Morningstar strategists advise investors to consider overweighting value and small-cap stocks, and to wait for market stabilization before making major moves.

title: Youth mental health: Corpus Christi ISD seeks to join lawsuit against social media companies
summary: Corpus Christi ISD's board of trustees has voted to join an ongoing federal lawsuit against major social media companies, alleging that their platforms contribute to a youth mental health crisis and disrupt the learning environment. The district will only pay legal fees if the lawsuit results in a successful judgment or settlement, aligning with approximately 800 other school districts and 46 Texas school districts awaiting attorney general approval to participate. This action reflects growing concerns from health officials and educators, highlighting issues like addiction, bullying, and the need for increased mental health support in schools due to social media use.

title: Meta says copying books was 'fair use' in authors' AI lawsuit
summary: Meta Platforms has argued in a U.S. court that its use of copyrighted books by authors like Ta-Nehisi Coates and Sarah Silverman to train its Llama AI system constitutes "fair use." The company is seeking to dismiss a lawsuit filed by the authors who claim Meta used pirated versions of their books without permission. Meta states its AI is transformative, not a substitute for reading the books, while the authors contend Meta systematically took expressive content without compensation.

title: AppLovin Corporation lays off dozens amid stock surge, class action lawsuit
summary: AppLovin Corporation laid off dozens of employees from its Palo Alto headquarters, announcing 97 permanent job cuts despite a recent surge in its stock price. The company's stock climbed significantly last week, even as AppLovin is facing a class-action lawsuit alleging securities fraud related to misleading advertising practices and inflated metrics.

title: Nuclear startup Terrestrial Energy goes public via SPAC, netting $280M in merger
summary: Terrestrial Energy, a nuclear startup based in North Carolina, has merged with a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC), raising $280 million. The company, which develops integral molten salt reactors (IMSRs), plans to list on Nasdaq under the symbol IMSR. This move follows the increasing interest in nuclear power as a solution to rising electricity demand, particularly from data centers and AI operations.

title: Qualcomm files antitrust complaints against ARM around the world
summary: Qualcomm has filed antitrust complaints against ARM Holdings plc with the European Commission, the US Federal Trade Commission, and the Korea Fair Trade Commission, alleging anti-competitive practices. This follows a Delaware court case where Qualcomm successfully defended itself against an ARM lawsuit regarding its AI-PC processors. Qualcomm claims ARM is restricting technology access and exploiting market dominance as it prepares to compete with its customers, a move indicated by ARM CEO Rene Haas's strategy papers and reports of ARM designing chips for data centers.

title: AppLovin shares plunge 20% after third short-selling firm slams company's ad technology
summary: AppLovin's shares dropped 20% after Muddy Waters became the third short-selling firm to accuse the company of violating app store terms of service by "impermissibly extracting" user data for targeted ads. Muddy Waters, following Fuzzy Panda Research and Culper Research, claimed AppLovin's ad tactics involve funneling targeted ads without consent and that e-commerce advertisers are abandoning the platform. AppLovin's CEO previously defended the company's technology against similar allegations, stating such claims are attempts to manipulate stock prices for financial gain.

title: AppLovin: Another Short-Seller Report
summary: Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of $284 per share for AppLovin despite a recent short-seller report from Muddy Waters Research. The report alleges that AppLovin overstates return on ad spending and violates platform terms of service by creating persistent identity graphs (PIGs). While shares fell 20% on the news, Morningstar highlights the "Very High Uncertainty Rating" but states some claims like aggressive retargeting seem misplaced, focusing on the allegation of unauthorized proprietary ID aggregation.

title: Abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund Q4 2024 Commentary (AOD)
summary: The abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund (AOD) underperformed its benchmark in Q4 2024, primarily due to stock selection and sector allocation, with Tesla, Amazon, and Meta Platforms being key detractors. Despite global equity market gains driven by optimism over Trump's policies, the fund maintains a cautious outlook given macroeconomic factors and recession risks. The investment team focuses on stock-level diversification and capital preservation.

title: eBay Finally Rolls Out Magical AI Listing Starting In Germany, Plus Updates On Facebook Marketplace Cross Posting
summary: eBay is beginning the rollout of its "Magical Listing" AI tool in Germany, allowing sellers to create listings simply by taking a picture, despite being delayed by over a year and a half. The company is also providing updates on its integration with Facebook Marketplace, which has faced challenges with listing visibility. This delayed AI tool places eBay behind competitors like Mercari, Depop, and Poshmark, all of whom have already launched similar features.

title: YMAG ETF Holdings List — AMEX:YMAG
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the holdings for the YMAG (YieldMax Magnificent 7 Fund of Option Income ETFs) ETF as of February 27, 2026. The fund primarily invests in option income strategy ETFs for major tech companies like Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, Tesla, Google, Apple, and Nvidia, alongside small allocations to U.S. Dollar reserves and a Government Obligations Fund. Each holding's weight, share count, and market value are presented.

title: US stock market outlook: Where we see opportunities in Q2
summary: The US stock market experienced a downturn in Q1 2025, primarily affecting AI-related growth stocks, leading to more attractive valuations across the board. Value, wide-moat, and small-cap stocks are now considered most attractive, with the overall market trading at a 5% discount to fair value. Investors are advised to overweight value and small-cap stocks, while underweighting growth, as Morningstar's economics team anticipates a reacceleration of the economy in early 2026.

title: SPMO ETF Holdings List — AMEX:SPMO
summary: This article provides the holdings list for the Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF (SPMO). As of March 12, 2026, the fund's top holdings include Broadcom Inc., NVIDIA Corporation, Meta Platforms Inc. Class A, JPMorgan Chase & Co., and Palantir Technologies Inc. The list details the weight percentage, number of shares, and market value for each holding.

title: UFC® Announces Comprehensive Multiyear Partnership With Meta
summary: UFC has announced a multiyear partnership with Meta, naming Meta its first Official Fan Technology Partner. This collaboration will leverage Meta's diverse technology platforms, including Meta AI, Meta Glasses, Meta Quest, and social media, to enhance fan engagement. Threads will also become the Official Social Media Partner of UFC, serving as a primary platform for exclusive content and fan interaction.

title: The AIs of Texas: The ANA's Conference on AI and Technology for Marketers Delivers on Insights and Inspiration
summary: The ANA's AI and Technology for Marketers Conference in Austin gathered over 1,000 marketers to discuss the integration of AI, MarTech, and data in the advertising world. Key themes included the shift to generative AI activation, the rise of agentic AI, the impact of AI search tools on traditional SEO, and the evolution of the Metaverse. The conference also highlighted new AI and MarTech products for ANA members and featured discussions on AI regulation, with a focus on transparency and accountability.

title: Chubb, Hartford Lose Bid to Stop Transfer of Meta Coverage Row
summary: A federal judicial panel has ruled that Meta Platforms Inc.'s dispute with its insurers, Chubb and Hartford, over coverage for social media lawsuits should be handled through multidistrict litigation. The insurers' efforts to prevent the transfer and keep the case in Delaware state court were denied. This decision means the coverage issues will be resolved alongside the underlying cases accusing Meta of addicting children to its platforms.

title: SoundHound AI’s Deep Dive: Legal Woes and Stock Impact
summary: SoundHound AI Inc. (SOUN) stock is down 11.45% due to class-action lawsuits alleging securities fraud, misleading statements, and internal control weaknesses between May 2024 and March 2025. The company faces scrutiny over its financial health, including a high price-to-sales ratio and significant cash burn, while simultaneously showcasing substantial market faith in its future AI prospects. The article delves into the legal challenges and financial metrics, presenting a complex picture of potential and pitfalls for the AI company.

title: Meta's content moderation contractor to cut 2,000 jobs in Barcelona
summary: Telus, a content moderation contractor for Meta, has laid off 2,000 employees in Barcelona after Meta severed its contract with the local centre. While Telus states Meta is diversifying services to other locations, Meta confirmed it moved services from Barcelona but is not reducing overall content review efforts. This follows Meta's previous actions to scale back content policing, including ending its U.S. fact-checking program and stopping proactive hate speech scanning.

title: Mark Zuckerberg says Meta’s latest Llama models put open-source AI in the driving seat
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has unveiled new additions to its Llama family of large language models, including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, which Zuckerberg claims will put open-source AI in the driving seat. These models are available through Meta AI assistant and are designed for enhanced reasoning skills and various tasks. Meta is also working on a larger model, Llama 4 Behemoth, intended to be the "highest performing base model in the world."

title: Bookworm: This ASX name meets many of Terry Smith’s criteria
summary: This article examines Terry Smith's criteria for a quality business, focusing on five key tenets: high return on operating capital, growth potential, intangible and difficult-to-replicate assets, resilience, and lack of reliance on leverage. The author then applies these criteria to the ASX company Cochlear (ASX: COH), highlighting how it aligns with many of Smith's principles despite being considered overvalued by analysts. The piece concludes by reinforcing Smith's investment philosophy of investing in good companies, not overpaying, and holding for the long term.

title: Judge Dismisses Challenge to Meta’s Louisiana Data Center
summary: A Baton Rouge judge has dismissed a second challenge to Entergy's plan to power Meta's $10 billion AI data center in northeast Louisiana, ruling that Meta does not need to be a party in the proposal before state regulators. Environmental and consumer nonprofits had argued that Meta's involvement was necessary to address critical information regarding job creation and sustainability. The judge sided with Entergy, stating that advocacy groups still have other avenues, such as subpoenas, to obtain information from Meta.

title: Stagwell (STGW) Unveils New SPORT BEACH 2025 Athletes and Brand Partners Including Erin Andrews, Sir Mo Farah, Chris Paul, Alex Rodriguez, George Russell, Charissa Thompson and More
summary: Stagwell (NASDAQ: STGW) announced the launch of registration for SPORT BEACH 2025 and an expanded lineup of athletes and brand partners. The event, held at Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity from June 16-19, 2025, will feature sports personalities like Erin Andrews, Sir Mo Farah, Chris Paul, Alex Rodriguez, George Russell, and Charissa Thompson. New brand partners include Business Insider, Diageo, La Fête Wine Company, LG Ad Solutions, Movember, Nielsen, Official AI, Qualcomm Technologies, STAPLE, TripleLift, and Zillow Group.

title: Hugo Barra is back in consumer tech with Sonos board seat
summary: Hugo Barra is re-entering the consumer tech space by joining the Sonos board of directors, replacing long-time director Mike Volpi. Barra brings extensive experience from his roles at Google, Xiaomi, and Meta, particularly in Android andculus VR development. This move could signal a strategic shift for Sonos, which has faced recent challenges, toward refocusing on its core strength as an audio manufacturer and potentially integrating AI into future products.

title: Türkiye: TikTok content moderators sue Telus over allegations of poor conditions & union retaliation; incl. co. response
summary: A group of 15 TikTok content moderators in Türkiye are suing Telus, alleging they were dismissed in retaliation for unionizing. The workers, who were reportedly low-paid and exposed to disturbing content, claim severe mental health issues and poor working conditions. Telus has provided a statement in response to these allegations, which are said to be part of a broader issue in outsourced content moderation.

title: George Gstar: The Hobbies of the Young, Rich, and Powerful
summary: This article details the luxurious hobbies and investment strategies of George Gstar, a 31-year-old multimillionaire with an estimated net worth of $250 million. It highlights his penchant for collecting rare cigars, expensive whisky, and jewelry, viewing these items as investments. The piece also explores how other young, ultra-wealthy individuals approach their money, balancing indulgence with entrepreneurial reinvestment and emphasizing philanthropy.

title: Meta AI Lawsuit: US Publishers File Amicus Brief
summary: The Association of American Publishers (AAP) has filed an amicus brief supporting a class-action lawsuit against Meta, arguing that Meta's use of copyrighted material for AI training is not "fair use." The AAP asserts that Meta systematically copied protected works for its LLaMA AI model, often from pirate sites, despite the existence of a growing market for licensing AI training materials. The brief highlights that Meta's actions undermine copyright protections and financial investments of authors and publishers.

title: Reddit, Pinterest, and Other Stocks That Look Like Better Buys Than Nvidia and the Rest of the Mag 7
summary: The article suggests that while the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks often experience selloffs, they still do not represent the best value for investors. It proposes that there are other, cheaper tech stocks available, such as Reddit and Pinterest, which offer better buying opportunities for long-term investors. A specific image caption also mentions Shopify as an alternative to the "Mag Seven" names.

title: Is The Trade Desk Stock A Bargain At $50?
summary: The Trade Desk (TTD) stock has dropped nearly 60% in 2025, now trading at $50, a level that has historically triggered rallies. Despite the decline, the company, a pure-play in programmatic advertising with strong revenue growth and rising operating margins, is not in decline. While its P/E and P/EBIT ratios remain high, continued revenue growth and margin expansion could lead to superior returns even if valuation multiples compress.

title: 智谱冲击大模型第一股，“六小虎”分野
summary: 智谱AI已提交IPO辅导备案，有望成为国内大模型领域首家启动上市进程的企业，最快2026年登陆A股。在资本热度回归理性、大厂挤压市场空间的情况下，智谱通过与地方国资合作完成产业化落地，并重点布局AI Agent和开源策略，以差异化优势和低幻觉率技术在行业中突围。其IPO进程及商业模式为大模型赛道提供了新的观察窗口。

title: Companies are cutting more managers, leaving employees directionless
summary: Companies are increasingly cutting management positions, leading to a "flatter" corporate structure primarily to reduce costs, as highlighted by a new report from Korn Ferry. This trend, which accelerated with tech layoffs, is causing employees to feel directionless and unmoored. While some organizations may have too many management layers, managers are crucial for aligning employee efforts towards common goals.

title: Why Meta is one of the most compelling tech stocks right now
summary: This article highlights why Meta Platforms is considered a compelling tech stock despite antitrust scrutiny. It references two articles by Laila Maidan that make the investment case for Meta, focusing on reasons for bullish sentiment and the potential for value-unlocking through a federal antitrust case requiring divestment of major business units. The company's social-media dominance through Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp is also noted.

title: Why Meta is one of the most compelling tech stocks right now
summary: MarketWatch columnist Philip van Doorn highlights why Meta Platforms is a compelling tech stock. Laila Maidan has made an investment case for Meta, citing three reasons for investor bullishness and discussing the potential unlocking of value if a federal antitrust case leads to the divestiture of major business units.

title: Carousel Ads: Best Uses and Formats Explained
summary: This article explains carousel ads, their benefits, and best practices for their use in digital marketing. It covers how carousel ads can improve engagement and conversion rates by showcasing multiple products or telling a deeper story, and provides strategies for both B2C and B2B companies, including A/B testing and retargeting methods.

title: 特朗普关税前，扎克伯格抛售股票套现超50亿元
summary: 在特朗普政府宣布关税政策之前，多位企业高管，包括Meta的马克·扎克伯格、甲骨文的萨夫拉·卡茨和摩根大通的杰米·戴蒙，抛售了数十亿美元的公司股票。扎克伯格通过其慈善机构出售了110万股Meta股票，套现7.33亿美元，此后Meta股价下跌。这些抛售行为发生在特朗普关税政策引起市场动荡和股市大跌之前，导致许多高管和全球富豪的财富有所缩水。

title: Live Nation Donated $500,000 to Trump Inauguration Committee
summary: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. donated $500,000 to Donald Trump's 2025 inauguration committee, making it one of the largest corporate backers. This contribution is particularly noteworthy as Live Nation is currently facing a landmark antitrust lawsuit from the U.S. Department of Justice aimed at breaking up its ticketing and live-events empire. The timing of the donation raises questions about influence, especially as the company continues to lobby against regulations and faces scrutiny over its business practices.

title: Private equity: A valuable tool for portfolio diversification
summary: Private equity is emerging as a critical tool for portfolio diversification, offering access to distinct economic opportunities not replicable in public markets. As public markets shrink and become less representative of the economy, private equity provides unique exposures, generates uncorrelated returns, and enhances overall risk-return profiles. Academic studies and real-world performance demonstrate its resilience and lower correlation with public equities, making it an essential component for modern portfolio construction.

title: Florida attorney general sues Snapchat, claims it's violating state's social media law
summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier has filed a lawsuit against Snap Inc., the operator of Snapchat, alleging violations of a 2024 state law (HB 3) aimed at preventing children under 16 from accessing certain social media platforms. The lawsuit claims Snapchat contracts with users under 14 and fails to seek parental consent for 14 and 15-year-olds, specifically highlighting the platform's addictive features like push notifications and disappearing messages. This legal action follows a federal judge's rejection of an injunction request by tech industry groups against the law, as the state intends to pursue further investigations and enforcement.

title: Meta struggles to dismiss Instagram addiction class action lawsuit
summary: A Massachusetts appeals court has declined to dismiss a class action lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc., alleging that its Instagram platform is addictive and harmful to children. The lawsuit, filed by Massachusetts Attorney General Andrea Joy Campbell, claims Meta uses features like infinite scrolling to keep children engaged despite knowing the platform's negative mental health impact. Meta's arguments for dismissal, based on Section 230 and First Amendment rights, are likely to fail as the lawsuit focuses on the company's conduct and alleged false safety claims.

title: Google Parent Alphabet Leads Adtech Stocks Higher on Signs of AI Success
summary: Alphabet's shares rose after its quarterly earnings beat expectations, driven by the success of AI features like AI Overviews in Google Search. This positive performance also lifted other adtech and AI-related stocks, and analysts have increased price targets for Alphabet, anticipating further growth in AI-driven services. Several other tech companies with cloud and digital advertising businesses are set to report earnings soon, potentially reflecting similar trends.

title: Is Borr Drilling’s 14% Spike Sustainable?
summary: Borr Drilling Limited’s stock spiked by 14% following new contract wins for three jack-up rigs, projected to generate $120M in revenue and having a combined duration of 774 days. The company's recent filing for an automatic mixed securities shelf aims to streamline future capital raising. Investors are keenly awaiting the Q1 2025 earnings report on May 21, 2025, to assess its financial health and growth prospects amidst considerations of historical revenue declines and stock volatility.

title: Behavioral Targeting: What It Is, How It Boosts Conversions
summary: Behavioral targeting is a marketing technique that leverages a consumer's online activities like website visits, product views, and search queries to deliver personalized advertisements. This method is crucial for increasing ad relevance, boosting conversion rates, and reducing wasted ad spend by focusing on high-intent audiences. The article explains how behavioral targeting works through data collection, profiling, audience segmentation, and AI-powered ad delivery, contrasting it with contextual and demographic targeting.

title: Another Tech-Adjacent Sector Could Lift QQQ
summary: The communication services sector, which emerged in 2018 from previously tech-classified components, holds significant weight in ETFs like Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). Despite recent struggles by some "Magnificent Seven" companies within this sector, opportunities abound for investors as several communication services stocks, including Alphabet, Meta Platforms, and Charter Communications, are seen as undervalued or having potential for long-term returns. This sector's strong presence and the attractive valuations of some of its constituents could contribute to the uplift of QQQ and QQQM.

title: Bay Area developer helps Valero assess vast Benicia refinery’s future
summary: Valero Energy Corp. is working with Bay Area real estate firm Signature Development to assess potential redevelopment opportunities for its 900-acre Benicia oil refinery complex in Solano County, following Valero's announcement of its intent to cease or restructure refining operations there. Signature Development, known for projects like Brooklyn Basin and Willow Village, will collaborate with the city of Benicia and the community to explore future plans for the site. The refinery is scheduled to close by April 2026.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Meta Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Meta Platforms (META) ahead of its Q1 2025 earnings report, focusing on AI monetization, Reels profitability, and Reality Labs spending. The stock is currently rated as undervalued with a 4-star rating and a fair value estimate of $770 per share, supported by a wide economic moat and strong financial health despite investments in AI and Reality Labs. Key areas to watch include ad monetization, capex plans, genAI monetization, and operational efficiencies, with antitrust concerns noted but considered less impactful than market speculation suggests.

title: Going into earnings, is Meta stock a buy, a sell, or fairly valued?
summary: Ahead of its Q1 2025 earnings release, Meta Platforms (META) is considered undervalued by Morningstar, with a fair value estimate of $770 per share. Key factors to watch include AI monetization, Reels profitability, and capital spending plans, with a focus on its ad monetization improvements and strong competitive advantages in its Family of Apps business. The company faces uncertainty due to investments in Reality Labs and generative AI, alongside ongoing antitrust concerns, but its core advertising business remains a robust cash generator.

title: Meta Faces Copyright Reckoning in Authors’ Generative AI Case
summary: A federal court in San Francisco is set to hear a crucial summary judgment in a copyright lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc., brought by authors including Sarah Silverman, Ta-Nehisi Coates, and Andrew Sean Greer. The case will examine whether Meta's use of pirated books to train its Llama AI model constitutes fair use, with the outcome potentially having significant implications for copyright law and the generative AI industry. The authors allege direct copyright infringement by Meta through the use of "shadow libraries," while Meta argues its AI model is a transformative product falling under fair use, regardless of how the training data was acquired.

title: Meta Reports First Quarter 2025 Results
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. reported strong financial results for the first quarter of 2025, with revenue increasing by 16% year-over-year to $42.31 billion and net income rising by 35% to $16.64 billion. CEO Mark Zuckerberg highlighted growth in community, business performance, and advancements in AI glasses and Meta AI. The company provided Q2 2025 revenue guidance of $42.5-$45.5 billion but updated its full-year 2025 capital expenditure outlook to $64-$72 billion to support AI initiatives, while acknowledging potential regulatory impacts from the EC's Digital Markets Act decision in Europe.

title: Meta's Q1 Earnings Beat Lifts Shares: ETFs to Buy
summary: Meta Platforms reported better-than-expected Q1 2025 earnings, with adjusted EPS of $6.43 and revenues of $42.3 billion, leading to a 5.4% jump in its shares after-market. The company also raised its capital expenditure guidance for 2025 due to increased data center investments for AI efforts. Investors looking to capitalize on Meta's growth can consider ETFs with significant allocations to the company, such as iShares Global Comm Services ETF (IXP), Fidelity MSCI Communication Services Index ETF (FCOM), and Vanguard Communication Services ETF (VOX).

title: Mark Zuckerberg Plans to Sell $166 Million of Meta Stock
summary: Mark Zuckerberg, the founder, Chairman, and CEO of Meta Platforms, intends to sell Meta stock worth up to $166 million. This move involves a significant amount of his holdings in the company he leads.

title: BlackRock, Inc. Reduces Stake in TTM Technologies Inc.
summary: BlackRock, Inc. has reduced its stake in TTM Technologies Inc. by 2,886,132 shares, a 15.77% decrease, though TTM Technologies still represents only 0.01% of BlackRock's portfolio. The transaction occurred at $20.51 per share, and despite the reduction and TTM Technologies being considered overvalued with a low GF Score, its stock price has increased by 22.04% since the transaction. First Eagle Investment Management, LLC remains TTM Technologies' largest holder, indicating continued investor interest.

title: Earnings call transcript: Viant Technology Q1 2025 earnings beat expectations
summary: Viant Technology Inc. (DSP) reported a significant beat in its Q1 2025 earnings, with an EPS of $0.03 against a forecasted loss of $0.07 and revenue of $70.6 million, far exceeding expectations. The company saw a 32% year-over-year revenue increase and a 76% increase in adjusted EBITDA, driven by strong performance in connected TV (CTV) advertising and AI solutions. Viant's stock rose 4.96% in aftermarket trading, reflecting investor confidence and an outlook for continued growth, despite minor macroeconomic uncertainties causing some Q2 campaign activations to shift to the second half of 2025.

title: Arista Networks beats expectations in its latest results, but stock moves lower
summary: Arista Networks reported strong first-quarter earnings and revenue, surpassing analyst expectations. Despite beating targets and seeing increased AI spending from key customers, the company's stock fell almost 4% after it warned of a sequential decline in gross and operating margins for the current quarter, disappointing investors. The company's board also approved an additional $1.5 billion in share repurchases.

title: Utility Evergy misses quarterly profit estimates on lower demand, higher costs
summary: Evergy Inc. missed Wall Street's first-quarter profit estimates due to higher interest expenses, increased operating costs, and reduced industrial demand from an unnamed large customer's unplanned maintenance shutdown. Despite these challenges, total revenue and retail sales increased year-over-year, and the company anticipates strong commercial and industrial load growth in the latter half of the year from new customers like Meta and Panasonic. Evergy reaffirmed its annual adjusted earnings forecast.

title: Google's Internet Traffic Share in Korea Hits Record 31%, Fees Unpaid
summary: Google's internet traffic share in Korea has reached a record 31.17%, the highest for two consecutive years, yet the company continues to avoid paying network usage fees amounting to hundreds of billions of won. This places a significant burden on Korean telecom companies, which are struggling to meet the rising demand for network investment due to increased traffic from streaming services. Despite growing calls for government and industry intervention to address Google's "network free-riding," factors like potential trade friction, a political vacuum, and an SK Telecom hacking incident have stalled efforts.

title: Advertisers accuse Meta of secretly inflating Facebook ad prices in class action lawsuit
summary: Iron Tribe Fitness has filed a class action lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc., alleging that Meta systematically overcharged advertisers on its Facebook platform by using a flawed "blended price" auction process instead of the purported "second price" auction. The lawsuit claims that a 2013 coding change led to inflated ad prices and that Meta delayed correcting the issue after engineers identified it in 2017. Iron Tribe Fitness is seeking to represent a nationwide class of Facebook advertisers and is demanding compensatory relief for breach of contract, unjust enrichment, and violations of California's Unfair Competition Law.

title: Expedia Launches Industry-First Feature That Turns Instagram Reels into Bookable Travel Itineraries
summary: Expedia has launched "Expedia Trip Matching," a new AI-powered feature that allows travelers to convert Instagram Reels into bookable travel itineraries. Users can share a travel-related Reel with Expedia's account to receive personalized recommendations and direct booking options. This initiative aims to meet travelers where they find inspiration, bridging social media discovery with real-world travel planning.

title: Bitcoin adoption: increasingly part of my investment portfolio
summary: The article discusses the increasing adoption of Bitcoin, detailing its evolution through three distinct phases characterized by ETF inflows, halving events, and institutional interest. Author Ryk de Klerk highlights how Bitcoin is becoming an integral part of investment portfolios, driven by factors like net ETF inflows and a growing recognition from asset managers and nations like Russia and the US. He suggests an overweight position in Bitcoin due to anticipated further adoption.

title: Cathie Wood Doubles Down on Nvidia (NVDA) Stock Despite Selloff. Should You Copy Her?
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest recently purchased over 107,000 shares of Nvidia (NVDA) and also invested in AMD, following a significant tech selloff and concerns about competition and chip export rules. The article discusses her rationale, including potential changes in U.S. chip export policies under a new administration, Nvidia's strong market position in AI accelerators, and growing demand from major tech companies. It then advises whether individual investors should follow Wood's lead, highlighting both the long-term potential of Nvidia as an AI pure-play and short-term risks such as increasing competition from Huawei and potential worsening chip restrictions.

title: Scaramucci: Bitcoin to Hit $1.1M with 1B Wallets
summary: Anthony Scaramucci predicts Bitcoin could reach $1.1 million if the number of active wallets grows to one billion, signifying its maturation into a full asset class. He promotes the First Trust SkyBridge Crypto Industry & Digital Economy ETF (CRPT) as a surrogate investment for those restricted from direct Bitcoin purchases, highlighting its 35% year-to-date growth. Scaramucci believes broader adoption will lead to decreased volatility, drawing parallels to established companies like Microsoft and Google.

title: Is SPDR MSCI USA StrategicFactors ETF (QUS) a Strong ETF Right Now?
summary: The SPDR MSCI USA StrategicFactors ETF (QUS), launched on April 15, 2015, offers exposure to the Style Box - Large Cap Blend category with a smart beta strategy. Managed by State Street Global Advisors, it has over $1.49 billion in assets and seeks to match the performance of the MSCI USA Factor Mix A-Series Index, with an expense ratio of 0.15%. The ETF's top holdings include Apple Inc., Microsoft Corp, and Meta Platforms Inc., and it has shown strong performance, adding 2.29% year-to-date and 11.24% over the last 12 months as of May 13, 2025.

title: Life360 shares are up more than 1,300% in 5 years. How does it compare to other apps?
summary: Life360 (ASX: 360) shares have surged over 1,300% in five years, reaching a 52-week high after reporting strong Q1 earnings with 33% revenue growth and a tripling of adjusted EBITDA. The family safety app, dual-listed on the ASX and Nasdaq, uses a freemium model and AI to offer location, driving, and digital safety features. While significantly smaller than social media giants like Facebook and Instagram, Life360 is gaining popularity, ranking 4th in the US social networking app category and forecasting continued growth through word-of-mouth and international expansion.

title: 3 AI ETFs Tapping Into the Heart of the AI Revolution
summary: The article highlights three AI-focused ETFs that allow investors to capitalize on the ongoing AI revolution, as confirmed by positive first-quarter earnings reports and significant capital expenditures in AI infrastructure. These ETFs offer diversified exposure to different facets of AI, including robotics and industrial applications (BOTZ), data center infrastructure (DTCR), and generative AI (CHAT). Each fund provides a unique strategy for investing in AI, from broad applications to specific infrastructure and cutting-edge generative technologies.

title: Netflix Is Turning Into an Advertising Company
summary: Netflix, once a pioneer of ad-free streaming, is rapidly transforming into an advertising company. Its ad-supported tier now boasts 94 million global monthly active users and is its fastest-growing segment. The company is actively developing new ad formats, including AI-powered contextual ads, to compete directly with major digital advertising players like Google, Meta, and Amazon, signaling a significant shift in its business model.

title: China's Top Private Equity Firms Reveal "U.S. Stock Market Report Card": Aggressively Increasing Holdings in China Concept Stocks, Optimistic About China's Prospects
summary: Top Chinese private equity firms like Hillhouse Capital, Gaoyi Asset Management, and Greenwoods Asset Management significantly increased their investments in US-listed Chinese companies in Q1 2025. This aggressive buying signals their strong optimism towards the prospects of Chinese assets despite global economic shifts. Conversely, Li Lu's Himalaya Capital, mirroring Warren Buffett, reduced holdings in major US financial and tech stocks, indicating a cautious stance on those sectors.

title: Gen-Z Trusts Social Media for Financial Advice More Than Any Other Generation
summary: A recent Spruce survey reveals that nearly 70% of Gen-Zers are influenced by financial trends found online, a higher percentage than Millennials and Gen-X. While many Gen-Zers turn to social media for financial education, they also acknowledge that online investment advice requires fact-checking. Despite this, they trust social media more than older generations do, with TikTok being the most popular platform for financial information.

title: Meta platforms’ CFO Susan Li sells shares worth $4.53 million
summary: Meta Platforms CFO Susan Li sold shares worth $4.53 million across two transactions on May 15, selling a total of 6,874 shares at $659.36 each. These sales were part of broader stock movements including acquisitions and disposals of restricted stock units (RSUs) by Li. The transactions occurred while Meta maintains a $1.61 trillion market cap and a "GREAT" financial health score, reflecting its strong financial position and 37% stock return over the past year.

title: Period Tracking App Users Win Class Status in Google, Meta Suit
summary: Users of the period tracking app Flo have been granted class-action status in a lawsuit alleging the app unlawfully shared sensitive health data with Google LLC and Meta Platforms Inc. A US District Court judge ruled that the millions of potential users share common legal and factual questions regarding privacy disclosures and the app's use of software development kits from the tech giants. This ruling follows a previous rejection of Google's summary judgment motion, setting the case for a jury trial in July.

title: Meta to buy 200 MW of wind energy from TransAlta as it expands footprint
summary: Meta has signed a long-term agreement to purchase 200 MW of wind energy and environmental attributes from TransAlta's Horizon Hill Wind Power Project in Oklahoma. This deal supports Meta's goal of powering global operations solely with wind and solar energy as its footprint expands. The project, expected to cost between US$290 million and US$310 million, contributes to TransAlta's goal of adding 2 GW of renewables by 2025.

title: AES, Meta Sign PPAs for 650 MW of Solar Power in Kansas and Texas
summary: AES Corporation has signed two long-term power purchase agreements (PPAs) with Meta for 650 MW of solar power to support Meta's data centers in Kansas and Texas. These projects will contribute to Meta's goal of using 100% clean and renewable energy, create jobs, and provide significant tax revenue to local communities. AES currently has 32.7 GW in operation and a substantial backlog of signed PPAs, including 10.1 GW with major hyperscalers.

title: AES and Meta Sign Long-Term PPAs to Deliver 650 MW of Solar Capacity in Texas and Kansas
summary: The AES Corporation has signed two long-term Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) with Meta to supply 650 MW of solar power to Meta's data centers in Texas and Kansas. These projects aim to support Meta's sustainability goals and rapid data center growth, providing reliable and affordable renewable energy. The collaboration highlights AES's role as a leading energy provider for corporate customers and is expected to bring economic benefits and job creation to local communities.

title: Applovin Corp Securities Lawsuit Investigation
summary: Shamis & Gentile P.A. is investigating potential claims against Applovin Corp for alleged misleading statements regarding its AXON 2.0 digital ad platform and AI technologies. The lawsuit claims Applovin misrepresented its capabilities, exploited Meta Platforms’ data, and used manipulative practices to inflate ad and app metrics, leading to a significant stock price drop. Investors who purchased or held Applovin securities between May 10, 2023, and February 25, 2025, may be entitled to compensation.

title: AES, Meta ink 650MW solar PV PPAs in Texas, Kansas
summary: AES has signed long-term power purchase agreements (PPAs) with Meta for two solar PV projects in Texas and Kansas, totaling 650MW, to power Meta's data centers. While Texas is second in US solar capacity, Kansas ranks last, with these projects contributing significantly to its solar infrastructure. This initiative supports Meta's 100% clean and renewable energy goal and addresses the surging electricity demand from data centers and AI, which is expected to more than double globally by 2030.

title: Introducing the Artificial Intelligence Crypto Sector
summary: Grayscale Research is introducing a new Artificial Intelligence Crypto Sector to categorize blockchain-based projects focused on AI development. This new sector, the sixth in their classification framework, currently includes 20 tokens with a combined market capitalization of $21 billion, up significantly from $4.5 billion in Q1 2023. The sector aims to address concerns about centralized control in AI by promoting decentralized and transparent AI systems, with Grayscale offering exposure to key assets within this burgeoning area.

title: Alphabet Inc: in 10 years, we'll laugh at these valuations, unless
summary: The article discusses Alphabet's (GOOG) valuation, suggesting its current forward P/E of 16x is low given its strong financials and potential for growth, especially with AI advancements. It highlights that major investors like Bill Ackman and value investors see AI as a strengthening factor for Alphabet's business model, contrasting with market fears of disruption. The author argues that Alphabet's substantial cash flow, R&D investments, and growing segments like Google Cloud indicate a potential for significant reinvention and growth, making its current valuation appear irrational with a long-term perspective.

title: META PLATFORMS INC-CLASS A (NASDAQ:META) – A Strong Growth Stock with Technical Breakout Potential
summary: META Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META) has been identified as a strong growth stock with a favorable technical setup, exhibiting solid fundamental growth metrics and a bullish chart pattern. The company shows strong revenue and earnings growth, high profitability with significant margins and returns, and a solid financial health rating. Technically, META displays positive short-term and long-term trends, is forming a bull flag pattern, and has clear support and resistance levels.

title: Nvidia delivers another earnings and revenue beat on rampant data center growth
summary: Nvidia's shares rose almost 5% after reporting better-than-expected earnings and revenue for the first quarter, driven by a 73% year-over-year growth in its data center business. Despite missing guidance due to U.S. export restrictions on advanced chips to China, the company's strong performance in AI infrastructure and other sectors, including gaming and automotive, reassured investors. CEO Jensen Huang acknowledged the closure of the Chinese AI chip market for U.S. industry but emphasized the overwhelming global demand for Nvidia's AI solutions.

title: Entergy Doesn’t Want Louisianans to Know How Expensive this Gas Project Could Be
summary: Entergy Louisiana is seeking approval for a $3.7 billion gas plant project to power a Meta data center, raising concerns about grid reliability, affordability for ratepayers, and transparency. Critics argue the project's costs are underestimated, and it poses risks of power outages and environmental harms, particularly if Meta does not extend its contract. AAE and UCS are intervening in the case, highlighting a lack of public information due to redactions and confidentiality agreements.

title: Meta Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. (Nasdaq: META) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.525 per share for its Class A and Class B common stock. The dividend is payable on June 26, 2025, to stockholders of record as of June 16, 2025. This declaration was made by the company's board of directors on May 29, 2025.

title: FDG: A High Volatility, Premium Valuation Growth ETF (NYSEARCA:FDG)
summary: The American Century Focused Dynamic Growth ETF (FDG) is characterized as a high-volatility, premium-valuation growth ETF with a concentrated portfolio focused on mega-cap tech, consumer discretionary, and healthcare. Despite its strong performance in bull markets, the fund exhibits limited diversification, higher costs, and lower liquidity compared to competitors, leading to underperformance and steeper drawdowns in downturns. The author maintains a neutral rating on FDG due to its elevated risk, premium valuation (28.8x earnings), and inconsistent long-term returns.

title: Constellation, Meta Sign 20-Year Deal for Clean, Reliable Nuclear Energy in Illinois
summary: Constellation and Meta have signed a 20-year power purchase agreement for the output of the Clinton Clean Energy Center in Illinois, providing 1,121 megawatts of emissions-free nuclear energy to support Meta's operations. This agreement, starting in June 2027, ensures the continued operation of the Clinton nuclear facility for two decades after its current Zero Emission Credit program expires, preserving jobs, tax revenue, and expanding clean energy output. The deal serves as a market-based solution to replace ratepayer support, enhances grid reliability, and includes a $1 million charitable investment in the local community.

title: June 2025 US stock market outlook: Has the storm passed?
summary: The US stock market is currently trading at a 3% discount to fair value, but Morningstar warns of impending volatility due to trade tariffs, slowing economic growth, and rising Treasury yields. Investors are advised to be cautious, overweight value and small-cap stocks, and prepare for potential market downturns despite the current calm. Several sectors and specific stocks are highlighted for their current valuations and performance.

title: Meta Teams Up with Nuclear Giant to Spark Uranium Stock Rally! Uranium Energy Soars 11.8% in a Single Day
summary: Uranium Energy (UEC) shares surged 11.8% after Meta Platforms (META) announced a 20-year power purchase agreement with Constellation Energy (CEG) for nuclear power. This deal highlights the growing reliance of tech giants on stable, carbon-free nuclear energy to power their AI-driven data centers, potentially boosting demand and profitability for uranium miners like Uranium Energy. The market anticipates this partnership could be the start of a trend, positioning uranium stocks as a key component of future energy portfolios.

title: Walmart Inc. Signs 338,000-SF Lease at Jay Paul Company’s Iconic Tech Corners Campus in Sunnyvale
summary: Walmart Inc.'s eCommerce division has signed a 338,307-square-foot lease at Jay Paul Company’s Tech Corners in Sunnyvale, California, marking the largest new office lease in Silicon Valley since 2023. This expansion strengthens Walmart's presence in the region within a modernized, wellness-focused campus. The transaction highlights robust momentum in Silicon Valley's office leasing market, particularly in Sunnyvale.

title: Apple, X, and Airbnb among growing number of Big Tech firms exploring crypto adoption
summary: A growing number of Big Tech firms, including Apple, X (formerly Twitter), Airbnb, and Google, are exploring the adoption of stablecoins to reduce transaction costs and optimize cross-border payments. This interest is driven by a perceived shift in the regulatory environment towards blockchain technology and early successes by companies like Google Cloud in integrating stablecoin payments. While discussions are initial for some, the industry sees stablecoins as a significant upgrade to traditional payment systems.

title: META PLATFORMS INC-CLASS A (NASDAQ:META) – A Strong Contender for Quality Investors
summary: META PLATFORMS INC-CLASS A (NASDAQ:META) is identified as a strong candidate for quality investors based on the Caviar Cruise screening strategy. The company exhibits robust revenue and profit growth, high returns on invested capital, and solid financial health. Despite a seemingly expensive valuation, its strong growth prospects and financial stability may justify the premium, earning it high marks in profitability and financial health.

title: Open Internet: Key Aspects and Importance for Advertisers
summary: This article defines the "open internet" in contrast to "walled gardens" like Google and Meta, emphasizing its importance for advertisers seeking transparency, control, and diverse audience reach, especially with the phasing out of third-party cookies. It highlights how AI-driven tools can be leveraged for programmatic advertising, contextual targeting, native ads, and creative optimization within the open web. The piece concludes by recommending a balanced advertising strategy, allocating budget to both open web platforms and walled gardens for optimal reach and data building.

title: OPSB Greenlights Williams-Backed Power Plant for Meta Data Center in Ohio
summary: The Ohio Power Siting Board (OPSB) has approved the construction of the Socrates South Power Generation Project, a 200 MW natural gas-fired power plant in New Albany, Ohio. This facility, backed by Williams Companies, will supply power to a Meta-affiliated data center and operate off-grid. Natural gas will be sourced from two future 24-inch pipelines.

title: Shareholder Proposal Demanding Accountability for Antisemitism and Hate Across Meta’s Platforms Ranks as Top-Performing Human Rights Concern
summary: A shareholder proposal (Proposal 8) from JLens, urging Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) to address hateful content including antisemitism, anti-LGBTQ+, and anti-disability hate, received 46.8% support from independent shareholders, making it the top-performing human rights proposal in the 2025 proxy season. This high level of support, despite Meta's dual-class share structure distorting the overall vote, signals a strong message from investors for Meta to enhance transparency and accountability regarding content moderation. The proposal calls for a public report evaluating Meta's policies and effectiveness in moderating hateful content.

title: Snap Plans to Launch New Consumer ‘Specs’ AR Glasses Next Year
summary: Snap, the company behind Snapchat, announced plans to release a new consumer version of its Spectacles AR glasses (aka 'Specs') in 2026. This updated version is teased to have a smaller form factor, lighter weight, and increased capabilities compared to the developer-focused Spectacles '24. Significant updates to Snap OS will also be introduced, including integrations with OpenAI and Google Cloud's Gemini to enable multimodal AI-powered Lenses.

title: How AI Could Be Raising Your Energy Bill
summary: The accelerated growth of AI data centers is creating an unprecedented demand for power, risking a shift of infrastructure costs onto consumers through increased electricity rates. Research from Harvard Law School's Environmental and Energy Law Program highlights how special contracts between utilities and data centers lack transparency, leading to consumers subsidizing the development for trillion-dollar tech companies. Regulators and transparent processes are crucial to ensure tech giants pay their fair share, or alternative private energy parks could be developed to isolate data center costs.

title: Meta Losing AI Spending War To Google, Microsoft, Warns 'Big Short' Investor Steve Eisman
summary: Steve Eisman, the investor known for predicting the 2008 collapse, warns that Meta Platforms Inc. is losing the AI spending war against Alphabet Inc.'s Google and Microsoft Corp. He attributes this to Meta's lack of a cloud business, which prevents it from generating revenue from its AI investments unlike Google and Microsoft, whose cash reserves are increasing while Meta's is plummeting. Despite this, Eisman believes the massive spending from all three tech giants indicates the AI boom is far from over.

title: How to weigh up companies after a big share price fall
summary: This article explores how investors should evaluate companies after a significant share price decline, drawing on research by Michael Mauboussin. It highlights the frequency of major drawdowns and the low recovery rate for many stocks, while also providing six key questions investors should ask to identify potential winners and avoid losers in such scenarios.

title: Intuit Mailchimp Unveils Powerful New Suite of Tools at FWD: London to Help Businesses Unlock Their Customer Data
summary: Intuit Mailchimp announced a new suite of tools at FWD: London designed to help businesses better understand and leverage customer data for growth and conversion. These advancements include enhanced lead generation integrations with major platforms, over 100 new pop-up templates, and more robust custom reports, building on Mailchimp's focus on driving revenue for brands. Upcoming features like new custom audience tools, SMS contact import via API, and Intuit’s AI Agents for QuickBooks further aim to provide comprehensive, data-driven solutions for businesses.

title: Oracle's stock closes out best week since 2001 on cloud momentum
summary: Oracle's stock experienced its best week since 2001, surging 24% after a strong earnings report indicated significant momentum in its cloud business. The company's CEO, Safra Catz, provided revenue guidance exceeding consensus expectations, fueled by increasing demand for Oracle's cloud infrastructure, particularly from clients needing to operate AI models. Oracle is rapidly expanding its data center capacity to meet this demand, outpacing other tech megacaps in stock performance this year.

title: IBM Up 10 Days in a Row: What’s Driving the Winning Streak?
summary: IBM shares have seen a significant rally, climbing over 25% in 2025 and more than doubling since early 2024, driven by strong earnings and ambitious plans in AI and quantum computing. The company's roadmap includes building a fault-tolerant quantum supercomputer by 2029 and focusing on agentic AI for enterprises.
